```text
    50→    dark: { accent: '#737373', text: '#a3a3a3', bg: '#0a0a0a' },
    51→  },
    52→]
    53→
    54→export function EditorPage() {
    55→  const theme = useTheme()
    56→  const [variant, setVariant] = useState<RichEditorVariant>('article')
    57→  const [editorState, setEditorState] = useState<SerializedEditorState | null>(
    58→    null,
    59→  )
    60→  const [showJson, setShowJson] = useState(false)
    61→  const [showRenderer, setShowRenderer] = useState(true)
    62→  const [showImport, setShowImport] = useState(false)
    63→  const [importJson, setImportJson] = useState('')
    64→  const editorRef = useRef<LexicalEditor | null>(null)
    65→  const [selectedPreset, setSelectedPreset] = useState('Default')
    66→  const [customAccent, setCustomAccent] = useState('')
    67→  const [enabledPlatforms, setEnabledPlatforms] = useState<Set<string>>(
    68→    new Set(),
    69→  )
    70→
    71→  const activePlatforms = useMemo(
    72→    () => extraMentionPlatforms.filter((p) => enabledPlatforms.has(p.key)),
    73→    [enabledPlatforms],
    74→  )
    75→
    76→  const togglePlatform = useCallback((key: string) => {
    77→    setEnabledPlatforms((prev) => {
    78→      const next = new Set(prev)
    79→      if (next.has(key)) next.delete(key)
    80→      else next.add(key)
    81→      return next
    82→    })
    83→  }, [])
    84→
    85→  const extraPlatformMeta = useMemo(() => {
    86→    const map: Record<
    87→      string,
    88→      { label: string; icon: React.ReactNode; getUrl: (h: string) => string }
    89→    > = {}
    90→    for (const p of activePlatforms) {
    91→      map[p.key] = { label: p.label, icon: p.icon, getUrl: p.getUrl }
    92→    }
    93→    return map
    94→  }, [activePlatforms])
    95→
    96→  const themeOverrideStyle = useMemo(() => {
    97→    if (customAccent) {
    98→      return createThemeStyle({
    99→        color: {
   100→          accent: customAccent,
   101→          link: customAccent,
   102→          accentLight: `${customAccent}33`,
   103→          quoteBorder: customAccent,
   104→        },
   105→      })
   106→    }
   107→    const preset = colorOverridePresets.find((p) => p.label === selectedPreset)
   108→    if (!preset) return
   109→    const colors = theme === 'dark' ? preset.dark : preset.light
   110→    if (!colors.accent) return
   111→    const overrides: Record<string, string> = {}
   112→    overrides.accent = colors.accent
   113→    overrides.link = colors.accent
   114→    overrides.accentLight = `${colors.accent}33`
   115→    overrides.quoteBorder = colors.accent
   116→    if (colors.text) overrides.text = colors.text
   117→    if (colors.bg) overrides.bg = colors.bg
   118→    return createThemeStyle({ color: overrides })
   119→  }, [selectedPreset, customAccent, theme])
   120→
   121→  const handleChange = useCallback((state: SerializedEditorState) => {
   122→    setEditorState(state)
   123→  }, [])
   124→
   125→  const handleEditorReady = useCallback((editor: LexicalEditor | null) => {
   126→    editorRef.current = editor
   127→  }, [])
   128→
   129→  const handleImport = useCallback(() => {
   130→    try {
   131→      const parsed = JSON.parse(importJson) as SerializedEditorState
   132→      if (parsed.root) {
   133→        setEditorState(parsed)
   134→        setShowImport(false)
   135→        setImportJson('')
   136→      }
   137→    } catch {
   138→      // invalid JSON
   139→    }
   140→  }, [importJson])
   141→
   142→  return (
   143→    <MentionPlatformProvider platforms={extraPlatformMeta}>
   144→      <div className="page">
   145→        {/* Toolbar */}
   146→        <div className="toolbar">
   147→          <div className="toolbar-group">
   148→            <span className="toolbar-label">Variant</span>
   149→            <button
   150→              className={variant === 'article' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   151→              onClick={() => setVariant('article')}
   152→            >
   153→              Article
   154→            </button>
   155→            <button
   156→              className={variant === 'comment' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   157→              onClick={() => setVariant('comment')}
   158→            >
   159→              Comment
   160→            </button>
   161→            <button
   162→              className={variant === 'note' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   163→              onClick={() => setVariant('note')}
   164→            >
   165→              Note
   166→            </button>
   167→          </div>
   168→
   169→          <div className="toolbar-group">
   170→            <span className="toolbar-label">Colors</span>
   171→            {colorOverridePresets.map((preset) => {
   172→              const colors = theme === 'dark' ? preset.dark : preset.light
   173→              return (
   174→                <button
   175→                  key={preset.label}
   176→                  className={
   177→                    !customAccent && selectedPreset === preset.label
   178→                      ? 'btn btn-active'
   179→                      : 'btn'
   180→                  }
   181→                  onClick={() => {
   182→                    setSelectedPreset(preset.label)
   183→                    setCustomAccent('')
   184→                  }}
   185→                >
   186→                  {colors.accent && (
   187→                    <span
   188→                      style={{
   189→                        display: 'inline-block',
   190→                        width: 10,
   191→                        height: 10,
   192→                        borderRadius: '50%',
   193→                        backgroundColor: colors.accent,
   194→                        marginRight: 4,
   195→                        verticalAlign: 'middle',
   196→                      }}
   197→                    />
   198→                  )}
   199→                  {preset.label}
   200→                </button>
   201→              )
   202→            })}
   203→            <label
   204→              className={customAccent ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   205→              style={{ display: 'inline-flex', alignItems: 'center', gap: 4 }}
   206→            >
   207→              Custom
   208→              <input
   209→                type="color"
   210→                value={customAccent || '#33a6b8'}
   211→                onChange={(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) =>
   212→                  setCustomAccent(e.target.value)
   213→                }
   214→                style={{ width: 20, height: 20, border: 'none', padding: 0 }}
   215→              />
   216→            </label>
   217→          </div>
   218→
   219→          <div className="toolbar-group">
   220→            <span className="toolbar-label">Mention+</span>
   221→            {extraMentionPlatforms.map((p) => (
   222→              <button
   223→                key={p.key}
   224→                className={
   225→                  enabledPlatforms.has(p.key) ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'
   226→                }
   227→                onClick={() => togglePlatform(p.key)}
   228→              >
   229→                {p.label}
   230→              </button>
   231→            ))}
   232→          </div>
   233→
   234→          <div className="toolbar-group">
   235→            <span className="toolbar-label">View</span>
   236→            <button
   237→              className={showRenderer ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   238→              onClick={() => setShowRenderer((v) => !v)}
   239→            >
   240→              Renderer
   241→            </button>
   242→            <button
   243→              className={showJson ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   244→              onClick={() => setShowJson((v) => !v)}
   245→            >
   246→              JSON
   247→            </button>
   248→            <button
   249→              className={showImport ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   250→              onClick={() => setShowImport((v) => !v)}
   251→            >
   252→              Import
   253→            </button>
   254→          </div>
   255→        </div>
   256→
   257→        {/* Editor panel */}
   258→        <Panel title="Editor" badge={variant}>
   259→          <ShiroEditor
   260→            initialValue={initialContent}
   261→            onChange={handleChange}
   262→            variant={variant}
   263→            theme={theme}
   264→            style={themeOverrideStyle}
   265→            placeholder="Start typing... Try markdown shortcuts like # heading, **bold**, *italic*, > quote, ||spoiler||"
   266→            onSubmit={() => console.info('[Submit] Cmd/Ctrl+Enter pressed')}
   267→            autoFocus
   268→            onEditorReady={handleEditorReady}
   269→            extraMentionPlatforms={
   270→              activePlatforms.length > 0 ? activePlatforms : undefined
   271→            }
   272→          />
   273→        </Panel>
   274→
   275→        {/* Renderer panel */}
   276→        {showRenderer && editorState && (
   277→          <Panel title="Renderer (readonly)" badge={variant}>
   278→            <ShiroRenderer
   279→              value={editorState}
   280→              variant={variant}
   281→              theme={theme}
   282→              style={themeOverrideStyle}
   283→            />
   284→          </Panel>
   285→        )}
   286→
   287→        {/* JSON panel */}
   288→        {showJson && editorState && (
   289→          <Panel
   290→            title="Serialized JSON (EditorState)"
   291→            headerExtra={
   292→              <button
   293→                className="btn btn-sm"
   294→                onClick={() => {
   295→                  navigator.clipboard.writeText(
   296→                    JSON.stringify(editorState, null, 2),
   297→                  )
   298→                }}
   299→              >
   300→                Copy
   301→              </button>
   302→            }
   303→          >
   304→            <pre className="json-pre">
   305→              {JSON.stringify(editorState, null, 2)}
   306→            </pre>
   307→          </Panel>
   308→        )}
   309→
   310→        {/* Import panel */}
   311→        {showImport && (
   312→          <Panel title="Import JSON">
   313→            <textarea
   314→              className="import-textarea"
   315→              value={importJson}
   316→              onChange={(e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLTextAreaElement>) =>
   317→                setImportJson(e.target.value)
   318→              }
   319→              placeholder="Paste serialized EditorState JSON here..."
   320→              rows={8}
   321→            />
   322→            <div style={{ marginTop: '8px', display: 'flex', gap: '8px' }}>
   323→              <button className="btn btn-active" onClick={handleImport}>
   324→                Load
   325→              </button>
   326→              <button
   327→                className="btn"
   328→                onClick={() => {
   329→                  setShowImport(false)
   330→                  setImportJson('')
   331→                }}
   332→              >
   333→                Cancel
   334→              </button>
   335→            </div>
   336→          </Panel>
   337→        )}
   338→
   339→        {/* Tips */}
   340→        <div className="tips">
   341→          <h3 className="tips-title">Markdown Shortcuts</h3>
   342→          <div className="tips-grid">
   343→            <code># Heading 1</code>
   344→            <code>## Heading 2</code>
   345→            <code>**bold**</code>
   346→            <code>*italic*</code>
   347→            <code>++underline++</code>
   348→            <code>~~strikethrough~~</code>
   349→            <code>`inline code`</code>
```

```text
{"threadId":"019c9f60-080a-7a42-b227-1ed6530da49f","content":"**Findings (ordered by severity)**\n\n1. **High**: Selection comment creation can fail because the range anchor is built at submit time, after focus has moved off the editor.  \n   - In [`SelectionCommentPopup.tsx:66`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/components/comments/SelectionCommentPopup.tsx:66), `buildRangeAnchor(editor)` runs when clicking `Add`, not when the selection is first captured.  \n   - The trigger button also uses plain `onClick` at [`SelectionCommentPopup.tsx:119`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/components/comments/SelectionCommentPopup.tsx:119), so selection loss on mousedown/blur is possible.  \n   - Result: “Add” may no-op (`!result.ok`) for valid user flows.  \n   - Fix: capture anchor (or full selection snapshot) before focus changes, and/or use `onMouseDown` with `preventDefault()` on popup controls.\n\n2. **Medium**: Block IDs may not be initialized on first render, which can make block/range anchors unavailable until the first editor update.  \n   - [`BlockIdPlugin.tsx:20`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/BlockIdPlugin.tsx:20) only normalizes IDs inside `registerUpdateListener`; there is no explicit initial normalization pass on mount.  \n   - `BlockCommentGutter` skips blocks without IDs at [`BlockCommentGutter.tsx:44`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/components/comments/BlockCommentGutter.tsx:44).  \n   - If no update fires immediately after mount, gutter anchors and `buildRangeAnchor` can return `no-block-id`.  \n   - Fix: run one normalization `editor.update(...)` in `useEffect` after registering.\n\n3. **Medium**: Highlight reconstruction can drift/fail for ranges containing line breaks.  \n   - Anchor offsets count Lexical line breaks as `+1` at [`comment-anchor.ts:107`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/utils/comment-anchor.ts:107).  \n   - DOM remapping in [`CommentHighlightPlugin.tsx:17`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/components/comments/CommentHighlightPlugin.tsx:17) walks only text nodes and does not account for `<br>` nodes.  \n   - Result: wrong highlight positions or dropped highlights after multiline edits.  \n   - Fix: include `<br>`/line-break accounting in DOM offset mapping.\n\n4. **Low**: Comments page layout is not made responsive, so mobile can overflow.  \n   - Two-column layout is fixed at [`demo.css:525`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/demo.css:525) with sidebar width `320px` at [`demo.css:537`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/demo.css:537).  \n   - Existing media query at [`demo.css:816`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/demo.css:816) does not adjust `.comments-layout`/`.comments-sidebar`/`.comment-gutter`.\n\n5. **Low**: Deleting the active comment can leave stale `activeCommentId`.  \n   - [`CommentsPage.tsx:41`](<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor-demo/src/pages/CommentsPage.tsx:41) removes from list but doesn’t clear `activeCommentId` if the deleted id was active.  \n   - Usually harmless, but it leaves transient stale UI state.\n\nNo correctness issues stood out in the `App.tsx` and `EditorPage.tsx` edits themselves.\n\nTypecheck note: `pnpm -C packages/rich-editor-demo exec tsc --noEmit` currently fails on unrelated existing code at `packages/rich-editor/src/transformers/code-block.ts(28,35)`."}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 13:58:34Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Good feedback. Let me address the critical issues:

1. **High - Selection loss on submit**: Capture the anchor when the popup button is first clicked, not at submit time. Or use `onMouseDown` + `preventDefault()`.
2. **Medium - Stale activeCommentId on delete**: Clear it when deleting the active comment.
3. **Low - Responsive**: Add responsive styles.

Let me also consider #3 about line breaks in highlight, but for a demo app this is an acceptable limitation.

Let me fix issues 1, 2, and 5.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor-demo/src/components/comments/SelectionCommentPopup.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { CommentAnchor } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { buildRangeAnchor } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     3→import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
     4→import { $getSelection, $isRangeSelection } from 'lexical'
     5→import { MessageSquarePlus } from 'lucide-react'
     6→import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     7→import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'
     8→
     9→interface SelectionCommentPopupProps {
    10→  onAdd: (text: string, anchor: CommentAnchor) => void
    11→}
    12→
    13→export function SelectionCommentPopup({ onAdd }: SelectionCommentPopupProps) {
    14→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    15→  const [position, setPosition] = useState<{
    16→    top: number
    17→    left: number
    18→  } | null>(null)
    19→  const [showInput, setShowInput] = useState(false)
    20→  const [inputText, setInputText] = useState('')
    21→  const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)
    22→
    23→  useEffect(() => {
    24→    return editor.registerUpdateListener(({ editorState }) => {
    25→      editorState.read(() => {
    26→        const selection = $getSelection()
    27→        if (
    28→          !$isRangeSelection(selection) ||
    29→          selection.isCollapsed() ||
    30→          showInput
    31→        ) {
    32→          if (!showInput) setPosition(null)
    33→          return
    34→        }
    35→
    36→        const nativeSelection = window.getSelection()
    37→        if (!nativeSelection || nativeSelection.rangeCount === 0) {
    38→          setPosition(null)
    39→          return
    40→        }
    41→
    42→        const range = nativeSelection.getRangeAt(0)
    43→        const rect = range.getBoundingClientRect()
    44→        if (rect.width === 0) {
    45→          setPosition(null)
    46→          return
    47→        }
    48→
    49→        setPosition({
    50→          top: rect.top - 40,
    51→          left: rect.left + rect.width / 2,
    52→        })
    53→      })
    54→    })
    55→  }, [editor, showInput])
    56→
    57→  const handleClick = useCallback(() => {
    58→    setShowInput(true)
    59→    requestAnimationFrame(() => inputRef.current?.focus())
    60→  }, [])
    61→
    62→  const handleSubmit = useCallback(() => {
    63→    const text = inputText.trim()
    64→    if (!text) return
    65→
    66→    const result = buildRangeAnchor(editor)
    67→    if (!result.ok) return
    68→
    69→    onAdd(text, result.anchor)
    70→    setInputText('')
    71→    setShowInput(false)
    72→    setPosition(null)
    73→  }, [editor, inputText, onAdd])
    74→
    75→  const handleKeyDown = useCallback(
    76→    (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
    77→      if (e.key === 'Enter') {
    78→        e.preventDefault()
    79→        handleSubmit()
    80→      } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
    81→        setShowInput(false)
    82→        setInputText('')
    83→      }
    84→    },
    85→    [handleSubmit],
    86→  )
    87→
    88→  const handleCancel = useCallback(() => {
    89→    setShowInput(false)
    90→    setInputText('')
    91→  }, [])
    92→
    93→  if (!position) return null
    94→
    95→  return createPortal(
    96→    <div
    97→      className="comment-selection-popup"
    98→      style={{ top: position.top, left: position.left }}
    99→    >
   100→      {showInput ? (
   101→        <div className="comment-inline-input">
   102→          <input
   103→            ref={inputRef}
   104→            type="text"
   105→            value={inputText}
   106→            onChange={(e) => setInputText(e.target.value)}
   107→            onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
   108→            placeholder="Add a comment..."
   109→            className="comment-input"
   110→          />
   111→          <button className="btn btn-sm btn-active" onClick={handleSubmit}>
   112→            Add
   113→          </button>
   114→          <button className="btn btn-sm" onClick={handleCancel}>
   115→            Cancel
   116→          </button>
   117→        </div>
   118→      ) : (
   119→        <button className="comment-add-btn" onClick={handleClick}>
   120→          <MessageSquarePlus size={14} />
   121→          Comment
   122→        </button>
   123→      )}
   124→    </div>,
   125→    document.body,
   126→  )
   127→}
   128→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 13:59:32Z)**_

零 error，两个 warning 皆为已有代码。实施完成。

**总结：**

创建 5 个新文件，修改 3 个现有文件：

**新建：**
- `CommentsPage.tsx` — 主页面，管理 comments state，双栏布局（editor + sidebar）
- `SelectionCommentPopup.tsx` — 选中文本时浮动气泡，点击捕获 anchor 后显示输入框
- `BlockCommentGutter.tsx` — 段落右侧 gutter，hover 显示 "+" 按钮添加 block 评论
- `CommentHighlightPlugin.tsx` — 用 CSS Custom Highlight API 高亮 range 评论对应文本
- `CommentSidebar.tsx` — 评论侧边栏，显示卡片列表，hover 联动高亮

**修改：**
- `App.tsx` — 新增 Comments tab（Editor 与 Node Showcase 之间）
- `EditorPage.tsx` — 移除 anchor 相关代码（showAnchors, editorRef, anchor panel 等）

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-kit-shiro/src/ShiroRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import {
     2→  codeSnippetNodes,
     3→  embedNodes,
     4→  enhancedRendererConfig,
     5→  galleryNodes,
     6→  TldrawNode,
     7→} from '@haklex/rich-renderers'
     8→import type { RichRendererProps } from '@haklex/rich-static-renderer'
     9→import { RichRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-static-renderer'
    10→import type { Klass, LexicalNode } from 'lexical'
    11→import { useMemo } from 'react'
    12→
    13→const defaultExtraNodes = [
    14→  TldrawNode,
    15→  ...embedNodes,
    16→  ...galleryNodes,
    17→  ...codeSnippetNodes,
    18→]
    19→
    20→export interface ShiroRendererProps extends Omit<
    21→  RichRendererProps,
    22→  'rendererConfig' | 'extraNodes'
    23→> {
    24→  extraNodes?: Array<Klass<LexicalNode>>
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function ShiroRenderer({ extraNodes, ...props }: ShiroRendererProps) {
    28→  const mergedNodes = useMemo(
    29→    () =>
    30→      extraNodes ? [...defaultExtraNodes, ...extraNodes] : defaultExtraNodes,
    31→    [extraNodes],
    32→  )
    33→
    34→  return (
    35→    <RichRenderer
    36→      {...props}
    37→      extraNodes={mergedNodes}
    38→      rendererConfig={enhancedRendererConfig}
    39→    />
    40→  )
    41→}
    42→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

```text
     1→import {
     2→  allNodes,
     3→  ColorSchemeProvider,
     4→  editorTheme,
     5→  FootnoteDefinitionsProvider,
     6→  getVariantClass,
     7→  NestedContentRendererProvider,
     8→  RendererConfigProvider,
     9→} from '@haklex/rich-editor/static'
    10→import { PortalThemeProvider } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
    11→import { createHeadlessEditor } from '@lexical/headless'
    12→import type { LexicalEditor, SerializedEditorState } from 'lexical'
    13→import { $getRoot } from 'lexical'
    14→import {
    15→  cloneElement,
    16→  createElement,
    17→  isValidElement,
    18→  type ReactNode,
    19→} from 'react'
    20→
    21→import { renderBuiltinNode } from './engine/renderBuiltinNode'
    22→import { renderTextNode } from './engine/renderTextNode'
    23→import { preprocessFootnotes } from './preprocess/footnote'
    24→import type { RichRendererProps } from './types'
    25→
    26→interface EditorConfig {
    27→  namespace: string
    28→  theme: Record<string, any>
    29→}
    30→
    31→function wrapDecoration(
    32→  serialized: any,
    33→  key: string,
    34→  decoration: ReactNode,
    35→): ReactNode {
    36→  switch (serialized.type) {
    37→    case 'alert-quote':
    38→      return createElement(
    39→        'div',
    40→        { key, className: `rich-alert rich-alert-${serialized.alertType}` },
    41→        decoration,
    42→      )
    43→    case 'banner':
    44→      return createElement(
    45→        'div',
    46→        { key, className: `rich-banner rich-banner-${serialized.bannerType}` },
    47→        decoration,
    48→      )
    49→    case 'grid-container':
    50→      return createElement(
    51→        'div',
    52→        { key, className: 'rich-grid-container' },
    53→        decoration,
    54→      )
    55→    default:
    56→      if (isValidElement(decoration)) {
    57→        return cloneElement(decoration, { key })
    58→      }
    59→      return decoration
    60→  }
    61→}
    62→
    63→function renderTree(
    64→  node: any,
    65→  editor: LexicalEditor,
    66→  editorConfig: EditorConfig,
    67→  headingSlugs: Map<string, number>,
    68→  key: string,
    69→): ReactNode {
    70→  const nodeKey = node.getKey ? node.getKey() : key
    71→
    72→  if (typeof node.decorate === 'function') {
    73→    try {
    74→      const decoration = node.decorate(editor, editorConfig)
    75→      if (decoration != null) {
    76→        const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}
    77→        return wrapDecoration(serialized, nodeKey, decoration)
    78→      }
    79→    } catch {
    80→      /* fallthrough to builtin */
    81→    }
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  const serialized = node.exportJSON ? node.exportJSON() : {}
    85→
    86→  if (serialized.type === 'text') {
    87→    return renderTextNode(serialized, nodeKey)
    88→  }
    89→
    90→  let children: ReactNode[] | null = null
    91→  if (node.getChildren) {
    92→    const childNodes = node.getChildren()
    93→    if (childNodes.length > 0) {
    94→      children = childNodes.map((child: any, i: number) =>
    95→        renderTree(
    96→          child,
    97→          editor,
    98→          editorConfig,
    99→          headingSlugs,
   100→          `${nodeKey}-${i}`,
   101→        ),
   102→      )
   103→    }
   104→  }
   105→
   106→  return renderBuiltinNode(serialized, nodeKey, children, headingSlugs)
   107→}
   108→
   109→function renderEditorToReact(
   110→  value: SerializedEditorState,
   111→  nodes: any[],
   112→): {
   113→  content: ReactNode
   114→  footnoteData: ReturnType<typeof preprocessFootnotes>
   115→  renderNestedContent: (state: SerializedEditorState) => ReactNode
   116→} {
   117→  const editor = createHeadlessEditor({
   118→    nodes,
   119→    theme: editorTheme,
   120→    editable: false,
   121→    onError: (error: Error) => {
   122→      console.error('[RichRenderer]', error)
   123→    },
   124→  })
   125→
   126→  const editorConfig: EditorConfig = { namespace: 'ssr', theme: editorTheme }
   127→
   128→  const editorState = editor.parseEditorState(value)
   129→  editor.setEditorState(editorState)
   130→
   131→  const footnoteData = preprocessFootnotes(value)
   132→
   133→  let content: ReactNode = null
   134→  editorState.read(() => {
   135→    const root = $getRoot()
   136→    const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
   137→    const children = root
   138→      .getChildren()
   139→      .map((child: any, i: number) =>
   140→        renderTree(child, editor, editorConfig, headingSlugs, `ssr-${i}`),
   141→      )
   142→    content = <>{children}</>
   143→  })
   144→
   145→  const renderNestedContent = (state: SerializedEditorState): ReactNode => {
   146→    const nestedEditor = createHeadlessEditor({
   147→      nodes,
   148→      theme: editorTheme,
   149→      editable: false,
   150→      onError: (error: Error) => {
   151→        console.error('[RichRenderer:nested]', error)
   152→      },
   153→    })
   154→    const nestedEditorConfig: EditorConfig = {
   155→      namespace: 'ssr-nested',
   156→      theme: editorTheme,
   157→    }
   158→    const nestedState = nestedEditor.parseEditorState(state)
   159→    nestedEditor.setEditorState(nestedState)
   160→    let nested: ReactNode = null
   161→    nestedState.read(() => {
   162→      const root = $getRoot()
   163→      const headingSlugs = new Map<string, number>()
   164→      const ch = root
   165→        .getChildren()
   166→        .map((child: any, i: number) =>
   167→          renderTree(
   168→            child,
   169→            nestedEditor,
   170→            nestedEditorConfig,
   171→            headingSlugs,
   172→            `nested-${i}`,
   173→          ),
   174→        )
   175→      nested = <>{ch}</>
   176→    })
   177→    return nested
   178→  }
   179→
   180→  return { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent }
   181→}
   182→
   183→export function RichRenderer({
   184→  value,
   185→  variant = 'article',
   186→  theme = 'light',
   187→  className,
   188→  style,
   189→  as: Component = 'div',
   190→  rendererConfig,
   191→  extraNodes,
   192→}: RichRendererProps) {
   193→  const variantClass = getVariantClass(variant)
   194→  const nodes = extraNodes ? [...allNodes, ...extraNodes] : allNodes
   195→
   196→  const { content, footnoteData, renderNestedContent } = renderEditorToReact(
   197→    value,
   198→    nodes,
   199→  )
   200→
   201→  const classes = ['rich-content', variantClass, className]
   202→    .filter(Boolean)
   203→    .join(' ')
   204→
   205→  return (
   206→    <PortalThemeProvider className={variantClass} theme={theme}>
   207→      <ColorSchemeProvider colorScheme={theme}>
   208→        <RendererConfigProvider
   209→          config={rendererConfig}
   210→          mode="renderer"
   211→          variant={variant}
   212→        >
   213→          <FootnoteDefinitionsProvider
   214→            definitions={footnoteData.definitions}
   215→            displayNumberMap={footnoteData.displayNumberMap}
   216→          >
   217→            <NestedContentRendererProvider value={renderNestedContent}>
   218→              <Component className={classes} style={style} data-theme={theme}>
   219→                {content}
   220→              </Component>
   221→            </NestedContentRendererProvider>
   222→          </FootnoteDefinitionsProvider>
   223→        </RendererConfigProvider>
   224→      </ColorSchemeProvider>
   225→    </PortalThemeProvider>
   226→  )
   227→}
   228→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 14:05:16Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand. ShiroRenderer uses `createHeadlessEditor` (not a live LexicalComposer), so there's no `LexicalComposerContext` available. All the comment plugins that use `useLexicalComposerContext()` will NOT work with ShiroRenderer.

The key differences:
- ShiroRenderer uses headless editor to parse and render static HTML - no live DOM, no LexicalComposer context
- ShiroEditor uses LexicalComposer with a live editor - full context available

For the comment demo to work with ShiroRenderer, we need a fundamentally different approach:
1. Parse the serialized editor state to extract block IDs and text content


2. Render using ShiroRenderer but with data attributes on blocks (or find blocks by DOM structure)
3. Use native DOM Selection API for text selection
4. Map DOM selections back to block IDs

The challenge is that ShiroRenderer generates blocks with generic keys like `ssr-0`, `ssr-1` rather than actual block IDs, and doesn't expose block ID information in the rendered DOM. So I'd need to extract block IDs directly from the serialized state by reading each child's `__state` property, then map the rendered DOM elements to those blocks by their position, and implement selection handling entirely through the native DOM API.

This fundamentally changes the approach—I can't rely on Lexical's plugin system or methods like `useLexicalComposerContext` and `editor.getElementByKey()`. Instead, I'd work directly with the serialized state, find DOM elements by index or data attributes, and use MutationObserver if needed to track changes. Since ShiroRenderer produces static content from the serialized state, the workflow becomes: parse the initial state to extract block metadata, render it with ShiroRenderer, then use refs or DOM queries to locate block elements by their render order and position comment overlays accordingly.

Now I need to figure out how block IDs are stored in the serialized state. The BlockIdPlugin defines a state that should attach `blockId` to each root block, but I need to understand exactly how Lexical serializes this node state into the JSON format so I can extract those IDs when working with the serialized data.

For the comments page implementation, I'm thinking about a practical approach where I parse the serialized editor state to extract block information and IDs, then render the content as read-only and layer the comment interactions on top.

Building the component structure... Writing component structure...

Now I'm checking how the blockId state gets serialized into the node's __state field when using Lexical's createState, so I can verify that the serialized JSON will contain the blockId information I need to map DOM elements back to their corresponding blocks.

Looking at the fixture content, the helper functions like `paragraph(text(...))` probably don't include __state initially—the BlockIdPlugin assigns those IDs at runtime when the editor initializes. This means if I use ShiroRenderer with raw initialContent, there won't be any block IDs present yet.

For the demo, I could either pre-assign block IDs in the fixture, generate them on the fly by index, or use index-based identification instead. Index-based identification would be the simplest approach for now.

Actually, since the comment demo uses static content with no editing, I don't really need the Lexical blockId system at all—I can just generate block IDs from the serialized state or use index-based identification for the renderer.

The real goal here is to demonstrate the comment anchor system working with ShiroRenderer: I need to extract block information from the serialized state, rewrite the gutter component to query the DOM instead of relying on Lexical context, adapt the selection popup to use the native Selection API, and update the highlight plugin to locate DOM elements by their block identifiers.

For mapping a native text selection back to blocks, I'll need to traverse from the selection's anchor and focus nodes up to their containing root elements, then calculate the text offsets within each block. This lets me build range anchors by extracting the quoted text and determining which block the selection spans.

Looking at how ShiroRenderer structures the output, each root block child gets rendered through `renderTree`, which produces semantic HTML elements like `<p>`, `<h1>`, `<h2>`, etc. nested directly under the main component. I can access these as direct children of the content ref, which makes finding the block element straightforward.

Wait, I need to account for nodes like alert-quote and banner that might have different rendering structures...

Actually, for the typical use case this approach should work fine. Let me start implementing the refactored components that use ShiroRenderer instead of Lexical context, relying on DOM queries and native selection events to handle comments.

Refactoring the components... Writing utility functions...

Now for the selection popup, I need to hook into native `selectionchange` events since there's no Lexical context available, then figure out which block the selection is in and build a range anchor from there. to `document` `selectionchange` event and check if the selection falls within the content container, then map it to block and offset information using DOM traversal to build a compatible anchor object. For the gutter, I'll receive the content ref and block infos as props, use ResizeObserver to position buttons, and build anchor objects on click. The highlight plugin becomes a regular component that maps range anchors back to DOM ranges using block indices and text offsets while still leveraging the CSS Highlight API. Now I'll start implementing these changes across the files.

```text
     1→import type { SerializedEditorState, SerializedLexicalNode } from 'lexical'
     2→
     3→// Re-export common node types (defined inline to avoid Lexical package import issues)
     4→type SerializedTextNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
     5→  type: 'text'
     6→  text: string
     7→  format: number
     8→  detail: number
     9→  mode: 'normal' | 'token' | 'segmented'
    10→  style: string
    11→}
    12→
    13→type SerializedParagraphNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    14→  type: 'paragraph'
    15→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    16→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    17→  format: string
    18→  indent: number
    19→  textFormat: number
    20→  textStyle: string
    21→}
    22→
    23→type SerializedHeadingNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    24→  type: 'heading'
    25→  tag: 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6'
    26→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    27→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    28→  format: string
    29→  indent: number
    30→}
    31→
    32→type SerializedQuoteNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    33→  type: 'quote'
    34→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    35→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    36→  format: string
    37→  indent: number
    38→}
    39→
    40→type SerializedListNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    41→  type: 'list'
    42→  listType: 'bullet' | 'number'
    43→  start: number
    44→  tag: 'ul' | 'ol'
    45→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    46→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    47→  format: string
    48→  indent: number
    49→}
    50→
    51→type SerializedListItemNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    52→  type: 'listitem'
    53→  value: number
    54→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    55→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    56→  format: string
    57→  indent: number
    58→}
    59→
    60→type SerializedLinkNode = SerializedLexicalNode & {
    61→  type: 'link'
    62→  url: string
    63→  rel: string | null
    64→  target: string | null
    65→  title: string | null
    66→  children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    67→  direction: 'ltr' | 'rtl' | null
    68→  format: string
    69→  indent: number
    70→}
    71→
    72→// Text formats (bitwise flags)
    73→export const FORMAT_BOLD = 1
    74→export const FORMAT_ITALIC = 1 << 1
    75→export const FORMAT_STRIKETHROUGH = 1 << 2
    76→export const FORMAT_UNDERLINE = 1 << 3
    77→export const FORMAT_CODE = 1 << 4
    78→export const FORMAT_SUBSCRIPT = 1 << 5
    79→export const FORMAT_SUPERSCRIPT = 1 << 6
    80→export const FORMAT_HIGHLIGHT = 1 << 7
    81→
    82→export function text(content: string, format = 0): SerializedTextNode {
    83→  return {
    84→    detail: 0,
    85→    format,
    86→    mode: 'normal',
    87→    style: '',
    88→    text: content,
    89→    type: 'text',
    90→    version: 1,
    91→  }
    92→}
    93→
    94→export function paragraph(
    95→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
    96→): SerializedParagraphNode {
    97→  return {
    98→    children,
    99→    direction: 'ltr',
   100→    format: '',
   101→    indent: 0,
   102→    type: 'paragraph',
   103→    version: 1,
   104→    textFormat: 0,
   105→    textStyle: '',
   106→  }
   107→}
   108→
   109→export function heading(
   110→  tag: 'h1' | 'h2' | 'h3' | 'h4' | 'h5' | 'h6',
   111→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   112→): SerializedHeadingNode {
   113→  return {
   114→    children,
   115→    direction: 'ltr',
   116→    format: '',
   117→    indent: 0,
   118→    tag,
   119→    type: 'heading',
   120→    version: 1,
   121→  }
   122→}
   123→
   124→export function quote(
   125→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   126→): SerializedQuoteNode {
   127→  return {
   128→    children,
   129→    direction: 'ltr',
   130→    format: '',
   131→    indent: 0,
   132→    type: 'quote',
   133→    version: 1,
   134→  }
   135→}
   136→
   137→export function list(
   138→  listType: 'bullet' | 'number',
   139→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   140→): SerializedListNode {
   141→  return {
   142→    children,
   143→    direction: 'ltr',
   144→    format: '',
   145→    indent: 0,
   146→    listType,
   147→    start: 1,
   148→    tag: listType === 'bullet' ? 'ul' : 'ol',
   149→    type: 'list',
   150→    version: 1,
   151→  }
   152→}
   153→
   154→export function listItem(
   155→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   156→): SerializedListItemNode {
   157→  return {
   158→    children,
   159→    direction: 'ltr',
   160→    format: '',
   161→    indent: 0,
   162→    type: 'listitem',
   163→    version: 1,
   164→    value: 1,
   165→  }
   166→}
   167→
   168→export function link(
   169→  url: string,
   170→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   171→): SerializedLinkNode {
   172→  return {
   173→    children,
   174→    direction: 'ltr',
   175→    format: '',
   176→    indent: 0,
   177→    type: 'link',
   178→    version: 1,
   179→    rel: null,
   180→    target: null,
   181→    title: null,
   182→    url,
   183→  }
   184→}
   185→
   186→export function horizontalRule() {
   187→  return {
   188→    type: 'horizontalrule',
   189→    version: 1,
   190→  }
   191→}
   192→
   193→export function lineBreak() {
   194→  return {
   195→    type: 'linebreak',
   196→    version: 1,
   197→  }
   198→}
   199→
   200→export function table(...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]) {
   201→  return {
   202→    children,
   203→    direction: 'ltr' as const,
   204→    format: '',
   205→    indent: 0,
   206→    type: 'table',
   207→    version: 1,
   208→  }
   209→}
   210→
   211→export function tableRow(...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]) {
   212→  return {
   213→    children,
   214→    direction: 'ltr' as const,
   215→    format: '',
   216→    indent: 0,
   217→    type: 'tablerow',
   218→    version: 1,
   219→  }
   220→}
   221→
   222→export function tableCell(
   223→  headerState: number,
   224→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   225→) {
   226→  return {
   227→    children,
   228→    colSpan: 1,
   229→    direction: 'ltr' as const,
   230→    format: '',
   231→    headerState,
   232→    indent: 0,
   233→    type: 'tablecell',
   234→    version: 1,
   235→    width: null,
   236→  }
   237→}
   238→
   239→export function alertQuote(
   240→  alertType: string,
   241→  ...children: SerializedLexicalNode[]
   242→) {
   243→  return {
   244→    type: 'alert-quote',
   245→    alertType,
   246→    content: {
   247→      root: {
   248→        children,
   249→        direction: 'ltr' as const,
   250→        format: '',

   252→        type: 'root' as const,
   253→        version: 1,
   254→      },
   255→    },
   256→    version: 1,
   257→  }
   258→}
   259→

   263→  width?: number
   264→  height?: number
   265→  caption?: string
   266→  thumbhash?: string

   272→    altText: payload.altText,
   273→    width: payload.width,
   274→    height: payload.height,
   275→    caption: payload.caption,

   283→  src: string
   284→  poster?: string
   285→  width?: number

   287→}) {
   288→  return {
   289→    type: 'video',

   292→    width: payload.width,
   293→    height: payload.height,
   294→    version: 1,
   295→  }
   296→}
   297→
   298→export function mermaid(diagram: string) {
   299→  return {
   300→    type: 'mermaid',
   301→    diagram,
   302→    version: 1,
   303→  }
   304→}
   305→

   308→    type: 'katex-block',
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 14:06:04Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the full picture. The serialized state doesn't have blockIds - those are added at runtime by BlockIdPlugin. ShiroRenderer is purely static.

Key architectural shift:
- No LexicalComposer context available
- Need to work with DOM directly
- Block identification by index (child position in the `.rich-content` container)
- Selection handling via native DOM Selection API
- Build anchors from DOM + serialized state, not from Lexical API

Let me rewrite all the comment components. I'll use a `blockId` based on index (e.g., `block-0`, `block-1`...) since the serialized state doesn't have real blockIds.


I need to extract text content recursively from the serialized nodes, and since ShiroRenderer maps each root block to a child in `.rich-content` by index, I can establish a 1:1 correspondence between DOM elements and serialized blocks—even when decorator nodes like alert-quote or banner add wrapper divs, the mapping stays consistent. Now I'll rewrite the component files to use ShiroRenderer with refs for block extraction and native DOM selection handling.

For the comment anchors, I'll stick with the BlockAnchor and RangeAnchor types from `@haklex/rich-editor` since they're structurally sound, but instead of relying on BlockIdPlugin's UUIDs, I'll construct them using index-based identifiers like `block-0` since ShiroRenderer doesn't provide those IDs. anchor objects manually.

Wait, but the types are in `@haklex/rich-editor` which requires... no, just the types are fine. TypeScript types don't need runtime availability.

Actually, I should import the type from the comment-anchor.ts which is exported from @haklex/rich-editor. The types are:
- BlockAnchor: { mode: 'block', blockId, blockType, blockFingerprint, snapshotText }
- RangeAnchor: { mode: 'range', blockId, blockType, blockFingerprint, snapshotText, quote, prefix, suffix, startOffset, endOffset }
- CommentAnchor: BlockAnchor | RangeAnchor

These are pure interfaces, so importing them is fine. I'll construct anchor objects manually from DOM state.

For computing blockFingerprint, I can reuse the same hash logic. I'll need a utility to recursively extract text content from serialized nodes, then apply a djb2 hash to compute the fingerprint. Let me write out the implementation now, keeping each component focused and concise. Writing utility functions...

For the BlockCommentGutter, I need to use the content reference to locate block elements and calculate their positions relative to the gutter, then set up a ResizeObserver to handle layout changes. The CommentHighlightPlugin will find blocks by their index and apply highlighting to the relevant DOM elements.

Now I'm ready to start implementing the files, beginning with CommentsPage since it establishes the shared types and structure. I need to define the BlockInfo type, extract block information from the serialized state, render everything with ShiroRenderer, and pass the content reference and block data down to the comment components. Since ShiroRenderer doesn't accept a ref directly, I'll wrap it in a div to capture the reference. I've got the structure figured out — the content container will hold the ShiroRenderer, and I can query for the `.rich-content` element from there when needed. The Comment interface doesn't need to change since we're building the anchor objects directly instead of using the helper functions, but the structure remains compatible. Now I'm ready to write out the implementation.
</details></think>

ShiroRenderer 用 headless editor 静态渲染，无 LexicalComposer context。需重构所有评论组件为纯 DOM 方式——基于 content 容器 ref + child index 定位 block，用原生 Selection API 处理选区。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 14:06:21Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import type { CommentAnchor, RichEditorVariant } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
import {
  MentionPlatformProvider,
  ShiroRenderer,
} from '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro'
import type { SerializedEditorState } from 'lexical'
import { useCallback, useMemo, useRef, useState } from 'react'

import { BlockCommentGutter } from '../components/comments/BlockCommentGutter'
import { CommentHighlighter } from '../components/comments/CommentHighlightPlugin'
import { CommentSidebar } from '../components/comments/CommentSidebar'
import { SelectionCommentPopup } from '../components/comments/SelectionCommentPopup'
import { useTheme } from '../context/ThemeContext'
import { initialContent } from '../fixtures/initial-content'

export interface Comment {
  id: string
  text: string
  anchor: CommentAnchor
  createdAt: number
}

export interface BlockInfo {
  index: number
  blockId: string
  type: string
  textContent: string
  fingerprint: string
}

function extractTextContent(node: any): string {
  if (node.text) return node.text as string
  if (node.children) {
    return (node.children as any[]).map(extractTextContent).join('')
  }
  return ''
}

function computeFingerprint(text: string): string {
  const input = text.slice(0, 200) + String(text.length)
  let hash = 5381
  for (let i = 0; i < input.length; i++) {
    hash = ((hash << 5) + hash + (input.codePointAt(i) ?? 0)) | 0
  }
  return (hash >>> 0).toString(16)
}

export function extractBlockInfos(state: SerializedEditorState): BlockInfo[] {
  return (state.root.children as any[]).map((child, index) => {
    const textContent = extractTextContent(child)
    return {
      index,
      blockId: (child as any).__state?.blockId || `block-${index}`,
      type: child.type,
      textContent,
      fingerprint: computeFingerprint(textContent),
    }
  })
}

export function CommentsPage() {
  const theme = useTheme()
  const [variant, setVariant] = useState<RichEditorVariant>('article')
  const [comments, setComments] = useState<Comment[]>([])
  const [activeCommentId, setActiveCommentId] = useState<string | null>(null)
  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)

  const blockInfos = useMemo(
    () => extractBlockInfos(initialContent),
    [],
  )

  const handleAddComment = useCallback(
    (text: string, anchor: CommentAnchor) => {
      const comment: Comment = {
        id: crypto.randomUUID(),
        text,
        anchor,
        createdAt: Date.now(),
      }
      setComments((prev) => [...prev, comment])
    },
    [],
  )

  const handleDeleteComment = useCallback((id: string) => {
    setComments((prev) => prev.filter((c) => c.id !== id))
    setActiveCommentId((prev) => (prev === id ? null : prev))
  }, [])

  return (
    <MentionPlatformProvider platforms={{}}>
      <div className="page">
        <div className="toolbar">
          <div className="toolbar-group">
            <span className="toolbar-label">Variant</span>
            <button
              className={variant === 'article' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
              onClick={() => setVariant('article')}
            >
              Article
            </button>
            <button
              className={variant === 'comment' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
              onClick={() => setVariant('comment')}
            >
              Comment
            </button>
            <button
              className={variant === 'note' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
              onClick={() => setVariant('note')}
            >
              Note
            </button>
          </div>
          <div className="toolbar-group">
            <span className="toolbar-label">Info</span>
            <span className="badge">{comments.length} comments</span>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div className="comments-layout">
          <div className="comments-editor" ref={containerRef}>
            <ShiroRenderer
              value={initialContent}
              variant={variant}
              theme={theme}
            />
            <SelectionCommentPopup
              containerRef={containerRef}
              blockInfos={blockInfos}
              onAdd={handleAddComment}
            />
            <BlockCommentGutter
              containerRef={containerRef}
              blockInfos={blockInfos}
              comments={comments}
              onAdd={handleAddComment}
            />
            <CommentHighlighter
              containerRef={containerRef}
              blockInfos={blockInfos}
              comments={comments}
              activeId={activeCommentId}
            />
          </div>

          <div className="comments-sidebar">
            <div className="panel">
              <div className="panel-header">
                <div className="panel-header-left">
                  <h3 className="panel-title">Comments</h3>
                  {comments.length > 0 && (
                    <span className="badge">{comments.length}</span>
                  )}
                </div>
              </div>
              <div className="panel-body">
                <CommentSidebar
                  comments={comments}
                  activeId={activeCommentId}
                  onHover={setActiveCommentId}
                  onDelete={handleDeleteComment}
                />
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </MentionPlatformProvider>
  )
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import type { CommentAnchor, RangeAnchor } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
import { MessageSquarePlus } from 'lucide-react'
import type { RefObject } from 'react'
import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
import { createPortal } from 'react-dom'

import type { BlockInfo } from '../../pages/CommentsPage'

interface SelectionCommentPopupProps {
  containerRef: RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
  blockInfos: BlockInfo[]
  onAdd: (text: string, anchor: CommentAnchor) => void
}

function getContentElement(container: HTMLDivElement): Element | null {
  return container.querySelector('.rich-content')
}

function findBlockIndex(contentEl: Element, node: Node): number {
  const children = Array.from(contentEl.children)
  for (let i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
    if (children[i].contains(node)) return i
  }
  return -1
}

function getTextOffset(
  container: Element,
  targetNode: Node,
  targetOffset: number,
): number {
  const walker = document.createTreeWalker(container, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
  let offset = 0
  let node: Text | null
  while ((node = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    if (node === targetNode) {
      return offset + targetOffset
    }
    offset += node.textContent?.length ?? 0
  }
  return offset
}

function buildRangeAnchorFromSelection(
  sel: Selection,
  contentEl: Element,
  blockInfos: BlockInfo[],
): RangeAnchor | null {
  if (sel.rangeCount === 0 || sel.isCollapsed) return null

  const { anchorNode, focusNode } = sel
  if (!anchorNode || !focusNode) return null

  const anchorIdx = findBlockIndex(contentEl, anchorNode)
  const focusIdx = findBlockIndex(contentEl, focusNode)
  if (anchorIdx < 0 || focusIdx < 0 || anchorIdx !== focusIdx) return null

  const info = blockInfos[anchorIdx]
  if (!info) return null

  const blockEl = contentEl.children[anchorIdx]
  let startOffset = getTextOffset(blockEl, anchorNode, sel.anchorOffset)
  let endOffset = getTextOffset(blockEl, focusNode, sel.focusOffset)
  if (startOffset > endOffset) {
    ;[startOffset, endOffset] = [endOffset, startOffset]
  }

  const { textContent } = info
  const quote = textContent.slice(startOffset, endOffset)
  if (!quote) return null

  return {
    mode: 'range',
    blockId: info.blockId,
    blockType: info.type,
    blockFingerprint: info.fingerprint,
    snapshotText: textContent.slice(0, 300),
    quote,
    prefix: textContent.slice(Math.max(0, startOffset - 50), startOffset),
    suffix: textContent.slice(endOffset, endOffset + 50),
    startOffset,
    endOffset,
  }
}

export function SelectionCommentPopup({
  containerRef,
  blockInfos,
  onAdd,
}: SelectionCommentPopupProps) {
  const [position, setPosition] = useState<{
    top: number
    left: number
  } | null>(null)
  const [showInput, setShowInput] = useState(false)
  const [inputText, setInputText] = useState('')
  const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)
  const capturedAnchorRef = useRef<RangeAnchor | null>(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    function onSelectionChange() {
      if (showInput) return

      const sel = window.getSelection()
      if (!sel || sel.isCollapsed || sel.rangeCount === 0) {
        setPosition(null)
        return
      }

      const container = containerRef.current
      if (!container) return
      const contentEl = getContentElement(container)
      if (!contentEl) return

      const { anchorNode } = sel
      if (!anchorNode || !contentEl.contains(anchorNode)) {
        setPosition(null)
        return
      }

      const range = sel.getRangeAt(0)
      const rect = range.getBoundingClientRect()
      if (rect.width === 0) {
        setPosition(null)
        return
      }

      setPosition({
        top: rect.top - 40,
        left: rect.left + rect.width / 2,
      })
    }

    document.addEventListener('selectionchange', onSelectionChange)
    return () =>
      document.removeEventListener('selectionchange', onSelectionChange)
  }, [containerRef, showInput])

  const handleClick = useCallback(() => {
    const sel = window.getSelection()
    const container = containerRef.current
    if (!sel || !container) return
    const contentEl = getContentElement(container)
    if (!contentEl) return

    const anchor = buildRangeAnchorFromSelection(sel, contentEl, blockInfos)
    if (!anchor) return

    capturedAnchorRef.current = anchor
    setShowInput(true)
    requestAnimationFrame(() => inputRef.current?.focus())
  }, [containerRef, blockInfos])

  const handleSubmit = useCallback(() => {
    const text = inputText.trim()
    if (!text || !capturedAnchorRef.current) return

    onAdd(text, capturedAnchorRef.current)
    capturedAnchorRef.current = null
    setInputText('')
    setShowInput(false)
    setPosition(null)
  }, [inputText, onAdd])

  const handleKeyDown = useCallback(
    (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
      if (e.key === 'Enter') {
        e.preventDefault()
        handleSubmit()
      } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
        setShowInput(false)
        setInputText('')
      }
    },
    [handleSubmit],
  )

  const handleCancel = useCallback(() => {
    setShowInput(false)
    setInputText('')
    capturedAnchorRef.current = null
  }, [])

  if (!position) return null

  return createPortal(
    <div
      className="comment-selection-popup"
      style={{ top: position.top, left: position.left }}
    >
      {showInput ? (
        <div className="comment-inline-input">
          <input
            ref={inputRef}
            type="text"
            value={inputText}
            onChange={(e) => setInputText(e.target.value)}
            onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
            placeholder="Add a comment..."
            className="comment-input"
          />
          <button className="btn btn-sm btn-active" onClick={handleSubmit}>
            Add
          </button>
          <button className="btn btn-sm" onClick={handleCancel}>
            Cancel
          </button>
        </div>
      ) : (
        <button
          className="comment-add-btn"
          onMouseDown={(e) => {
            e.preventDefault()
            handleClick()
          }}
        >
          <MessageSquarePlus size={14} />
          Comment
        </button>
      )}
    </div>,
    document.body,
  )
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import type { BlockAnchor, CommentAnchor } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
import { MessageSquare, Plus } from 'lucide-react'
import type { RefObject } from 'react'
import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'

import type { BlockInfo, Comment } from '../../pages/CommentsPage'

interface GutterBlock {
  index: number
  blockId: string
  top: number
  commentCount: number
}

interface BlockCommentGutterProps {
  containerRef: RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
  blockInfos: BlockInfo[]
  comments: Comment[]
  onAdd: (text: string, anchor: CommentAnchor) => void
}

function getContentElement(container: HTMLDivElement): Element | null {
  return container.querySelector('.rich-content')
}

export function BlockCommentGutter({
  containerRef,
  blockInfos,
  comments,
  onAdd,
}: BlockCommentGutterProps) {
  const [blocks, setBlocks] = useState<GutterBlock[]>([])
  const [hoveredIdx, setHoveredIdx] = useState<number | null>(null)
  const [inputForIdx, setInputForIdx] = useState<number | null>(null)
  const [inputText, setInputText] = useState('')
  const gutterRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
  const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null)

  const updatePositions = useCallback(() => {
    const container = containerRef.current
    if (!container) return
    const contentEl = getContentElement(container)
    if (!contentEl) return

    const containerRect = container.getBoundingClientRect()
    const infos: GutterBlock[] = []

    for (let i = 0; i < contentEl.children.length && i < blockInfos.length; i++) {
      const child = contentEl.children[i]
      const rect = child.getBoundingClientRect()
      const info = blockInfos[i]
      const count = comments.filter((c) => c.anchor.blockId === info.blockId).length

      infos.push({
        index: i,
        blockId: info.blockId,
        top: rect.top - containerRect.top,
        commentCount: count,
      })
    }

    setBlocks(infos)
  }, [containerRef, blockInfos, comments])

  useEffect(() => {
    updatePositions()

    const container = containerRef.current
    if (!container) return

    const observer = new ResizeObserver(updatePositions)
    observer.observe(container)

    return () => observer.disconnect()
  }, [containerRef, updatePositions])

  const handleAddClick = useCallback((idx: number) => {
    setInputForIdx(idx)
    setInputText('')
    requestAnimationFrame(() => inputRef.current?.focus())
  }, [])

  const handleSubmit = useCallback(() => {
    if (inputForIdx == null || !inputText.trim()) return

    const info = blockInfos[inputForIdx]
    if (!info) return

    const anchor: BlockAnchor = {
      mode: 'block',
      blockId: info.blockId,
      blockType: info.type,
      blockFingerprint: info.fingerprint,
      snapshotText: info.textContent.slice(0, 300),
    }

    onAdd(inputText.trim(), anchor)
    setInputForIdx(null)
    setInputText('')
  }, [blockInfos, inputForIdx, inputText, onAdd])

  const handleKeyDown = useCallback(
    (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
      if (e.key === 'Enter') {
        e.preventDefault()
        handleSubmit()
      } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
        setInputForIdx(null)
        setInputText('')
      }
    },
    [handleSubmit],
  )

  return (
    <div className="comment-gutter" ref={gutterRef}>
      {blocks.map((block) => (
        <div
          key={block.blockId}
          className="comment-gutter-item"
          style={{ top: block.top }}
          onMouseEnter={() => setHoveredIdx(block.index)}
          onMouseLeave={() => setHoveredIdx(null)}
        >
          {block.commentCount > 0 && (
            <span className="comment-gutter-count">
              <MessageSquare size={12} />
              {block.commentCount}
            </span>
          )}
          {(hoveredIdx === block.index || inputForIdx === block.index) && (
            <>
              {inputForIdx === block.index ? (
                <div className="comment-gutter-input">
                  <input
                    ref={inputRef}
                    type="text"
                    value={inputText}
                    onChange={(e) => setInputText(e.target.value)}
                    onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
                    onBlur={() => {
                      if (!inputText.trim()) {
                        setInputForIdx(null)
                      }
                    }}
                    placeholder="Comment..."
                    className="comment-input comment-input-sm"
                  />
                  <button
                    className="btn btn-sm btn-active"
                    onClick={handleSubmit}
                  >
                    Add
                  </button>
                </div>
              ) : (
                <button
                  className="comment-gutter-btn"
                  onClick={() => handleAddClick(block.index)}
                  title="Add comment to this block"
                >
                  <Plus size={14} />
                </button>
              )}
            </>
          )}
        </div>
      ))}
    </div>
  )
}

```

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import type { RefObject } from 'react'
import { useEffect } from 'react'

import type { BlockInfo, Comment } from '../../pages/CommentsPage'

interface CommentHighlighterProps {
  containerRef: RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
  blockInfos: BlockInfo[]
  comments: Comment[]
  activeId: string | null
}

function getContentElement(container: HTMLDivElement): Element | null {
  return container.querySelector('.rich-content')
}

function findDomTextNode(
  container: Element,
  targetOffset: number,
): { node: Text; offset: number } | null {
  const walker = document.createTreeWalker(container, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
  let consumed = 0
  let textNode: Text | null
  while ((textNode = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    const len = textNode.textContent?.length ?? 0
    if (consumed + len >= targetOffset) {
      return { node: textNode, offset: targetOffset - consumed }
    }
    consumed += len
  }
  return null
}

export function CommentHighlighter({
  containerRef,
  blockInfos,
  comments,
  activeId,
}: CommentHighlighterProps) {
  useEffect(() => {
    if (typeof CSS === 'undefined' || !('highlights' in CSS)) return

    const { highlights } = CSS as any as { highlights: Map<string, Highlight> }

    function updateHighlights() {
      const normalRanges: Range[] = []
      const activeRanges: Range[] = []

      const rangeComments = comments.filter((c) => c.anchor.mode === 'range')
      if (rangeComments.length === 0) {
        highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
        highlights.delete('comment-highlight-active')
        return
      }

      const container = containerRef.current
      if (!container) return
      const contentEl = getContentElement(container)
      if (!contentEl) return

      for (const comment of rangeComments) {
        const { anchor } = comment
        if (anchor.mode !== 'range') continue

        const blockIdx = blockInfos.findIndex(
          (b) => b.blockId === anchor.blockId,
        )
        if (blockIdx < 0 || blockIdx >= contentEl.children.length) continue

        const blockEl = contentEl.children[blockIdx]
        const start = findDomTextNode(blockEl, anchor.startOffset)
        const end = findDomTextNode(blockEl, anchor.endOffset)
        if (!start || !end) continue

        try {
          const range = new Range()
          range.setStart(start.node, start.offset)
          range.setEnd(end.node, end.offset)
          if (comment.id === activeId) {
            activeRanges.push(range)
          } else {
            normalRanges.push(range)
          }
        } catch {
          // offset out of bounds after layout change
        }
      }

      if (normalRanges.length > 0) {
        highlights.set('comment-highlight', new Highlight(...normalRanges))
      } else {
        highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
      }

      if (activeRanges.length > 0) {
        highlights.set(
          'comment-highlight-active',
          new Highlight(...activeRanges),
        )
      } else {
        highlights.delete('comment-highlight-active')
      }
    }

    updateHighlights()

    return () => {
      highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
      highlights.delete('comment-highlight-active')
    }
  }, [containerRef, blockInfos, comments, activeId])

  return null
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 14:08:13Z)**_

编译成功。已完成重构：

**改动概要：** CommentsPage 改用 `ShiroRenderer`（只读静态渲染），所有评论组件从 Lexical plugin 模式重构为纯 DOM 方式：

- **CommentsPage** — `ShiroEditor` → `ShiroRenderer`，从序列化 state 提取 block 信息（index/blockId/text/fingerprint），通过 `containerRef` 传递 DOM 引用
- **SelectionCommentPopup** — 监听 `document.selectionchange`，用原生 Selection API 定位选区，纯 DOM 遍历计算 block index + text offset，构建 RangeAnchor
- **BlockCommentGutter** — 通过 `.rich-content` 子元素定位 block DOM 位置，用 `ResizeObserver` 跟踪布局变化
- **CommentHighlighter** — 按 blockId → block index → DOM 元素查找，CSS Custom Highlight API 不变
- **CommentSidebar** — 无需改动

---

_**User (2026-02-27 14:09:20Z)**_

当鼠标悬浮在当前的 block 上时，在整体这个 block 的右侧出现一个评论的 button，可以对当前 block 进行评论。

另外，在画词评论之后，我们需要对当前画词评论这一条增加下划线的装饰。

```text
    94→      snapshotText: info.textContent.slice(0, 300),
    95→    }
    96→
    97→    onAdd(inputText.trim(), anchor)
    98→    setInputForIdx(null)
    99→    setInputText('')
   100→  }, [blockInfos, inputForIdx, inputText, onAdd])
   101→
   102→  const handleKeyDown = useCallback(
   103→    (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {
   104→      if (e.key === 'Enter') {
   105→        e.preventDefault()
   106→        handleSubmit()
   107→      } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   108→        setInputForIdx(null)
   109→        setInputText('')
   110→      }
   111→    },
   112→    [handleSubmit],
   113→  )
   114→
   115→  return (
   116→    <div className="comment-gutter" ref={gutterRef}>
   117→      {blocks.map((block) => (
   118→        <div
   119→          key={block.blockId}
   120→          className="comment-gutter-item"
   121→          style={{ top: block.top }}
   122→          onMouseEnter={() => setHoveredIdx(block.index)}
   123→          onMouseLeave={() => setHoveredIdx(null)}
   124→        >
   125→          {block.commentCount > 0 && (
   126→            <span className="comment-gutter-count">
   127→              <MessageSquare size={12} />
   128→              {block.commentCount}
   129→            </span>
   130→          )}
   131→          {(hoveredIdx === block.index || inputForIdx === block.index) && (
   132→            <>
   133→              {inputForIdx === block.index ? (
   134→                <div className="comment-gutter-input">
   135→                  <input
   136→                    ref={inputRef}
   137→                    type="text"
   138→                    value={inputText}
   139→                    onChange={(e) => setInputText(e.target.value)}
   140→                    onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}
   141→                    onBlur={() => {
   142→                      if (!inputText.trim()) {
   143→                        setInputForIdx(null)
   144→                      }
   145→                    }}
   146→                    placeholder="Comment..."
   147→                    className="comment-input comment-input-sm"
   148→                  />
   149→                  <button
   150→                    className="btn btn-sm btn-active"
   151→                    onClick={handleSubmit}
   152→                  >
   153→                    Add
   154→                  </button>
   155→                </div>
   156→              ) : (
   157→                <button
   158→                  className="comment-gutter-btn"
   159→                  onClick={() => handleAddClick(block.index)}
   160→                  title="Add comment to this block"
   161→                >
   162→                  <Plus size={14} />
   163→                </button>
   164→              )}
   165→            </>
   166→          )}
   167→        </div>
   168→      ))}
   169→    </div>
   170→  )
   171→}
   172→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor-demo/src/components/comments/CommentHighlightPlugin.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { RefObject } from 'react'
     2→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     3→
     4→import type { BlockInfo, Comment } from '../../pages/CommentsPage'
     5→
     6→interface CommentHighlighterProps {
     7→  containerRef: RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
     8→  blockInfos: BlockInfo[]
     9→  comments: Comment[]
    10→  activeId: string | null
    11→}
    12→
    13→function getContentElement(container: HTMLDivElement): Element | null {
    14→  return container.querySelector('.rich-content')
    15→}
    16→
    17→function findDomTextNode(
    18→  container: Element,
    19→  targetOffset: number,
    20→): { node: Text; offset: number } | null {
    21→  const walker = document.createTreeWalker(container, NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT)
    22→  let consumed = 0
    23→  let textNode: Text | null
    24→  while ((textNode = walker.nextNode() as Text | null)) {
    25→    const len = textNode.textContent?.length ?? 0
    26→    if (consumed + len >= targetOffset) {
    27→      return { node: textNode, offset: targetOffset - consumed }
    28→    }
    29→    consumed += len
    30→  }
    31→  return null
    32→}
    33→
    34→export function CommentHighlighter({
    35→  containerRef,
    36→  blockInfos,
    37→  comments,
    38→  activeId,
    39→}: CommentHighlighterProps) {
    40→  useEffect(() => {
    41→    if (typeof CSS === 'undefined' || !('highlights' in CSS)) return
    42→
    43→    const { highlights } = CSS as any as { highlights: Map<string, Highlight> }
    44→
    45→    function updateHighlights() {
    46→      const normalRanges: Range[] = []
    47→      const activeRanges: Range[] = []
    48→
    49→      const rangeComments = comments.filter((c) => c.anchor.mode === 'range')
    50→      if (rangeComments.length === 0) {
    51→        highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
    52→        highlights.delete('comment-highlight-active')
    53→        return
    54→      }
    55→
    56→      const container = containerRef.current
    57→      if (!container) return
    58→      const contentEl = getContentElement(container)
    59→      if (!contentEl) return
    60→
    61→      for (const comment of rangeComments) {
    62→        const { anchor } = comment
    63→        if (anchor.mode !== 'range') continue
    64→
    65→        const blockIdx = blockInfos.findIndex(
    66→          (b) => b.blockId === anchor.blockId,
    67→        )
    68→        if (blockIdx === -1 || blockIdx >= contentEl.children.length) continue
    69→
    70→        const blockEl = contentEl.children[blockIdx]
    71→        const start = findDomTextNode(blockEl, anchor.startOffset)
    72→        const end = findDomTextNode(blockEl, anchor.endOffset)
    73→        if (!start || !end) continue
    74→
    75→        try {
    76→          const range = new Range()
    77→          range.setStart(start.node, start.offset)
    78→          range.setEnd(end.node, end.offset)
    79→          if (comment.id === activeId) {
    80→            activeRanges.push(range)
    81→          } else {
    82→            normalRanges.push(range)
    83→          }
    84→        } catch {
    85→          // offset out of bounds after layout change
    86→        }
    87→      }
    88→
    89→      if (normalRanges.length > 0) {
    90→        highlights.set('comment-highlight', new Highlight(...normalRanges))
    91→      } else {
    92→        highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
    93→      }
    94→
    95→      if (activeRanges.length > 0) {
    96→        highlights.set(
    97→          'comment-highlight-active',
    98→          new Highlight(...activeRanges),
    99→        )
   100→      } else {
   101→        highlights.delete('comment-highlight-active')
   102→      }
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    updateHighlights()
   106→
   107→    return () => {
   108→      highlights.delete('comment-highlight')
   109→      highlights.delete('comment-highlight-active')
   110→    }
   111→  }, [containerRef, blockInfos, comments, activeId])
   112→
   113→  return null
   114→}
   115→
```
</details></tool-use>
