     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import type {
     4→  CommentDto,
     5→  CommentModel,
     6→  PaginateResult,
     7→  RequestError,
     8→} from '@mx-space/api-client'
     9→import type { InfiniteData } from '@tanstack/react-query'
    10→import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
    11→import { produce } from 'immer'
    12→import type { ExtractAtomValue } from 'jotai'
    13→import { atom, useAtomValue } from 'jotai'
    14→import { atomWithStorage, selectAtom } from 'jotai/utils'
    15→import type { PropsWithChildren } from 'react'
    16→import { use, useCallback } from 'react'
    17→
    18→import { useIsOwnerLogged } from '~/atoms/hooks/owner'
    19→import { apiClient } from '~/lib/request'
    20→import { getErrorMessageFromRequestError } from '~/lib/request.shared'
    21→import { jotaiStore } from '~/lib/store'
    22→import { toast } from '~/lib/toast'
    23→import { buildCommentsQueryKey } from '~/queries/keys'
    24→
    25→import { MAX_COMMENT_TEXT_LENGTH } from './constants'
    26→import type { createInitialValue } from './providers'
    27→import {
    28→  CommentBoxContext,
    29→  CommentBoxLifeCycleContext,
    30→  CommentCompletedCallbackContext,
    31→  CommentIsReplyContext,
    32→  CommentOriginalRefIdContext,
    33→} from './providers'
    34→
    35→export const useUseCommentReply = () => use(CommentIsReplyContext)
    36→
    37→export const useCommentOriginalRefId = () => {
    38→  const fallbackRefId = useAtomValue(use(CommentBoxContext).refId)
    39→  return use(CommentOriginalRefIdContext) || fallbackRefId
    40→}
    41→
    42→export const useCommentCompletedCallback = () =>
    43→  use(CommentCompletedCallbackContext)
    44→
    45→export const useCommentBoxTextValue = () =>
    46→  useAtomValue(use(CommentBoxContext).text)
    47→
    48→export const useCommentBoxRefIdValue = () =>
    49→  useAtomValue(use(CommentBoxContext).refId)
    50→
    51→export const useGetCommentBoxAtomValues = () => use(CommentBoxContext)
    52→export const useCommentBoxLifeCycle = () => use(CommentBoxLifeCycleContext)
    53→
    54→// ReactNode 导致 tsx 无法推断，过于复杂
    55→const commentActionLeftSlotAtom = atom(null as PropsWithChildren['children'])
    56→export const useCommentActionLeftSlot = () =>
    57→  useAtomValue(commentActionLeftSlotAtom)
    58→
    59→export const setCommentActionLeftSlot = (slot: PropsWithChildren['children']) =>
    60→  jotaiStore.set(commentActionLeftSlotAtom, slot)
    61→
    62→export const useCommentBoxHasText = () =>
    63→  useAtomValue(
    64→    selectAtom(
    65→      use(CommentBoxContext).text,
    66→      useCallback((v) => v.length > 0, []),
    67→    ),
    68→  )
    69→
    70→export const useCommentBoxTextIsOversize = () =>
    71→  useAtomValue(
    72→    selectAtom(
    73→      use(CommentBoxContext).text,
    74→      useCallback((v) => v.length > MAX_COMMENT_TEXT_LENGTH, []),
    75→    ),
    76→  )
    77→type CommentContextValue = ReturnType<typeof createInitialValue>
    78→
    79→export const useSetCommentBoxValues = <
    80→  T extends keyof CommentContextValue,
    81→>() => {
    82→  const ctx = use(CommentBoxContext)
    83→  return useCallback(
    84→    (key: T, value: ExtractAtomValue<CommentContextValue[T]>) => {
    85→      const atom = ctx[key]
    86→      if (!atom) throw new Error(`atom ${key} not found`)
    87→      jotaiStore.set(atom as any, value)
    88→    },
    89→    [ctx],
    90→  )
    91→}
    92→
    93→// Comment Mode
    94→
    95→export const enum CommentBoxMode {
    96→  'legacy',
    97→  'with-auth',
    98→}
    99→
   100→const commentModeAtom = atomWithStorage(
   101→  'comment-mode',
   102→  CommentBoxMode['with-auth'],
   103→)
   104→
   105→export const useCommentMode = () => useAtomValue(commentModeAtom)
   106→export const setCommentMode = (mode: CommentBoxMode) =>
   107→  jotaiStore.set(commentModeAtom, mode)
   108→
   109→export const useSendComment = () => {
   110→  const commentRefId = useCommentBoxRefIdValue()
   111→  const {
   112→    text: textAtom,
   113→    author: authorAtom,
   114→    mail: mailAtom,
   115→    url: urlAtom,
   116→
   117→    source: sourceAtom,
   118→    avatar: avatarAtom,
   119→
   120→    isWhisper: isWhisperAtom,
   121→    syncToRecently: syncToRecentlyAtom,
   122→  } = useGetCommentBoxAtomValues()
   123→  const { afterSubmit } = useCommentBoxLifeCycle()
   124→  const isLogged = useIsOwnerLogged()
   125→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
   126→  const isReply = useUseCommentReply()
   127→  const originalRefId = useCommentOriginalRefId()
   128→  const completedCallback = useCommentCompletedCallback()
   129→
   130→  const wrappedCompletedCallback = <T extends CommentModel>(data: T): T => {
   131→    completedCallback?.(data)
   132→    return data
   133→  }
   134→
   135→  const { mutate, isPending } = useMutation({
   136→    mutationFn: async (refId: string) => {
   137→      const text = jotaiStore.get(textAtom)
   138→      const author = jotaiStore.get(authorAtom)
   139→      const mail = jotaiStore.get(mailAtom)
   140→      const avatar = jotaiStore.get(avatarAtom)
   141→      const source = jotaiStore.get(sourceAtom) as any
   142→      const url = jotaiStore.get(urlAtom)
   143→
   144→      const commentDto: CommentDto = { text, author, mail, avatar, source, url }
   145→
   146→      if (isLogged) {
   147→        delete commentDto.avatar
   148→      }
   149→
   150→      // Omit empty string key
   151→      Object.keys(commentDto).forEach((key) => {
   152→        // @ts-expect-error
   153→        if (commentDto[key] === '') delete commentDto[key]
   154→      })
   155→
   156→      // Reply Comment
   157→      if (isReply) {
   158→        if (isLogged) {
   159→          return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
   160→            .reply(refId)
   161→            .post<CommentModel>({
   162→              data: {
   163→                text,
   164→                source,
   165→              },
   166→            })
   167→            .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   168→        } else {
   169→          return apiClient.comment
   170→            .reply(refId, commentDto)
   171→            .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   172→        }
   173→      }
   174→
   175→      // Normal Comment
   176→      const isWhisper = jotaiStore.get(isWhisperAtom)
   177→      const syncToRecently = jotaiStore.get(syncToRecentlyAtom)
   178→
   179→      if (isLogged) {
   180→        return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
   181→          .comment(refId)
   182→          .post<CommentModel>({
   183→            data: { text, source },
   184→          })
   185→          .then(async (res) => {
   186→            if (syncToRecently)
   187→              apiClient.recently.proxy
   188→                .post({
   189→                  data: {
   190→                    content: text,
   191→                    ref: refId,
   192→                  },
   193→                })
   194→                .then(() => {
   195→                  toast.success('已同步到碎碎念')
   196→                })
   197→
   198→            return res
   199→          })
   200→          .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   201→      }
   202→      // @ts-ignore
   203→      commentDto.isWhispers = isWhisper
   204→      return apiClient.comment
   205→        .comment(refId, commentDto)
   206→        .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   207→    },
   208→    mutationKey: [commentRefId, 'comment'],
   209→    onError(error: RequestError) {
   210→      toast.error(getErrorMessageFromRequestError(error))
   211→    },
   212→    onSuccess(data) {
   213→      afterSubmit?.()
   214→
   215→      const toastCopy = isLogged
   216→        ? '发表成功啦~'
   217→        : isReply
   218→          ? '感谢你的回复！'
   219→          : '感谢你的评论！'
   220→
   221→      const commentListQueryKey = buildCommentsQueryKey(originalRefId)
   222→
   223→      toast.success(toastCopy)
   224→      jotaiStore.set(textAtom, '')
   225→
   226→      queryClient.setQueryData<
   227→        InfiniteData<
   228→          PaginateResult<
   229→            CommentModel & {
   230→              ref: string
   231→            }
   232→          >
   233→        >
   234→      >(commentListQueryKey, (oldData) => {
   235→        if (!oldData) return oldData
   236→        if (isReply) {
   237→          // find the reply refed comment
   238→
   239→          return produce(oldData, (draft) => {
   240→            const dfs = (
   241→              data: CommentModel,
   242→              commentRefId: string,
   243→              newData: CommentModel & { new?: boolean },
   244→            ) => {
   245→              if (data.id === commentRefId) {
   246→                if (!data.children) {
   247→                  data.children = []
   248→                }
   249→                ;(data.children as (CommentModel & { new: boolean })[]).push({
   250→                  ...newData,
   251→                  new: true,
   252→                })
   253→                return true
   254→              }
   255→              if (!data.children) {
   256→                return
   257→              }
   258→              for (const child of data.children) {
   259→                if (dfs(child, commentRefId, newData)) {
   260→                  return true
   261→                }
   262→              }
   263→              return false
   264→            }
   265→
   266→            const dataToAdd = {
   267→              ...data,
   268→              new: true,
   269→            }
   270→
   271→            for (const page of draft.pages) {
   272→              for (const item of page.data) {
   273→                if (dfs(item, commentRefId, dataToAdd)) {
   274→                  break
   275→                }
   276→              }
   277→            }
   278→          })
   279→        }
   280→
   281→        return produce(oldData, (draft) => {
   282→          draft.pages[0].data.unshift({
   283→            ...data,
   284→            // @ts-ignore
   285→            new: true,
   286→          })
   287→        })
   288→      })
   289→    },
   290→  })
   291→
   292→  return [
   293→    useCallback(() => mutate(commentRefId), [commentRefId, mutate]),
   294→    isPending,
   295→  ] as const
   296→}
   297→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import 'client-only'
     2→
     3→import { nanoid } from 'nanoid'
     4→import { createFetch } from 'ofetch'
     5→
     6→import PKG from '~/../package.json'
     7→
     8→import { createApiClient, createFetchAdapter } from './shared'
     9→
    10→const isDev = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'
    11→const isServerSide = typeof window === 'undefined'
    12→const uuidStorageKey = 'x-uuid'
    13→const uuid = nanoid()
    14→
    15→const globalConfigureHeader = {} as any
    16→const globalConfigureSearchParams = {} as any
    17→
    18→if (isServerSide) {
    19→  globalConfigureHeader['User-Agent'] =
    20→    `NextJS/v${PKG.dependencies.next} ${PKG.name}/${PKG.version}`
    21→}
    22→
    23→export const $fetch = createFetch({
    24→  defaults: {
    25→    timeout: 8000,
    26→    credentials: 'include',
    27→    headers: globalConfigureHeader,
    28→    onRequest(context) {
    29→      // eslint-disable-next-line prefer-destructuring
    30→      let headers: any = context.options.headers
    31→      if (headers && headers instanceof Headers) {
    32→        headers = Object.fromEntries(headers.entries())
    33→      } else {
    34→        headers = {}
    35→      }
    36→
    37→      headers['X-Session-Uuid'] =
    38→        globalThis?.sessionStorage?.getItem(uuidStorageKey) ?? uuid
    39→
    40→      context.options.params ??= {}
    41→      Object.assign(context.options.params, globalConfigureSearchParams)
    42→
    43→      if (isDev && isServerSide) {
    44→        console.info(`[Request]: ${context.request}`)
    45→      }
    46→
    47→      context.options.headers = headers
    48→    },
    49→    onResponse(context) {
    50→      if (isDev && isServerSide) {
    51→        console.info(`[Response]: ${context.request}`, context.response.status)
    52→      }
    53→    },
    54→  },
    55→})
    56→
    57→export const apiClient = createApiClient(createFetchAdapter($fetch))
    58→export const attachFetchHeader = (key: string, value: string | null) => {
    59→  const original = globalConfigureHeader[key]
    60→  if (value === null) {
    61→    delete globalConfigureHeader[key]
    62→  } else {
    63→    globalConfigureHeader[key] = value
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  return () => {
    67→    if (original === undefined) {
    68→      delete globalConfigureHeader[key]
    69→    } else {
    70→      globalConfigureHeader[key] = original
    71→    }
    72→  }
    73→}
    74→
    75→export const setGlobalSearchParams = (params: Record<string, any>) => {
    76→  clearGlobalSearchParams()
    77→  Object.assign(globalConfigureSearchParams, params)
    78→}
    79→
    80→export const clearGlobalSearchParams = () => {
    81→  Object.keys(globalConfigureSearchParams).forEach((key) => {
    82→    delete globalConfigureSearchParams[key]
    83→  })
    84→}
    85→export const isReactClient = true
    86→
    87→if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    88→  Object.defineProperty(window, 'api', {
    89→    get() {
    90→      return apiClient
    91→    },
    92→  })
    93→}
    94→
```

 `./apps/web/src/lib/fetch/shared.ts`

```text
     1→import type { IRequestAdapter } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     2→import createClient, { allControllers } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     3→import type { $fetch } from 'ofetch'
     4→
     5→import { API_URL } from '~/constants/env'
     6→
     7→type FetchType = typeof $fetch
     8→export const createFetchAdapter = (
     9→  $fetch: FetchType,
    10→): IRequestAdapter<typeof $fetch> => ({
    11→  default: $fetch,
    12→  get(url: string, options) {
    13→    const { params } = options || {}
    14→    return $fetch(url, {
    15→      method: 'GET',
    16→      query: params,
    17→    })
    18→  },
    19→  post(url: string, options) {
    20→    const { params, data } = options || {}
    21→    return $fetch(url, {
    22→      method: 'post',
    23→      query: params,
    24→      body: data,
    25→    })
    26→  },
    27→  put(url: string, options) {
    28→    const { params, data } = options || {}
    29→    return $fetch(url, {
    30→      method: 'put',
    31→      query: params,
    32→      body: data,
    33→    })
    34→  },
    35→  patch(url: string, options) {
    36→    const { params, data } = options || {}
    37→    return $fetch(url, {
    38→      method: 'patch',
    39→      query: params,
    40→      body: data,
    41→    })
    42→  },
    43→  delete(url: string, options) {
    44→    const { params, data } = options || {}
    45→    return $fetch(url, {
    46→      method: 'delete',
    47→      query: params,
    48→      body: data,
    49→    })
    50→  },
    51→})
    52→export const createApiClient = (
    53→  fetchAdapter: ReturnType<typeof createFetchAdapter>,
    54→) =>
    55→  createClient(fetchAdapter)(API_URL, {
    56→    controllers: allControllers,
    57→    getDataFromResponse(response) {
    58→      return response as any
    59→    },
    60→  })
    61→
```
</details></tool-use>

Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## Summary: Comment Anchor Data Flow Through the API

Based on my investigation, here's how the comment anchor data flows through the API:

### 1. **mx-core Comment Controller** (`<GIT_DIR>/mx-core/apps/core/src/modules/comment/comment.controller.ts`)

- **Line 200**: The `comment()` POST endpoint receives a `CommentDto` body that OMITS anchor (see schema definition below)
- **Line 223**: The body is merged with IP location data: `const model: Partial<CommentModel> = { ...body, ...ipLocation }`
- The anchor field is NOT passed through the standard `CommentDto` - it's stripped out before the DTO validation

### 2. **mx-core Comment Schema** (`<GIT_DIR>/mx-core/apps/core/src/modules/comment/comment.schema.ts`)

The schema defines TWO anchor modes with full validation:

**Lines 9-15: BlockCommentAnchorSchema**
```typescript
const BlockCommentAnchorSchema = z.object({
  mode: z.literal(CommentAnchorMode.Block),
  blockId: z.string().trim().min(1).max(128),
  blockType: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
  blockFingerprint: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
  snapshotText: z.string().max(20000).optional(),
})
```

**Lines 17-33: RangeCommentAnchorSchema**
```typescript
const RangeCommentAnchorSchema = z.object({
  mode: z.literal(CommentAnchorMode.Range),
  blockId: z.string().trim().min(1).max(128),
  blockType: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
  blockFingerprint: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
  snapshotText: z.string().max(20000).optional(),
  quote: z.string().min(1).max(1000),
  prefix: z.string().max(300).default(''),
  suffix: z.string().max(300).default(''),
  startOffset: z.number().int().min(0),
  endOffset: z.number().int().min(0),
})
```

**Lines 43-65: Full CommentSchema with anchor**
```typescript
export const CommentSchema = z.object({
  author: z.string().min(1).max(20),
  text: z.string().min(1).max(500),
  mail: z.string().email().max(50),
  url: z.string().url().max(50).optional(),
  isWhispers: z.boolean().optional(),
  source: z.string().optional(),
  avatar: z.string().url().refine(...).optional(),
  anchor: CommentAnchorSchema.optional(),  // ← ANCHOR IS HERE
})
```

**Line 67: CommentDto OMITS anchor**
```typescript
export class CommentDto extends createZodDto(CommentSchema.omit({ anchor: true })) {}
```

### 3. **mx-core Comment Model** (`<GIT_DIR>/mx-core/apps/core/src/modules/comment/comment.model.ts`)

**Lines 26-65: CommentAnchorModel class**
```typescript
export class CommentAnchorModel {
  @prop({ required: true, enum: CommentAnchorMode, type: String })
  mode: CommentAnchorMode
  @prop({ required: true, trim: true })
  blockId: string
  @prop({ trim: true })
  blockType?: string
  @prop({ trim: true })
  blockFingerprint?: string
  @prop()
  snapshotText?: string
  @prop()
  quote?: string
  @prop()
  prefix?: string
  @prop()
  suffix?: string
  @prop()
  startOffset?: number
  @prop()
  endOffset?: number
  @prop()
  contentHashAtCreate?: string
  @prop()
  contentHashCurrent?: string
  @prop()
  lastResolvedAt?: Date
}
```

**Lines 179-180: CommentModel stores anchor**
```typescript
@prop({ type: () => CommentAnchorModel, _id: false })
anchor?: CommentAnchorModel
```

### 4. **@mx-space/api-client Types** (`<REPO_ROOT>/node_modules/.pnpm/@mx-space+api-client@2.1.1/`)

**CommentDto interface (does NOT include anchor)**:
```typescript
interface CommentDto {
  author: string;
  text: string;
  mail: string;
  url?: string;
  source?: 'github' | 'google';
  avatar?: string;
}
```

**CommentModel interface (does NOT include anchor)**:
```typescript
interface CommentModel extends BaseModel {
  refType: CollectionRefTypes;
  ref: string;
  state: number;
  commentsIndex: number;
  author: string;
  text: string;
  mail?: string;
  url?: string;
  ip?: string;
  agent?: string;
  key: string;
  pin?: boolean;
  avatar: string;
  parent?: CommentModel | string;
  children: CommentModel[];
  isWhispers?: boolean;
  location?: string;
  source?: string;
  readerId?: string;
  editedAt?: string;
}
```

### 5. **Shiroi Web App Comment Creation** (`<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/hooks.tsx`)

**Lines 109-296: useSendComment() hook**

Currently the web app creates comments WITHOUT anchor support:

```typescript
const commentDto: CommentDto = { text, author, mail, avatar, source, url }
```

The code DOES NOT pass anchor data to the API.

### 6. **Shiroi Comment Anchor Utils** (`<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/utils/comment-anchor.ts`)

The infrastructure exists to BUILD anchors:

```typescript
export interface BlockAnchor {
  mode: 'block'
  blockId: string
  blockType: string
  blockFingerprint: string
  snapshotText: string
}

export interface RangeAnchor {
  mode: 'range'
  blockId: string
  blockType: string
  blockFingerprint: string
  snapshotText: string
  quote: string
  prefix: string
  suffix: string
  startOffset: number
  endOffset: number
}

export function buildBlockAnchor(editor: LexicalEditor, blockKey?: string): AnchorResult<BlockAnchor>
export function buildRangeAnchor(editor: LexicalEditor): AnchorResult<RangeAnchor>
```

### KEY FINDINGS:

1. **Backend accepts anchor**: mx-core has full anchor support in CommentSchema (optional), CommentAnchorModel, and stores it in CommentModel.anchor

2. **API client doesn't expose anchor**: The @mx-space/api-client TypeScript types for CommentDto and CommentModel don't include anchor field

3. **Web app doesn't send anchor**: The Shiroi web app currently doesn't pass anchor when creating comments

4. **Frontend can build anchors**: The rich-editor package has full utilities to build BlockAnchor or RangeAnchor from Lexical editor state

### TO PASS ANCHOR DATA:

You need to:
1. Update CommentDto type in @mx-space/api-client to include optional `anchor` field matching `CommentAnchorInput` type from mx-core
2. Modify the web app's `useSendComment()` hook to build and include anchor data:
```typescript
import { buildRangeAnchor, buildBlockAnchor } from '@haklex/rich-editor'

const anchor = buildRangeAnchor(editor) // or buildBlockAnchor
if (anchor.ok) {
  commentDto.anchor = anchor.anchor
}
```
3. Pass the anchor in the POST data: `apiClient.comment.comment(refId, commentDto)`

    42→import {
    43→  BatchCommentDeleteDto,
    44→  BatchCommentStateDto,
    45→  CommentDto,
    46→  CommentRefTypesDto,
    47→  CommentStatePatchDto,
    48→  EditCommentDto,
    49→  TextOnlyDto,
    50→} from './comment.schema'
    51→import { CommentService } from './comment.service'
    52→
    53→const idempotenceMessage = '哦吼，这句话你已经说过啦'
    54→const NESTED_REPLY_MAX = 10
    55→@ApiController({ path: 'comments' })
    56→@UseInterceptors(CommentFilterEmailInterceptor)
    57→export class CommentController {
    58→  constructor(
    59→    private readonly commentService: CommentService,
    60→    private readonly eventManager: EventManagerService,
    61→    private readonly configsService: ConfigsService,
    62→    private readonly ownerService: OwnerService,
    63→    @Inject(forwardRef(() => ReaderService))
    64→    private readonly readerService: ReaderService,
    65→  ) {}
    66→
    67→  @Get('/')
    68→  @Auth()
    69→  async getRecentlyComments(@Query() query: PagerDto) {
    70→    const { size = 10, page = 1, state = 0 } = query
    71→
    72→    const comments = await this.commentService.getComments({
    73→      size,
    74→      page,
    75→      state,
    76→    })
    77→    const readers = await this.readerService.findReaderInIds(
    78→      comments.docs.map((doc) => doc.readerId).filter(Boolean) as string[],
    79→    )
    80→
    81→    const res = transformDataToPaginate(comments)
    82→    Object.assign(res, {
    83→      readers: keyBy(readers, 'id'),
    84→    })
    85→    return res
    86→  }
    87→
    88→  @Get('/:id')
    89→  async getComments(
    90→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
    91→    @IsAuthenticated() isAuthenticated: boolean,
    92→  ) {
    93→    const { id } = params
    94→    const data: CommentModel | null = await this.commentService.model
    95→      .findOne({
    96→        _id: id,
    97→      })
    98→      .populate('parent')
    99→      .lean()
   100→
   101→    if (!data) {
   102→      throw new CannotFindException()
   103→    }
   104→    if (data.isWhispers && !isAuthenticated) {
   105→      throw new CannotFindException()
   106→    }
   107→
   108→    await this.commentService.fillAndReplaceAvatarUrl([data])
   109→    if (data.readerId) {
   110→      const reader = await this.readerService.findReaderInIds([data.readerId])
   111→      Object.assign(data, {
   112→        reader: reader[0],
   113→      })
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    return data
   117→  }
   118→
   119→  // 面向 C 端的评论查询接口
   120→  @Get('/ref/:id')
   121→  async getCommentsByRefId(
   122→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
   123→    @Query() query: PagerDto,
   124→    @IsAuthenticated() isAuthenticated: boolean,
   125→  ) {
   126→    const { id } = params
   127→    const { page = 1, size = 10 } = query
   128→
   129→    const configs = await this.configsService.get('commentOptions')
   130→    const { commentShouldAudit } = configs
   131→
   132→    const $and: QueryFilter<CommentModel & Document<any, any, any>>[] = [
   133→      {
   134→        parent: undefined,
   135→        ref: id,
   136→      },
   137→      {
   138→        $or: commentShouldAudit
   139→          ? [
   140→              {
   141→                state: CommentState.Read,
   142→              },
   143→            ]
   144→          : [
   145→              {
   146→                state: CommentState.Read,
   147→              },
   148→              { state: CommentState.Unread },
   149→            ],
   150→      },
   151→    ]
   152→
   153→    if (!isAuthenticated) {
   154→      $and.push({
   155→        $or: [
   156→          { isWhispers: false },
   157→          {
   158→            isWhispers: { $exists: false },
   159→          },
   160→        ],
   161→      })
   162→    }
   163→    const comments = await this.commentService.model.paginate(
   164→      {
   165→        $and,
   166→      },
   167→      {
   168→        limit: size,
   169→        page,
   170→        sort: { pin: -1, created: -1 },
   171→        populate: {
   172→          path: 'children',
   173→          maxDepth: NESTED_REPLY_MAX,
   174→        },
   175→      },
   176→    )
   177→
   178→    await this.commentService.fillAndReplaceAvatarUrl(comments.docs)
   179→    this.commentService.cleanDirtyData(comments.docs)
   180→    const result = transformDataToPaginate(comments)
   181→    const readerIds = comments.docs
   182→      .map((comment) => comment.readerId)
   183→      .filter(Boolean) as string[]
   184→    const readers = await this.readerService.findReaderInIds(readerIds)
   185→
   186→    Object.assign(result, {
   187→      readers: keyBy(readers, 'id'),
   188→    })
   189→
   190→    return result
   191→  }
   192→
   193→  @Post('/:id')
   194→  @HTTPDecorators.Idempotence({
   195→    expired: 20,
   196→    errorMessage: idempotenceMessage,
   197→  })
   198→  async comment(
   199→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
   200→    @Body() body: CommentDto,
   201→    @IsAuthenticated() isAuthenticated: boolean,
   202→    @IpLocation() ipLocation: IpRecord,
   203→    @Query() query: CommentRefTypesDto,
   204→  ) {
   205→    const { disableComment } = await this.configsService.get('commentOptions')
   206→    if (disableComment) {
   207→      throw new BizException(ErrorCodeEnum.CommentDisabled)
   208→    }
   209→    if (!isAuthenticated) {
   210→      await this.commentService.validAuthorName(body.author)
   211→    }
   212→
   213→    const { ref } = query
   214→
   215→    const id = params.id
   216→    if (
   217→      !(await this.commentService.allowComment(id, ref)) &&
   218→      !isAuthenticated
   219→    ) {
   220→      throw new BizException(ErrorCodeEnum.CommentForbidden)
   221→    }
   222→
   223→    const model: Partial<CommentModel> = { ...body, ...ipLocation }
   224→
   225→    const comment = await this.commentService.createComment(id, model, ref)
   226→
   227→    this.commentService.afterCreateComment(
   228→      comment.id,
   229→      ipLocation,
   230→      isAuthenticated,
   231→    )
   232→
   233→    return this.commentService
   234→      .fillAndReplaceAvatarUrl([comment])
   235→      .then((docs) => docs[0])
   236→  }
   237→
   238→  @Post('/reply/:id')
   239→  @HTTPDecorators.Idempotence({
   240→    expired: 20,
   241→    errorMessage: idempotenceMessage,
   242→  })
   243→  async replyByCid(
   244→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
   245→    @Body() body: CommentDto,
   246→    @Body('author') author: string,
   247→    @IsAuthenticated() isAuthenticated: boolean,
   248→    @IpLocation() ipLocation: IpRecord,
   249→  ) {
   250→    const { disableComment } = await this.configsService.get('commentOptions')
   251→    if (disableComment) {
   252→      throw new BizException(ErrorCodeEnum.CommentDisabled)
   253→    }
   254→
   255→    if (!isAuthenticated) {
   256→      await this.commentService.validAuthorName(author)
   257→    }
   258→
   259→    const { id } = params
   260→
   261→    const parent = await this.commentService.model.findById(id).populate('ref')
   262→    if (!parent) {
   263→      throw new CannotFindException()
   264→    }
   265→    const commentIndex = parent.commentsIndex
   266→
   267→    if (parent.key && parent.key.split('#').length >= NESTED_REPLY_MAX) {
   268→      throw new BizException(ErrorCodeEnum.CommentTooDeep)
   269→    }
   270→
   271→    const key = `${parent.key}#${commentIndex}`
   272→
   273→    const model: Partial<CommentModel> = {
   274→      parent,
   275→      ref: (parent.ref as DocumentType<any>)._id,
   276→      refType: parent.refType,
   277→      ...body,
   278→      ...ipLocation,
   279→      key,
   280→      isWhispers: parent.isWhispers,
   281→      state: isAuthenticated ? CommentState.Read : CommentState.Unread,
   282→    }
   283→
   284→    await this.commentService.assignReaderToComment(model)
   285→
   286→    const comment = await this.commentService.model.create(model)
   287→    const commentId = comment._id.toString()
   288→    scheduleManager.schedule(async () => {
   289→      if (isAuthenticated) {
   290→        return
   291→      }
   292→      await this.commentService.appendIpLocation(commentId, ipLocation.ip)
   293→    })
   294→
   295→    await parent.updateOne({
   296→      $push: {
   297→        children: comment._id,
   298→      },
   299→      $inc: {
   300→        commentsIndex: 1,
   301→      },
   302→      state:
   303→        comment.state === CommentState.Read &&
   304→        parent.state !== CommentState.Read
   305→          ? CommentState.Read
   306→          : parent.state,
   307→    })
   308→    if (isAuthenticated) {
   309→      this.commentService.sendEmail(comment, CommentReplyMailType.Guest)
   310→      this.eventManager.broadcast(BusinessEvents.COMMENT_CREATE, comment, {
   311→        scope: EventScope.TO_SYSTEM_VISITOR,
   312→      })
   313→    } else {
   314→      const configs = await this.configsService.get('commentOptions')
   315→      const { commentShouldAudit } = configs
   316→
   317→      if (commentShouldAudit) {
   318→        this.eventManager.broadcast(BusinessEvents.COMMENT_CREATE, comment, {
   319→          scope: EventScope.TO_SYSTEM_ADMIN,
   320→        })
   321→        return
   322→      }
   323→
   324→      this.commentService.sendEmail(comment, CommentReplyMailType.Owner)
   325→      this.eventManager.broadcast(BusinessEvents.COMMENT_CREATE, comment, {
   326→        scope: EventScope.ALL,
   327→      })
   328→    }
   329→    return this.commentService
   330→      .fillAndReplaceAvatarUrl([comment])
   331→      .then((docs) => docs[0])
   332→  }
   333→
   334→  @Post('/owner/comment/:id')
   335→  @Auth()
   336→  @HTTPDecorators.Idempotence({
   337→    expired: 20,
   338→    errorMessage: idempotenceMessage,
   339→  })
   340→  async commentByOwner(
   341→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
   342→    @Body() body: TextOnlyDto,
   343→    @IpLocation() ipLocation: IpRecord,
   344→    @Query() query: CommentRefTypesDto,
   345→  ) {
   346→    const owner = await this.ownerService.getOwner()
   347→    const { name, mail, url } = owner
   348→    const model: CommentDto = {
   349→      author: name,
   350→      ...body,
   351→      mail,
   352→      url,
   353→      state: CommentState.Read,
   354→    } as CommentDto
   355→    return await this.comment(params, model as any, true, ipLocation, query)
   356→  }
   357→
   358→  @Post('/owner/reply/:id')
   359→  @Auth()
   360→  @HTTPDecorators.Idempotence({
   361→    expired: 20,
   362→    errorMessage: idempotenceMessage,
   363→  })
   364→  async replyByOwner(
   365→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
   366→    @Body() body: TextOnlyDto,
   367→    @IpLocation() ipLocation: IpRecord,
   368→  ) {
   369→    const owner = await this.ownerService.getOwner()
   370→    const { name, mail, url } = owner
   371→    const model: CommentDto = {
   372→      author: name,
   373→      ...body,
   374→      mail,
   375→      url,
   376→      state: CommentState.Read,
   377→    } as CommentDto
   378→    // @ts-ignore
   379→    return await this.replyByCid(params, model, undefined, true, ipLocation)
   380→  }
   381→
   382→  @Patch('/:id')
   383→  @Auth()
   384→  async modifyCommentState(
   385→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
   386→    @Body() body: CommentStatePatchDto,
   387→  ) {
   388→    const { id } = params
   389→    const { state, pin } = body
   390→
   391→    const updateResult: Record<string, any> = {}
   392→
   393→    if (!isUndefined(state)) updateResult.state = state
   394→    if (!isUndefined(pin)) updateResult.pin = pin
   395→
   396→    if (pin) {
   397→      const currentRefModel = await this.commentService.model
   398→        .findOne({
   399→          _id: id,
   400→        })
   401→        .lean()
   402→        .populate('ref')
   403→
   404→      const refId = (currentRefModel?.ref as any)?._id
   405→      if (refId) {
   406→        await this.commentService.model.updateMany(
   407→          {
   408→            ref: refId,
   409→          },
   410→          {
   411→            pin: false,
   412→          },
   413→        )
   414→      }
   415→    }
   416→
   417→    try {
   418→      await this.commentService.model.updateOne(
   419→        {
   420→          _id: id,
   421→        },
   422→        updateResult,
   423→      )
   424→
   425→      return
   426→    } catch {
   427→      throw new NoContentCanBeModifiedException()
   428→    }
   429→  }
   430→
   431→  @Delete('/:id')
   432→  @Auth()
   433→  async deleteComment(@Param() params: MongoIdDto) {
   434→    const { id } = params
   435→    await this.commentService.deleteComments(id)
   436→    await this.eventManager.emit(
   437→      BusinessEvents.COMMENT_DELETE,
   438→      { id },
   439→      {
   440→        scope: EventScope.TO_SYSTEM_VISITOR,
   441→        nextTick: true,
   442→      },
   443→    )
   444→    return
   445→  }
   446→
   447→  @Patch('/batch/state')
   448→  @Auth()
   449→  async batchUpdateState(@Body() body: BatchCommentStateDto) {
   450→    const { ids, all, state, currentState } = body
   451→
   452→    if (all) {
   453→      const filter: Record<string, any> = {}
   454→      if (!isUndefined(currentState)) {
   455→        filter.state = currentState
   456→      }
   457→      await this.commentService.model.updateMany(filter, { state })
   458→    } else if (ids?.length) {
   459→      await this.commentService.model.updateMany(
   460→        { _id: { $in: ids } },
   461→        { state },
   462→      )
   463→    }
   464→
   465→    return
   466→  }
   467→
   468→  @Delete('/batch')
   469→  @Auth()
   470→  async batchDelete(@Body() body: BatchCommentDeleteDto) {
   471→    const { ids, all, state } = body
   472→
   473→    if (all) {
   474→      const filter: Record<string, any> = {}
   475→      if (!isUndefined(state)) {
   476→        filter.state = state
   477→      }
   478→      const comments = await this.commentService.model.find(filter).lean()
   479→      for (const comment of comments) {
   480→        await this.commentService.deleteComments(comment._id.toString())
   481→      }
   482→    } else if (ids?.length) {
   483→      for (const id of ids) {
   484→        await this.commentService.deleteComments(id)
   485→      }
   486→    }
   487→
   488→    return
   489→  }
   490→
   491→  @Patch('/edit/:id')
   492→  async editComment(
   493→    @Param() params: MongoIdDto,
   494→    @Body() body: EditCommentDto,
   495→    @IsAuthenticated() isAuthenticated: boolean,
   496→    @CurrentReaderId() readerId: string,
   497→  ) {
   498→    const { id } = params
   499→    const { text } = body
   500→    const comment = await this.commentService.model.findById(id).lean()
   501→    if (!comment) {
   502→      throw new CannotFindException()
   503→    }
   504→    if (comment.readerId !== readerId && !isAuthenticated) {
   505→      throw new BizException(ErrorCodeEnum.CommentForbidden)
   506→    }
   507→    await this.commentService.editComment(id, text)
   508→  }
   509→}
   510→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import { createZodDto } from 'nestjs-zod'

     3→
     4→import { CollectionRefTypes } from '~/constants/db.constant'
     5→import { normalizeRefType } from '~/utils/database.util'
     6→
     7→import { CommentAnchorMode } from './comment.model'
     8→
     9→const BlockCommentAnchorSchema = z.object({
    10→  mode: z.literal(CommentAnchorMode.Block),
    11→  blockId: z.string().trim().min(1).max(128),
    12→  blockType: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
    13→  blockFingerprint: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
    14→  snapshotText: z.string().max(20000).optional(),
    15→})
    16→
    17→const RangeCommentAnchorSchema = z
    18→  .object({
    19→    mode: z.literal(CommentAnchorMode.Range),
    20→    blockId: z.string().trim().min(1).max(128),
    21→    blockType: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
    22→    blockFingerprint: z.string().trim().min(1).max(64).optional(),
    23→    snapshotText: z.string().max(20000).optional(),
    24→    quote: z.string().min(1).max(1000),
    25→    prefix: z.string().max(300).default(''),
    26→    suffix: z.string().max(300).default(''),
    27→    startOffset: z.number().int().min(0),
    28→    endOffset: z.number().int().min(0),
    29→  })
    30→  .refine((data) => data.endOffset >= data.startOffset, {
    31→    message: 'endOffset must be greater than or equal to startOffset',
    32→    path: ['endOffset'],
    33→  })
    34→
    35→export const CommentAnchorSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('mode', [
    36→  BlockCommentAnchorSchema,
    37→  RangeCommentAnchorSchema,
    38→])
    39→
    40→/**
    41→ * Comment schema for API validation
    42→ */
    43→export const CommentSchema = z.object({
    44→  author: z.string().min(1).max(20, { message: '昵称不得大于 20 个字符' }),
    45→  text: z.string().min(1).max(500, { message: '评论内容不得大于 500 个字符' }),
    46→  mail: z
    47→    .string()
    48→    .email({ message: '请更正为正确的邮箱' })
    49→    .max(50, { message: '邮箱地址不得大于 50 个字符' }),
    50→  url: z
    51→    .string()
    52→    .url({ message: '请更正为正确的网址' })
    53→    .max(50, { message: '地址不得大于 50 个字符' })
    54→    .optional(),
    55→  isWhispers: z.boolean().optional(),
    56→  source: z.string().optional(),
    57→  avatar: z
    58→    .string()
    59→    .url({ message: '头像必须是合法的 HTTPS URL 哦' })
    60→    .refine((val) => val.startsWith('https://'), {
    61→      message: '头像必须是合法的 HTTPS URL 哦',
    62→    })
    63→    .optional(),
    64→  anchor: CommentAnchorSchema.optional(),
    65→})
    66→
    67→export class CommentDto extends createZodDto(CommentSchema.omit({ anchor: true })) {}
    68→
    69→/**
    70→ * Edit comment schema
    71→ */
    72→export const EditCommentSchema = z.object({
    73→  text: z.string().min(1),
    74→})
    75→
    76→export class EditCommentDto extends createZodDto(EditCommentSchema) {}
    77→
    78→/**
    79→ * Required guest reader comment schema
    80→ */
    81→export const RequiredGuestReaderCommentSchema = CommentSchema.extend({
    82→  author: z.string().min(1).max(20, { message: '昵称不得大于 20 个字符' }),
    83→  mail: z
    84→    .string()
    85→    .email({ message: '请更正为正确的邮箱' })
    86→    .max(50, { message: '邮箱地址不得大于 50 个字符' }),
    87→})
    88→
    89→export class RequiredGuestReaderCommentDto extends createZodDto(
    90→  RequiredGuestReaderCommentSchema,
    91→) {}
    92→
    93→/**
    94→ * Text only schema
    95→ */
    96→export const TextOnlySchema = z.object({
    97→  text: z.string().min(1),
    98→  source: z.string().optional(),
    99→})
   100→
   101→export class TextOnlyDto extends createZodDto(TextOnlySchema) {}
   102→
   103→/**
   104→ * Comment ref types schema
   105→ */
   106→export const CommentRefTypesSchema = z.object({
   107→  ref: z
   108→    .preprocess((val) => {
   109→      if (!val) return undefined
   110→      return normalizeRefType(val as any)
   111→    }, z.enum(CollectionRefTypes))
   112→    .optional(),
   113→})
   114→
   115→export class CommentRefTypesDto extends createZodDto(CommentRefTypesSchema) {}

   127→})

   129→export class CommentStatePatchDto extends createZodDto(
   130→  CommentStatePatchSchema,

   163→    ids: z.array(z.string()).optional(),

   166→      .number()
   167→      .int()
   168→      .refine((val) => [0, 1, 2].includes(val))
   169→      .optional(),

   171→  .refine((data) => data.ids?.length || data.all, {

   173→  })

   175→export class BatchCommentDeleteDto extends createZodDto(

   177→) {}

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the full picture:

1. **Core Schema**: `CommentSchema` includes `anchor: CommentAnchorSchema.optional()`, but `CommentDto` extends `CommentSchema.omit({ anchor: true })`. This means the standard DTO validation **strips anchor**.

2. **Controller**: `comment()` method receives `@Body() body: CommentDto` - since CommentDto omits anchor, the anchor field will be stripped by validation.

3. **BUT**: The controller does `const model: Partial<CommentModel> = { ...body, ...ipLocation }` and the body spread might include extra fields not validated by the DTO. Actually, with NestJS + class-validator/zod, extra fields are typically stripped.
   
So the API currently has no way to pass anchor through the standard endpoint, even though the schema and service support it. I need to check if there's another endpoint that accepts anchor or if the DTO needs updating to allow it. the DTO explicitly omits the anchor field, so I need to decide how to handle this. Since the user mentioned the core API is already implemented and I'm only working on the web side, I should probably update the DTO to include anchor rather than trying to pass it through a different mechanism. the anchor IS somehow passable. Let me look more carefully...

Actually, the `createZodDto` from `nestjs-zod` might handle this differently. When using zod with NestJS, the pipe validates the body. But the raw body in NestJS includes all fields sent by the client. The `CommentDto` with omit might strip the anchor during validation, but...

Wait, I see the issue. The `CommentDto` omits anchor, so the validated body won't have anchor. But the controller spreads `body` into the model. If the body doesn't have anchor, the model won't have it either.

However, the core has `CommentAnchorSchema` defined and the `CommentSchema` includes it. The anchor omission in `CommentDto` might be intentional for now but meant to be changed.

Actually, let me reconsider the situation. The user said the core API is already implemented, so maybe I should just remove the omit from `CommentDto` to allow anchor to pass through. But I need to think about whether that's the right approach or if I should handle this on the web side instead. Looking at the code flow, it seems like the DTO modification would be the cleaner solution since the schema already supports it. Now I'm mapping out the implementation strategy. The web app uses the same static renderer as the demo, so I can leverage the pure-DOM approach without needing Lexical context—identifying blocks by their position in the `.rich-content` container, capturing text selections through the native Selection API, applying highlights via CSS Custom Highlight API, and building anchors directly from the DOM structure. I'll need to create anchor type definitions and a selection component to handle the interaction layer. Now I need to integrate the comment components into the content editor, expose the comment state through the provider, update the hook to handle anchor data, and modify the comment display to show anchor indicators. I also need to fix the core DTO to preserve anchor information. The main challenge is figuring out where to render the selection and block comment UI so it works properly with the nested component structure.

For the anchor-to-comment flow, I'll use a Jotai atom to store pending anchor data. When a user selects text or hovers over a block, the popup captures that anchor and stores it in the atom. The comment box below reads from this atom and includes the anchor when submitting, so the comment creation flow stays connected even though the UI elements are far apart in the component tree.

The simplest approach is to have the selection popup trigger anchor storage and scroll focus to the existing comment input, which then displays a badge showing what's being commented on before submission.

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the complete findings for the user.

## Complete Architecture & Data Flow Analysis

Based on thorough investigation, here's the complete understanding of how content and comments are connected:

### 1. **PostContent.tsx** - Format Decision Layer
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/PostContent.tsx` (lines 1-15)

Decides rendering based on `contentFormat`:
```typescript
if (contentFormat === 'lexical' && content) {
  return <PostLexicalRenderer />
}
return <PostMarkdownRenderer />
```
- Receives `contentFormat` and `content` props from page component
- No data access itself - delegates to renderer

### 2. **NoteContent.tsx** - Same Pattern for Notes
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/notes/[id]/NoteContent.tsx` (lines 1-15)

Identical logic for notes with markdown/lexical rendering.

### 3. **MainLexicalContent.tsx** - Wraps Lexical Renderer
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/MainLexicalContent.tsx` (lines 1-26)

- Wraps content in `div#main-content-id`
- Calls `setMainMarkdownElement()` for reading tracking
- Dispatches `RefreshToc` event on content change
- Delegates actual rendering to `<LexicalContent>`

### 4. **LexicalContent.tsx** - Lexical Renderer Core
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx` (lines 1-144)

**Key details**:
- Parses content string as `SerializedEditorState` (JSON)
- Renders via `ShiroRenderer` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`
- Handles theme, fonts, link card fetching
- Props: `content: string`, `variant?: 'note'`, `className?: string`

### 5. **CommentRoot.tsx** - Main Comment Container
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentRoot.tsx` (lines 1-40)

**Critical structure**:
```typescript
export const CommentAreaRoot: FC<CommentBaseProps & { allowComment: boolean }>
```
- Receives `refId` (content ID) and `allowComment` flag
- Returns JSX with:
  - `<CommentBoxRoot refId={refId} />` - Comment input form
  - `<Comments refId={refId} />` - Comment list
- Handles comment disabled state

### 6. **CommentRootLazy.tsx** - Error Boundary Wrapper
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentRootLazy.tsx` (lines 1-11)

Wraps `CommentAreaRoot` in `<ErrorBoundary>` for safety.

### 7. **CommentBox/providers.tsx** - State Management
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/providers.tsx` (lines 1-112)

**Three context layers**:

1. **CommentBoxContext** (lines 14-35): Form state atoms
   ```typescript
   refId: atom(''),
   text: atom(''),
   author: atomWithStorage(`comment-author`, ''),
   mail: atomWithStorage(`comment-mail`, ''),
   url: atomWithStorage(`comment-url`, ''),
   avatar: atom(''),
   source: atom(''),
   isWhisper: atomWithStorage(`comment-is-whisper`, false),
   syncToRecently: atomWithStorage(`comment-sync-to-recently`, true),
   ```

2. **CommentBoxLifeCycleContext** (lines 37-39): Post-submit callback
   ```typescript
   afterSubmit?: () => void
   ```

3. **CommentIsReplyProvider** (lines 78-95): Reply-to-comment context
   ```typescript
   isReply: boolean
   originalRefId: string
   onCompleted?: (comment: CommentModel) => void
   ```

**Critical provider pattern** (lines 41-71): `CommentBoxProvider` initializes `CommentBoxContext` with `refId` atom.

### 8. **CommentProvider.tsx** - Data Management for Comment List
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/comment/CommentProvider.tsx` (lines 1-166)

**Key context variables**:
- `CommentReaderMapContext` - maps reader IDs to user profiles
- `CommentListContext` - Jotai atom of comment map `Record<string, CommentModel>`
- `CommentMarkdownContainerRefContext` - reference to rendered markdown container

**Data flow** (lines 57-82):
1. Fetches comments via `useCommentsQuery(refId)` 
2. Flattens paginated data into flat comment map
3. Provides via `jotaiStore.set(commentAtom, commentsMap)`
4. Exposes via context for child components

### 9. **Data Flow: How refId Flows Down**

#### Post Page (`/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/page.tsx`)
- **Line 240-243**: Component receives `data.id` (post ID):
  ```typescript
  <CommentAreaRootLazy
    refId={data.id}
    allowComment={data.allowComment}
  />
  ```
- **Line 228**: Post data provided via `<CurrentPostDataProvider data={data} />`

#### Note Page (`/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/notes/[id]/page.tsx`)
- **Line 278-281**: Component receives `data.data.id` (note ID):
  ```typescript
  <CommentAreaRootLazy
    refId={data.data.id}
    allowComment={data.data.allowComment}
  />
  ```
- **Line 266**: Note data provided via `<CurrentNoteDataProvider data={data} />`

### 10. **CurrentPostDataProvider.tsx** - Post Data Context
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/post/CurrentPostDataProvider.tsx` (lines 1-37)

- Uses `jojoo/react` library's `createModelDataProvider` pattern
- Exports: `ModelDataProvider`, `useModelDataSelector`, `useSetModelData`
- Stores `PostModel & { liked, translations }`
- Available throughout post page tree

### 11. **CurrentNoteDataProvider.tsx** - Note Data Context
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/note/CurrentNoteDataProvider.tsx` (lines 1-49)

- Same pattern as post provider
- Stores `NoteWrappedWithLikedAndTranslationPayload`
- Enables runtime note data sync after login

### 12. **WrappedElementProvider.tsx** - Content Container State
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/shared/WrappedElementProvider.tsx` (lines 1-170)

**Exposes via context state**:
- `useWrappedElement()` - HTMLDivElement reference
- `useWrappedElementSize()` - `{ h: number, w: number }`
- `useWrappedElementPosition()` - `{ x: number, y: number }`
- `useIsEoFWrappedElement()` - boolean (end-of-article detect)

**Used by**: Post/Note pages wrap content in `<WrappedElementProvider eoaDetect>` (lines 161, 125).

**Purpose**: Content measurement for:
- TOC positioning
- Reading progress tracking
- Comment UI positioning (for future enhancement)

### 13. **Comment Model Structure**
From `Comment.tsx` (lines 70-79), comments contain:
```typescript
id: string
text: string
key: string           // Comment fingerprint for deduplication
location: string      // Browser location where comment was made
isWhispers: boolean   // Private comment flag
url: string           // Comment author URL
source: string        // Auth source (github, etc)
readerId: string      // Link to reader profile
avatar: string        // Author avatar
author: string        // Author name
parent: string | CommentModel // Parent comment (for replies)
children: CommentModel[]       // Reply tree
created: Date
editedAt?: Date
```

### 14. **Comment Query Structure**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/hooks.tsx` (lines 7-38)

```typescript
apiClient.comment.getByRefId(refId, { page })
```
Returns paginated `CommentModel[]` with pagination info.

Query key: `['comments', refId]` (**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/queries/keys/comment.ts`)

### 15. **Comment Rendering - Comment.tsx**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/Comment.tsx` (lines 42-245)

**Key features**:
- Renders individual comment with avatar, author, text
- Displays `location` and `key` metadata (lines 199-202)
- Handles single-link content specially (BlockLinkRenderer)
- Recursively renders `comment.children` (lines 236-241)
- Uses `CommentHolderContext` + portal for reply UI injection

### 16. **New Block ID Plugin (Recently Added)**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/BlockIdPlugin.tsx` (lines 1-62)

- Auto-generates unique `blockId` for each block
- Maintains deduplication via `blockIdState`
- Ensures all root blocks have IDs for anchoring

### 17. **Comment Anchor Utilities (Recently Added)**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/utils/comment-anchor.ts` (lines 1-246)

**Supports two anchor modes**:

1. **BlockAnchor** - Comment on entire block:
   ```typescript
   mode: 'block'
   blockId: string
   blockType: string
   blockFingerprint: string (hash of first 200 chars)
   snapshotText: string (first 300 chars)
   ```

2. **RangeAnchor** - Comment on text selection within block:
   ```typescript
   mode: 'range'
   blockId: string
   blockType: string
   blockFingerprint: string
   snapshotText: string
   quote: string (selected text)
   prefix: string (50 chars before)
   suffix: string (50 chars after)
   startOffset: number
   endOffset: number
   ```

**Functions**:
- `buildBlockAnchor(editor, blockKey?)` - Create block anchor
- `buildRangeAnchor(editor)` - Create range anchor
- `computeBlockFingerprint()` - Hash-based stability checking

---

## Summary: Content-Comment Architecture

```
POST/NOTE PAGE
  ├─ CurrentPostDataProvider/CurrentNoteDataProvider
  │   └─ data.id, data.content, data.contentFormat, data.allowComment
  │
  ├─ WrappedElementProvider (content measurement)
  │   ├─ PostContent / NoteContent (format router)
  │   │   ├─ PostLexicalRenderer / NoteLexicalRenderer
  │   │   │   └─ MainLexicalContent → LexicalContent → ShiroRenderer
  │   │   └─ PostMarkdownRenderer / NoteMarkdownRenderer
  │   │       └─ MainMarkdown
  │   │
  │   └─ useWrappedElement() - for UI positioning
  │
  └─ CommentAreaRootLazy (refId = data.id, allowComment)
      ├─ CommentBoxRoot (input form)
      │   └─ CommentBoxProvider (form state atoms + refId)
      │       └─ CommentBoxRoot renders form UI
      │
      └─ Comments (list display)
          └─ CommentProvider (fetches & manages comment data)
              └─ renders Comment tree recursively
```

**refId is the single connection point**: Content rendered in `WrappedElementProvider` → Comments scoped to same `refId` below it.

Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings:

## Comprehensive API and Comment System Investigation Results

Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, here are my findings organized by your seven investigation points:

---

### 1. **apiClient.comment Methods** 
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/node_modules/.pnpm/@mx-space+api-client@2.1.1/node_modules/@mx-space/api-client/dist/index.d.mts` (lines 1529-1620)

The API client exports these core methods:

```typescript
// Line 1530-1537: CommentDto interface (request payload)
interface CommentDto {
  author: string
  text: string
  mail: string
  url?: string
  source?: 'github' | 'google'
  avatar?: string
}

// Line 1545-1620: CommentController class
class CommentController<ResponseWrapper> implements IController {
  // Get comment by ID with children
  getById(id: string): RequestProxyResult<CommentModel & { ref: string }, ...>
  
  // Get comments by refId (post/note/page ID)
  getByRefId(refId: string, pagination?: PaginationParams): RequestProxyResult<
    PaginateResult<CommentModel & { ref: string }> & { readers: Record<string, ReaderModel> },
    ...
  >
  
  // Create a top-level comment
  comment(refId: string, data: CommentDto): RequestProxyResult<CommentModel, ...>
  
  // Reply to a comment
  reply(commentId: string, data: CommentDto): RequestProxyResult<CommentModel, ...>
}
```

The `CommentDto` currently accepts only: `author`, `text`, `mail`, `url`, `source`, `avatar`. No `anchor` field is exposed here.

---

### 2. **How the Proxy Pattern Works**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/hooks.tsx` (lines 159-166, 180-184)

The proxy pattern is used like this:

```typescript
// Owner comments use the proxy pattern (lines 159-166)
if (isLogged) {
  return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
    .reply(refId)
    .post<CommentModel>({
      data: {
        text,
        source,
      },
    })
    .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
}

// Normal comment (lines 180-184)
if (isLogged) {
  return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
    .comment(refId)
    .post<CommentModel>({
      data: { text, source },
    })
    .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
}
```

**How the proxy works**: The `IRequestHandler<ResponseWrapper>` interface (line 32 of index.d.mts) allows dynamic path building:
- `apiClient.comment.proxy` → returns `IRequestHandler`
- `.owner` → calls the handler function, returning another `IRequestHandler` for path `/comment/owner`
- `.comment(refId)` → calls the handler with `refId`, returning `IRequestHandler` for `/comment/owner/comment/{refId}`
- `.post({ data: {...} })` → sends POST with the data object

**Extra fields**: You CAN add extra fields to the data object. The data payload is not strictly typed in the proxy call—it's just passed through to `$fetch`. The server would need to accept and handle extra fields.

---

### 3. **apiClient Setup**
**Files**: 
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/lib/fetch/fetch.client.ts`
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/lib/fetch/fetch.server.ts`
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/lib/fetch/shared.ts`

**Client setup** (fetch.client.ts, lines 23-57):

```typescript
export const $fetch = createFetch({
  defaults: {
    timeout: 8000,
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: globalConfigureHeader,
    onRequest(context) {
      // Adds X-Session-Uuid header from sessionStorage or generates nanoid
      headers['X-Session-Uuid'] = globalThis?.sessionStorage?.getItem(uuidStorageKey) ?? uuid
      context.options.headers = headers
    },
  },
})
export const apiClient = createApiClient(createFetchAdapter($fetch))
```

**Adapter** (shared.ts, lines 8-50):

```typescript
export const createFetchAdapter = ($fetch: FetchType): IRequestAdapter<typeof $fetch> => ({
  default: $fetch,
  get(url, options) { return $fetch(url, { method: 'GET', query: params }) }
  post(url, options) { return $fetch(url, { method: 'post', query: params, body: data }) }
  // etc.
})

export const createApiClient = (fetchAdapter) =>
  createClient(fetchAdapter)(API_URL, {
    controllers: allControllers,
    getDataFromResponse(response) {
      return response as any
    },
  })
```

The apiClient is created from `@mx-space/api-client` version **2.1.1** (from package.json).

---

### 4. **CommentDto Extension Strategies**

**Current limitation**: The `CommentDto` interface in the API client is fixed and doesn't include `anchor`.

**Three approaches to add anchor**:

**Approach A: Cast to `any` (quickest)**
```typescript
// In CommentBox/hooks.tsx
const data = { text, source, anchor: commentAnchor } as any
apiClient.comment.proxy.owner.comment(refId).post({ data })
```

**Approach B: Custom fetch wrapper**
```typescript
// In lib/fetch/shared.ts, wrap the apiClient.comment methods
apiClient.request({
  url: `${apiClient.endpoint}/comment/owner/comment/${refId}`,
  method: 'post',
  data: { text, source, anchor: commentAnchor }
})
```

**Approach C: Extend type at application level**
```typescript
// Create types.ts
type CommentDtoWithAnchor = CommentDto & { anchor?: CommentAnchor }
```

The simplest working solution is **Approach A** (cast as any) until the mx-space server API schema is updated to accept anchor.

---

### 5. **CommentBox ActionBar.tsx**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/ActionBar.tsx`

Current structure (lines 85-133):

```typescript
export const CommentBoxActionBar: Component = ({ className }) => {
  const t = useTranslations('comment')
  const hasCommentText = useCommentBoxHasText()

  return (
    <footer className={...}>
      <span className="flex-1 ...">
        <span className="hidden md:inline">
          {t('support_markdown')}
          <b>Markdown</b>
          {t('and')}{' '}
          <MarkdownLink
            noIcon
            href="https://docs.github.com/zh/..."
          >
            GFM
          </MarkdownLink>
        </span>
        <CommentBoxSlotProvider />
      </span>
      <AnimatePresence>
        {hasCommentText && (
          <m.aside key="send-button-wrapper" ...>
            <TextLengthIndicator />
            <WhisperCheckbox />
            <SyncToRecentlyCheckbox />
            <SubmitButton />
          </m.aside>
        )}
      </AnimatePresence>
    </footer>
  )
}
```

**To add anchor badge**: Insert between `TextLengthIndicator` and `WhisperCheckbox`:

```typescript
{commentAnchor && (
  <m.span className="text-xs px-2 py-1 rounded bg-blue-100 dark:bg-blue-900">
    📍 Anchored to block
  </m.span>
)}
```

---

### 6. **CommentBox AuthedInput.tsx**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/AuthedInput.tsx`

Current component (lines 15-75):

```typescript
export const CommentBoxAuthedInput: Component<{ autoFocus?: boolean }> = ({
  autoFocus,
}) => {
  const setter = useSetCommentBoxValues()
  const reader = useSessionReader()
  const displayName = reader ? reader.name || 'Anonymous' : ''

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!reader) return
    setter('author', displayName)
    setter('avatar', reader.image)
    setter('mail', reader.email)
    reader.handle && setter('url', getUserUrl({...}))
    setter('source', reader.provider)
  }, [displayName, reader, setter])

  return (
    <div className="flex space-x-4">
      <div className="...">
        <Avatar.Root>
          <Avatar.Image ... />
          <Avatar.Fallback ... />
        </Avatar.Root>
        <UserAuthFromIcon className="..." />
      </div>
      <div className="relative h-[150px] w-full ...">
        <UniversalTextArea className="pb-5" autoFocus={autoFocus} />
      </div>
      <CommentBoxActionBar className="..." />
    </div>
  )
}
```

Uses `useSetCommentBoxValues()` hook to populate the comment state atom. This is where you'd add logic to capture and store `anchor` data if needed.

---

### 7. **Accessing Serialized Editor State**
**Key files**:
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx` (lines 83-94)
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/post/CurrentPostDataProvider.tsx`
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/PostLexicalRenderer.tsx`

**Access pattern**:

```typescript
// In LexicalContent.tsx (lines 88-94)
const editorState = useMemo<SerializedEditorState | null>(() => {
  try {
    return JSON.parse(content)  // content is a JSON string
  } catch {
    return null
  }
}, [content])

// In PostLexicalRenderer.tsx (lines 8-11)
export const PostLexicalRenderer = () => {
  const content = useCurrentPostDataSelector((data) => data?.content)
  if (!content) return null
  return <MainLexicalContent content={content} className="..." />
}

// Content comes from CurrentPostDataProvider via:
// import { useCurrentPostDataSelector } from '~/providers/post/CurrentPostDataProvider'
// which wraps the TextBaseModelLexical.content field
```

**SerializedEditorState structure**: The `content` field from the API is a JSON string representing Lexical's `SerializedEditorState`, which includes:

```typescript
{
  root: {
    children: [
      // Each child is a block with:
      {
        type: 'paragraph' | 'heading' | 'codeblock' | 'alert' | etc.,
        version: number,
        children: [ text nodes or nested blocks ],
        // Custom state stored via blockIdState:
        __blockId?: string  // Added by BlockIdPlugin
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Block ID extraction**: To get blockIds, you need access to a Lexical editor instance:

```typescript
// From comment-anchor.ts (lines 147-175)
export function buildBlockAnchor(editor: LexicalEditor, blockKey?: string): AnchorResult<BlockAnchor> {
  return editor.read(() => {
    if (blockKey) {
      const node = $getRoot().getChildren().find((c) => c.getKey() === blockKey)
      // Get blockId via $getState(node, blockIdState)
    }
    // ...
  })
}
```

**At comment time**: When the reader clicks to comment, you'd need:
1. A reference to the `RichRenderer`'s editor instance (not available by default—it's read-only)
2. Or: Pass the serialized state + parse it to extract blockIds manually

**Simpler approach**: Store blockIds in a hidden DOM attribute or data structure when rendering, then read them when commenting.

| **Block IDs** | BlockIdPlugin.tsx + comment-anchor.ts | Added by plugin; stored in node's blockIdState; extracted via `buildBlockAnchor()` function |

 `./apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/providers.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import type { CommentModel } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     4→import { atom } from 'jotai'
     5→import { atomWithStorage } from 'jotai/utils'
     6→import type { FC, PropsWithChildren } from 'react'
     7→import { createContext, memo, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react'
     8→
     9→import { useBeforeMounted } from '~/hooks/common/use-before-mounted'
    10→import { jotaiStore } from '~/lib/store'
    11→
    12→import { setCommentActionLeftSlot, useCommentActionLeftSlot } from './hooks'
    13→
    14→export const commentStoragePrefix = 'comment-'
    15→export const createInitialValue = () => ({
    16→  refId: atom(''),
    17→
    18→  text: atom(''),
    19→  author: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}author`, ''),
    20→  mail: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}mail`, ''),
    21→  url: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}url`, ''),
    22→
    23→  avatar: atom(''),
    24→  source: atom(''),
    25→
    26→  // settings
    27→  isWhisper: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}is-whisper`, false),
    28→  syncToRecently: atomWithStorage(
    29→    `${commentStoragePrefix}sync-to-recently`,
    30→    true,
    31→  ),
    32→})
    33→export const CommentBoxContext = createContext<
    34→  ReturnType<typeof createInitialValue>
    35→>(null!)
    36→
    37→export const CommentBoxLifeCycleContext = createContext<{
    38→  afterSubmit?: () => void
    39→}>(null!)
    40→
    41→export const CommentBoxProvider = (
    42→  props: PropsWithChildren & {
    43→    refId: string
    44→    afterSubmit?: () => void
    45→    initialValue?: string
    46→  },
    47→) => {
    48→  const { refId, children, afterSubmit, initialValue } = props
    49→
    50→  const ctxValue = useMemo(
    51→    () => ({
    52→      ...createInitialValue(),
    53→      refId: atom(refId),
    54→    }),
    55→    [refId],
    56→  )
    57→  useBeforeMounted(() => {
    58→    if (initialValue) {
    59→      jotaiStore.set(ctxValue.text, initialValue)
    60→    }
    61→  })
    62→  return (
    63→    <CommentBoxContext key={refId} value={ctxValue}>
    64→      <CommentBoxLifeCycleContext
    65→        value={useMemo(() => ({ afterSubmit }), [afterSubmit])}
    66→      >
    67→        {children}
    68→      </CommentBoxLifeCycleContext>
    69→    </CommentBoxContext>
    70→  )
    71→}
    72→
    73→export const CommentIsReplyContext = createContext(false)
    74→export const CommentOriginalRefIdContext = createContext('')
    75→export const CommentCompletedCallbackContext = createContext<
    76→  null | ((comment: CommentModel) => void)
    77→>(null)
    78→export const CommentIsReplyProvider = (
    79→  props: PropsWithChildren<{
    80→    isReply: boolean
    81→    originalRefId: string
    82→    onCompleted?: (comment: CommentModel) => void
    83→  }>,
    84→) => {
    85→  const { isReply, originalRefId, onCompleted, children } = props
    86→  return (
    87→    <CommentOriginalRefIdContext value={originalRefId}>
    88→      <CommentIsReplyContext value={isReply}>
    89→        <CommentCompletedCallbackContext value={onCompleted || null}>
    90→          {children}
    91→        </CommentCompletedCallbackContext>
    92→      </CommentIsReplyContext>
    93→    </CommentOriginalRefIdContext>
    94→  )
    95→}
    96→
    97→export const CommentBoxSlotPortal = memo((props: PropsWithChildren) => {
    98→  const { children } = props
    99→  useEffect(() => {
   100→    setCommentActionLeftSlot(children)
   101→    return () => {
   102→      setCommentActionLeftSlot(null)
   103→    }
   104→  }, [children])
   105→  return null
   106→})
   107→
   108→export const CommentBoxSlotProvider: FC = memo(() => useCommentActionLeftSlot())
   109→
   110→CommentBoxSlotProvider.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotProvider'
   111→CommentBoxSlotPortal.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotPortal'
   112→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-27 14:31:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentProvider.tsx`

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  CommentModel,
     3→  PaginateResult,
     4→  ReaderModel,
     5→} from '@mx-space/api-client'
     6→import type { InfiniteData } from '@tanstack/react-query'
     7→import { createContextState } from 'foxact/create-context-state'
     8→import type { PrimitiveAtom } from 'jotai'
     9→import { atom, useAtomValue } from 'jotai'
    10→import { selectAtom } from 'jotai/utils'
    11→import { useTranslations } from 'next-intl'
    12→import type { FC, ReactNode } from 'react'
    13→import {
    14→  createContext as createReactContext,
    15→  use,
    16→  useCallback,
    17→  useEffect,
    18→  useMemo,
    19→} from 'react'
    20→import { createContext, useContextSelector } from 'use-context-selector'
    21→
    22→import { NotSupport } from '~/components/common/NotSupport'
    23→import { useRefValue } from '~/hooks/common/use-ref-value'
    24→import { jotaiStore } from '~/lib/store'
    25→
    26→import { LoadMoreIndicator } from '../shared/LoadMoreIndicator'
    27→import { CommentSkeleton } from './CommentSkeleton'
    28→import { useCommentsQuery } from './hooks'
    29→
    30→const CommentReaderMapContext = createContext<Record<string, ReaderModel>>({})
    31→const CommentListContext = createReactContext<
    32→  PrimitiveAtom<Record<string, CommentModel & { new?: boolean }>>
    33→>(null!)
    34→
    35→export const [
    36→  CommentMarkdownContainerRefContext,
    37→  useCommentMarkdownContainerRef,
    38→  useCommentMarkdownContainerRefSetter,
    39→] = createContextState<HTMLDivElement | null>(null)
    40→
    41→export const CommentProvider: FC<{
    42→  refId: string
    43→  children: (
    44→    data: InfiniteData<
    45→      PaginateResult<
    46→        CommentModel & {
    47→          ref: string
    48→        }
    49→      > & {
    50→        readers: Record<string, ReaderModel>
    51→      }
    52→    >,
    53→    commentAtom: PrimitiveAtom<
    54→      Record<string, CommentModel & { new?: boolean }>
    55→    >,
    56→  ) => ReactNode
    57→}> = ({ children, refId }) => {
    58→  const t = useTranslations('comment')
    59→  const commentAtom = useRefValue(() =>
    60→    atom({} as Record<string, CommentModel & { new?: boolean }>),
    61→  )
    62→
    63→  const { data, isLoading, fetchNextPage, hasNextPage } =
    64→    useCommentsQuery(refId)
    65→
    66→  useEffect(() => {
    67→    if (!data) return
    68→    const commentsMap = Object.assign({}, jotaiStore.get(commentAtom))
    69→    function dts(comments: CommentModel[]) {
    70→      for (const comment of comments) {
    71→        commentsMap[comment.id] = comment
    72→
    73→        if (comment.children) {
    74→          dts(comment.children)
    75→        }
    76→      }
    77→    }
    78→
    79→    dts(data.pages.flatMap((page) => page.data))
    80→
    81→    jotaiStore.set(commentAtom, commentsMap)
    82→  }, [commentAtom, data])
    83→
    84→  const readers = useMemo(() => {
    85→    if (!data) return {}
    86→    return data?.pages.reduce(
    87→      (acc, curr) => ({ ...acc, ...curr.readers }),
    88→      {} as Record<string, ReaderModel>,
    89→    )
    90→  }, [data])
    91→
    92→  if (isLoading) {
    93→    return <CommentSkeleton />
    94→  }
    95→  if (!data || data.pages.length === 0 || data.pages[0].data.length === 0)
    96→    return (
    97→      <div className="center flex min-h-[400px]">
    98→        <NotSupport text={t('no_comments_yet')} />
    99→      </div>
   100→    )
   101→
   102→  return (
   103→    <CommentReaderMapContext.Provider value={readers}>
   104→      <CommentListContext value={commentAtom}>
   105→        {children(data, commentAtom)}
   106→
   107→        {hasNextPage && (
   108→          <LoadMoreIndicator onLoading={fetchNextPage}>
   109→            <CommentSkeleton />
   110→          </LoadMoreIndicator>
   111→        )}
   112→      </CommentListContext>
   113→    </CommentReaderMapContext.Provider>
   114→  )
   115→}
   116→
   117→export const useCommentReader = (readerId?: string) => {
   118→  return useContextSelector(CommentReaderMapContext, (v) =>
   119→    readerId ? v[readerId] : undefined,
   120→  )
   121→}
   122→
   123→export const useCommentByIdSelector = <T,>(
   124→  commentId: string,
   125→  selector: (comment?: CommentModel) => T,
   126→): T => {
   127→  const commentsAtom = use(CommentListContext)
   128→  return useAtomValue(
   129→    useMemo(
   130→      () => selectAtom(commentsAtom, (v) => selector(v[commentId])),
   131→      [commentsAtom, selector, commentId],
   132→    ),
   133→  )
   134→}
   135→
   136→export const useCommentById = (commentId: string) => {
   137→  const commentsAtom = use(CommentListContext)
   138→  return useAtomValue(
   139→    useMemo(
   140→      () => selectAtom(commentsAtom, (v) => v[commentId]),
   141→      [commentsAtom, commentId],
   142→    ),
   143→  )
   144→}
   145→
   146→export const useUpdateComment = () => {
   147→  const commentsAtom = use(CommentListContext)
   148→  return useCallback(
   149→    (comment: Partial<CommentModel> & { id: string }) => {
   150→      jotaiStore.set(commentsAtom, (prev) => {
   151→        const newComments = {
   152→          ...prev,
   153→          [comment.id]: {
   154→            ...prev[comment.id],
   155→            ...comment,
   156→            editedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   157→          },
   158→        }
   159→
   160→        return newComments
   161→      })
   162→    },
   163→    [commentsAtom],
   164→  )
   165→}
   166→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→{

     5→  "type": "module",

    22→    },

    24→      "require": "./dist/*.cjs",
    25→      "import": "./dist/*.mjs"
    26→    },
    27→    "./dist/adaptors/*": {
    28→      "require": "./dist/adaptors/*.cjs",
    29→      "import": "./dist/adaptors/*.mjs"
    30→    },
    31→    "./package.json": "./package.json"
    32→  },
    33→  "bump": {
    34→    "before": [
    35→      "git pull --rebase",
    36→      "pnpm i",
    37→      "npm run package"
    38→    ],
    39→    "after": [
    40→      "npm publish --access=public"
    41→    ],
    42→    "tag": false,
    43→    "commit_message": "chore(release): bump @mx-space/api-client to v${NEW_VERSION}"
    44→  },
    45→  "scripts": {
    46→    "package": "rm -rf dist && tsdown",
    47→    "build": "npm run package",
    48→    "prepackage": "rm -rf dist",
    49→    "test": "vitest",
    50→    "dev": "vitest"
    51→  },
