```text
    24→
    25→import { MAX_COMMENT_TEXT_LENGTH } from './constants'
    26→import type { createInitialValue } from './providers'
    27→import {
    28→  CommentBoxContext,
    29→  CommentBoxLifeCycleContext,
    30→  CommentCompletedCallbackContext,
    31→  CommentIsReplyContext,
    32→  CommentOriginalRefIdContext,
    33→} from './providers'
    34→
    35→export const useUseCommentReply = () => use(CommentIsReplyContext)
    36→
    37→export const useCommentOriginalRefId = () => {
    38→  const fallbackRefId = useAtomValue(use(CommentBoxContext).refId)
    39→  return use(CommentOriginalRefIdContext) || fallbackRefId
    40→}
    41→
    42→export const useCommentCompletedCallback = () =>
    43→  use(CommentCompletedCallbackContext)
    44→
    45→export const useCommentBoxTextValue = () =>
    46→  useAtomValue(use(CommentBoxContext).text)
    47→
    48→export const useCommentBoxRefIdValue = () =>
    49→  useAtomValue(use(CommentBoxContext).refId)
    50→
    51→export const useGetCommentBoxAtomValues = () => use(CommentBoxContext)
    52→export const useCommentBoxLifeCycle = () => use(CommentBoxLifeCycleContext)
    53→
    54→// ReactNode 导致 tsx 无法推断，过于复杂
    55→const commentActionLeftSlotAtom = atom(null as PropsWithChildren['children'])
    56→export const useCommentActionLeftSlot = () =>
    57→  useAtomValue(commentActionLeftSlotAtom)
    58→
    59→export const setCommentActionLeftSlot = (slot: PropsWithChildren['children']) =>
    60→  jotaiStore.set(commentActionLeftSlotAtom, slot)
    61→
    62→export const useCommentBoxHasText = () =>
    63→  useAtomValue(
    64→    selectAtom(
    65→      use(CommentBoxContext).text,
    66→      useCallback((v) => v.length > 0, []),
    67→    ),
    68→  )
    69→
    70→export const useCommentBoxTextIsOversize = () =>
    71→  useAtomValue(
    72→    selectAtom(
    73→      use(CommentBoxContext).text,
    74→      useCallback((v) => v.length > MAX_COMMENT_TEXT_LENGTH, []),
    75→    ),
    76→  )
    77→type CommentContextValue = ReturnType<typeof createInitialValue>
    78→
    79→export const useSetCommentBoxValues = <
    80→  T extends keyof CommentContextValue,
    81→>() => {
    82→  const ctx = use(CommentBoxContext)
    83→  return useCallback(
    84→    (key: T, value: ExtractAtomValue<CommentContextValue[T]>) => {
    85→      const atom = ctx[key]
    86→      if (!atom) throw new Error(`atom ${key} not found`)
    87→      jotaiStore.set(atom as any, value)
    88→    },
    89→    [ctx],
    90→  )
    91→}
    92→
    93→// Comment Mode
    94→
    95→export const enum CommentBoxMode {
    96→  'legacy',
    97→  'with-auth',
    98→}
    99→
   100→const commentModeAtom = atomWithStorage(
   101→  'comment-mode',
   102→  CommentBoxMode['with-auth'],
   103→)
   104→
   105→export const useCommentMode = () => useAtomValue(commentModeAtom)
   106→export const setCommentMode = (mode: CommentBoxMode) =>
   107→  jotaiStore.set(commentModeAtom, mode)
   108→
   109→export const useSendComment = () => {
   110→  const commentRefId = useCommentBoxRefIdValue()
   111→  const {
   112→    text: textAtom,
   113→    author: authorAtom,
   114→    mail: mailAtom,
   115→    url: urlAtom,
   116→
   117→    source: sourceAtom,
   118→    avatar: avatarAtom,
   119→
   120→    isWhisper: isWhisperAtom,
   121→    syncToRecently: syncToRecentlyAtom,
   122→  } = useGetCommentBoxAtomValues()
   123→  const { afterSubmit } = useCommentBoxLifeCycle()
   124→  const isLogged = useIsOwnerLogged()
   125→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
   126→  const isReply = useUseCommentReply()
   127→  const originalRefId = useCommentOriginalRefId()
   128→  const completedCallback = useCommentCompletedCallback()
   129→
   130→  const wrappedCompletedCallback = <T extends CommentModel>(data: T): T => {
   131→    completedCallback?.(data)
   132→    return data
   133→  }
   134→
   135→  const { mutate, isPending } = useMutation({
   136→    mutationFn: async (refId: string) => {
   137→      const text = jotaiStore.get(textAtom)
   138→      const author = jotaiStore.get(authorAtom)
   139→      const mail = jotaiStore.get(mailAtom)
   140→      const avatar = jotaiStore.get(avatarAtom)
   141→      const source = jotaiStore.get(sourceAtom) as any
   142→      const url = jotaiStore.get(urlAtom)
   143→
   144→      const commentDto: CommentDto = { text, author, mail, avatar, source, url }
   145→
   146→      if (isLogged) {
   147→        delete commentDto.avatar
   148→      }
   149→
   150→      // Omit empty string key
   151→      Object.keys(commentDto).forEach((key) => {
   152→        // @ts-expect-error
   153→        if (commentDto[key] === '') delete commentDto[key]
   154→      })
   155→
   156→      // Reply Comment
   157→      if (isReply) {
   158→        if (isLogged) {
   159→          return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
   160→            .reply(refId)
   161→            .post<CommentModel>({
   162→              data: {
   163→                text,
   164→                source,
   165→              },
   166→            })
   167→            .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   168→        } else {
   169→          return apiClient.comment
   170→            .reply(refId, commentDto)
   171→            .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   172→        }
   173→      }
   174→
   175→      // Normal Comment
   176→      const isWhisper = jotaiStore.get(isWhisperAtom)
   177→      const syncToRecently = jotaiStore.get(syncToRecentlyAtom)
   178→
   179→      if (isLogged) {
   180→        return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
   181→          .comment(refId)
   182→          .post<CommentModel>({
   183→            data: { text, source },
   184→          })
   185→          .then(async (res) => {
   186→            if (syncToRecently)
   187→              apiClient.recently.proxy
   188→                .post({
   189→                  data: {
   190→                    content: text,
   191→                    ref: refId,
   192→                  },
   193→                })
   194→                .then(() => {
   195→                  toast.success('已同步到碎碎念')
   196→                })
   197→
   198→            return res
   199→          })
   200→          .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   201→      }
   202→      // @ts-ignore
   203→      commentDto.isWhispers = isWhisper
   204→      return apiClient.comment
   205→        .comment(refId, commentDto)
   206→        .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
   207→    },
   208→    mutationKey: [commentRefId, 'comment'],
   209→    onError(error: RequestError) {
   210→      toast.error(getErrorMessageFromRequestError(error))
   211→    },
   212→    onSuccess(data) {
   213→      afterSubmit?.()
   214→
   215→      const toastCopy = isLogged
   216→        ? '发表成功啦~'
   217→        : isReply
   218→          ? '感谢你的回复！'
   219→          : '感谢你的评论！'
   220→
   221→      const commentListQueryKey = buildCommentsQueryKey(originalRefId)
   222→
   223→      toast.success(toastCopy)
   224→      jotaiStore.set(textAtom, '')
   225→
   226→      queryClient.setQueryData<
   227→        InfiniteData<
   228→          PaginateResult<
   229→            CommentModel & {
   230→              ref: string
   231→            }
   232→          >
   233→        >
   234→      >(commentListQueryKey, (oldData) => {
   235→        if (!oldData) return oldData
   236→        if (isReply) {
   237→          // find the reply refed comment
   238→
   239→          return produce(oldData, (draft) => {
   240→            const dfs = (
   241→              data: CommentModel,
   242→              commentRefId: string,
   243→              newData: CommentModel & { new?: boolean },
   244→            ) => {
   245→              if (data.id === commentRefId) {
   246→                if (!data.children) {
   247→                  data.children = []
   248→                }
   249→                ;(data.children as (CommentModel & { new: boolean })[]).push({
   250→                  ...newData,
   251→                  new: true,
   252→                })
   253→                return true
   254→              }
   255→              if (!data.children) {
   256→                return
   257→              }
   258→              for (const child of data.children) {
   259→                if (dfs(child, commentRefId, newData)) {
   260→                  return true
   261→                }
   262→              }
   263→              return false
   264→            }
   265→
   266→            const dataToAdd = {
   267→              ...data,
   268→              new: true,
   269→            }
   270→
   271→            for (const page of draft.pages) {
   272→              for (const item of page.data) {
   273→                if (dfs(item, commentRefId, dataToAdd)) {
   274→                  break
   275→                }
   276→              }
   277→            }
   278→          })
   279→        }
   280→
   281→        return produce(oldData, (draft) => {
   282→          draft.pages[0].data.unshift({
   283→            ...data,
   284→            // @ts-ignore
   285→            new: true,
   286→          })
   287→        })
   288→      })
   289→    },
   290→  })
   291→
   292→  return [
   293→    useCallback(() => mutate(commentRefId), [commentRefId, mutate]),
   294→    isPending,
   295→  ] as const
   296→}
   297→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import type { CommentModel, PaginateResult } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     2→import { CommentState } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     3→import type { InfiniteData, MutationOptions } from '@tanstack/react-query'
     4→import { useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
     5→import { produce } from 'immer'
     6→import { useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation'
     7→
     8→import { apiClient } from '~/lib/request'
     9→import { toast } from '~/lib/toast'
    10→
    11→import { commentAdmin } from '../definition/comment'
    12→
    13→const useGetCurrentCommentStateFromQuery = () => {
    14→  const search = useSearchParams()
    15→  const state = search.get('tab') as any as CommentState
    16→  return state || CommentState.Unread
    17→}
    18→export const useUpdateCommentStateMutation = (
    19→  options?: MutationOptions<any>,
    20→) => {
    21→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    22→
    23→  const state = useGetCurrentCommentStateFromQuery()
    24→  return useMutation({
    25→    mutationKey: ['comment', 'updateState'],
    26→    mutationFn: async ({ id, state }: { id: string; state: CommentState }) =>
    27→      apiClient.proxy.comments(id).patch({ data: { state } }),
    28→    onMutate: async ({ id }) => {
    29→      queryClient.setQueryData<InfiniteData<PaginateResult<CommentModel>>>(
    30→        commentAdmin.byState(state).queryKey,
    31→        produce((draft) => {
    32→          draft?.pages.forEach((page) => {
    33→            page.data = page.data.filter((comment) => comment.id !== id)
    34→          })
    35→        }),
    36→      )
    37→    },
    38→    onSuccess: (...rest) => {
    39→      options?.onSuccess?.apply(null, rest as any)
    40→      toast.success('操作成功')
    41→    },
    42→    onError: () => {
    43→      toast.error('操作失败')
    44→    },
    45→  })
    46→}
    47→
    48→export const useDeleteCommentMutation = (options?: MutationOptions<any>) => {
    49→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    50→  const state = useGetCurrentCommentStateFromQuery()
    51→
    52→  return useMutation({
    53→    mutationKey: ['comment', 'delete'],
    54→    mutationFn: async ({ id }: { id: string }) =>
    55→      apiClient.proxy.comments(id).delete(),
    56→    onMutate: async ({ id }) => {
    57→      queryClient.setQueryData<InfiniteData<PaginateResult<CommentModel>>>(
    58→        commentAdmin.byState(state).queryKey,
    59→        produce((draft) => {
    60→          draft?.pages.forEach((page) => {
    61→            page.data = page.data.filter((comment) => comment.id !== id)
    62→          })
    63→        }),
    64→      )
    65→    },
    66→    onSuccess: (...rest) => {
    67→      toast.success('删除成功')
    68→      options?.onSuccess?.apply(null, rest as any)
    69→    },
    70→    onError: () => {
    71→      toast.error('删除失败')
    72→    },
    73→  })
    74→}
    75→
    76→export const useReplyCommentMutation = () => {
    77→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    78→  const state = useGetCurrentCommentStateFromQuery()
    79→
    80→  return useMutation({
    81→    mutationKey: ['comment', 'reply'],
    82→    mutationFn: async ({ id, content }: { id: string; content: string }) =>
    83→      apiClient.proxy.comments.owner.reply(id).post({
    84→        data: {
    85→          text: content,
    86→        },
    87→      }),
    88→    onMutate({ id }) {
    89→      queryClient.setQueryData<InfiniteData<PaginateResult<CommentModel>>>(
    90→        commentAdmin.byState(state).queryKey,
    91→        produce((draft) => {
    92→          draft?.pages.forEach((page) => {
    93→            page.data = page.data.filter((comment) => comment.id !== id)
    94→          })
    95→        }),
    96→      )
    97→    },
    98→    onSuccess: (_) => {
    99→      toast.success('回复成功')
   100→    },
   101→    onError: () => {
   102→      queryClient.invalidateQueries({
   103→        queryKey: commentAdmin.byState(state).queryKey,
   104→      })
   105→
   106→      toast.error('回复失败')
   107→    },
   108→  })
   109→}
   110→
```

```text
    33→        getUserUrl({

    35→          handle: reader.handle,
    36→        }) || '',
    37→      )

    39→  }, [displayName, reader, setter])
    40→

    42→

    46→        className={clsx(
    47→          'relative mb-2 shrink-0 select-none self-end rounded-full',

    49→          'backface-hidden ml-[2px]',
    50→        )}
    51→      >
    52→        <Avatar.Root>
    53→          <Avatar.Image
    54→            className="rounded-full object-cover"
    55→            src={reader.image}
    56→            alt={`${displayName}'s avatar`}
    57→            width={48}
    58→            height={48}
    59→          />
    60→          <Avatar.Fallback
    61→            delayMs={600}
    62→            className="block size-[48px] shrink-0 rounded-full"
    63→          />
    64→        </Avatar.Root>
    65→
    66→        <UserAuthFromIcon className="absolute -bottom-1 right-0" />
    67→      </div>
    68→      <div className="relative h-[150px] w-full rounded-xl bg-zinc-200/50 dark:bg-zinc-800/50">
    69→        <UniversalTextArea className="pb-5" autoFocus={autoFocus} />
    70→      </div>
    71→
    72→      <CommentBoxActionBar className="absolute bottom-0 left-14 right-0 mb-2 ml-4 w-auto px-4" />
    73→    </div>
    74→  )
    75→}
    76→
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-27 14:32:20Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/BlockIdPlugin.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
     2→import {
     3→  $addUpdateTag,
     4→  $getRoot,
     5→  $getState,
     6→  $setState,
     7→  createState,
     8→} from 'lexical'
     9→import { useEffect } from 'react'
    10→
    11→export const blockIdState = createState('blockId', {
    12→  parse: (v: unknown) => (typeof v === 'string' ? v : ''),
    13→})
    14→
    15→const NORMALIZATION_TAG = 'block-id-normalization'
    16→
    17→export function BlockIdPlugin() {
    18→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    19→
    20→  useEffect(() => {
    21→    return editor.registerUpdateListener(({ tags }) => {
    22→      if (tags.has(NORMALIZATION_TAG)) return
    23→
    24→      editor.read(() => {
    25→        const children = $getRoot().getChildren()
    26→        let needsUpdate = false
    27→        const seen = new Set<string>()
    28→
    29→        for (const child of children) {
    30→          const id = $getState(child, blockIdState)
    31→          if (id === '' || seen.has(id)) {
    32→            needsUpdate = true
    33→            break
    34→          }
    35→          seen.add(id)
    36→        }
    37→
    38→        if (!needsUpdate) return
    39→
    40→        editor.update(
    41→          () => {
    42→            $addUpdateTag('history-merge')
    43→            const children = $getRoot().getChildren()
    44→            const seen = new Set<string>()
    45→
    46→            for (const child of children) {
    47→              let id = $getState(child, blockIdState)
    48→              if (id === '' || seen.has(id)) {
    49→                id = crypto.randomUUID()
    50→                $setState(child, blockIdState, id)
    51→              }
    52→              seen.add(id)
    53→            }
    54→          },
    55→          { tag: NORMALIZATION_TAG },
    56→        )
    57→      })
    58→    })
    59→  }, [editor])
    60→
    61→  return null
    62→}
    63→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-02-27 14:32:20Z)**_

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  ElementNode,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  RangeSelection,
     6→} from 'lexical'
     7→import {
     8→  $getRoot,
     9→  $getSelection,
    10→  $getState,
    11→  $isElementNode,
    12→  $isLineBreakNode,
    13→  $isRangeSelection,
    14→  $isTextNode,
    15→} from 'lexical'
    16→
    17→import { blockIdState } from '../plugins/BlockIdPlugin'
    18→
    19→export interface BlockAnchor {
    20→  mode: 'block'
    21→  blockId: string
    22→  blockType: string
    23→  blockFingerprint: string
    24→  snapshotText: string
    25→}
    26→
    27→export interface RangeAnchor {
    28→  mode: 'range'
    29→  blockId: string
    30→  blockType: string
    31→  blockFingerprint: string
    32→  snapshotText: string
    33→  quote: string
    34→  prefix: string
    35→  suffix: string
    36→  startOffset: number
    37→  endOffset: number
    38→}
    39→
    40→export type CommentAnchor = BlockAnchor | RangeAnchor
    41→
    42→export type AnchorError =
    43→  | 'no-selection'
    44→  | 'cross-block'
    45→  | 'collapsed'
    46→  | 'no-block-id'
    47→  | 'not-root-block'
    48→
    49→export type AnchorResult<T> =
    50→  | { ok: true; anchor: T }
    51→  | { ok: false; error: AnchorError }
    52→
    53→function computeBlockFingerprint(block: ElementNode): string {
    54→  const text = block.getTextContent()
    55→  const input = text.slice(0, 200) + String(text.length)
    56→  let hash = 5381
    57→  for (let i = 0; i < input.length; i++) {
    58→    hash = ((hash << 5) + hash + (input.codePointAt(i) ?? 0)) | 0
    59→  }
    60→  return (hash >>> 0).toString(16)
    61→}
    62→
    63→function $getRootBlock(node: LexicalNode): ElementNode | null {
    64→  const root = $getRoot()
    65→  let current: LexicalNode | null = node
    66→  while (current) {
    67→    const parent: ElementNode | null = current.getParent()
    68→    if (parent === root && 'getChildren' in current) {
    69→      return current as ElementNode
    70→    }
    71→    current = parent
    72→  }
    73→  return null
    74→}
    75→
    76→function $resolveSelectionPoint(
    77→  selection: RangeSelection,
    78→  which: 'anchor' | 'focus',
    79→): { node: LexicalNode; offset: number } {
    80→  const point = selection[which]
    81→  const node = point.getNode()
    82→
    83→  if ($isElementNode(node)) {
    84→    const children = node.getChildren()
    85→    if (point.offset < children.length) {
    86→      return { node: children[point.offset], offset: 0 }
    87→    }
    88→    const last = children.at(-1)
    89→    if (last && $isTextNode(last)) {
    90→      return { node: last, offset: last.getTextContentSize() }
    91→    }
    92→    return { node, offset: 0 }
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  return { node, offset: point.offset }
    96→}
    97→
    98→function $getTextOffsetInBlock(
    99→  block: ElementNode,
   100→  targetNode: LexicalNode,
   101→  targetOffset: number,
   102→): number {
   103→  let offset = 0
   104→
   105→  function walk(node: LexicalNode): boolean {
   106→    if (node.is(targetNode)) {
   107→      offset += targetOffset
   108→      return true
   109→    }
   110→    if ($isTextNode(node)) {
   111→      offset += node.getTextContentSize()
   112→    } else if ($isLineBreakNode(node)) {
   113→      offset += 1
   114→    } else if ($isElementNode(node)) {
   115→      for (const child of node.getChildren()) {
   116→        if (walk(child)) return true
   117→      }
   118→    }
   119→    return false
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  for (const child of block.getChildren()) {
   123→    if (walk(child)) break
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  return offset
   127→}
   128→
   129→function $buildBlockAnchorData(block: ElementNode): AnchorResult<BlockAnchor> {
   130→  const blockId = $getState(block, blockIdState)
   131→  if (!blockId) {
   132→    return { ok: false, error: 'no-block-id' }
   133→  }
   134→
   135→  return {
   136→    ok: true,
   137→    anchor: {
   138→      mode: 'block',
   139→      blockId,
   140→      blockType: block.getType(),
   141→      blockFingerprint: computeBlockFingerprint(block),
   142→      snapshotText: block.getTextContent().slice(0, 300),
   143→    },
   144→  }
   145→}
   146→
   147→export function buildBlockAnchor(
   148→  editor: LexicalEditor,
   149→  blockKey?: string,
   150→): AnchorResult<BlockAnchor> {
   151→  return editor.read(() => {
   152→    if (blockKey) {
   153→      const node = $getRoot()
   154→        .getChildren()
   155→        .find((c) => c.getKey() === blockKey)
   156→      if (!node || !('getChildren' in node)) {
   157→        return { ok: false, error: 'not-root-block' }
   158→      }
   159→      return $buildBlockAnchorData(node as ElementNode)
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    const selection = $getSelection()
   163→    if (!$isRangeSelection(selection)) {
   164→      return { ok: false, error: 'no-selection' }
   165→    }
   166→
   167→    const anchorNode = selection.anchor.getNode()
   168→    const block = $getRootBlock(anchorNode)
   169→    if (!block) {
   170→      return { ok: false, error: 'not-root-block' }
   171→    }
   172→
   173→    return $buildBlockAnchorData(block)
   174→  })
   175→}
   176→
   177→export function buildRangeAnchor(
   178→  editor: LexicalEditor,
   179→): AnchorResult<RangeAnchor> {
   180→  return editor.read(() => {
   181→    const selection = $getSelection()
   182→    if (!$isRangeSelection(selection)) {
   183→      return { ok: false, error: 'no-selection' }
   184→    }
   185→
   186→    if (selection.isCollapsed()) {
   187→      return { ok: false, error: 'collapsed' }
   188→    }
   189→
   190→    const anchorBlock = $getRootBlock(selection.anchor.getNode())
   191→    const focusBlock = $getRootBlock(selection.focus.getNode())
   192→
   193→    if (!anchorBlock || !focusBlock) {
   194→      return { ok: false, error: 'not-root-block' }
   195→    }
   196→
   197→    if (anchorBlock !== focusBlock) {
   198→      return { ok: false, error: 'cross-block' }
   199→    }
   200→
   201→    const block = anchorBlock
   202→    const blockId = $getState(block, blockIdState)
   203→    if (!blockId) {
   204→      return { ok: false, error: 'no-block-id' }
   205→    }
   206→
   207→    const anchorPoint = $resolveSelectionPoint(selection, 'anchor')
   208→    const focusPoint = $resolveSelectionPoint(selection, 'focus')
   209→
   210→    let startOffset = $getTextOffsetInBlock(
   211→      block,
   212→      anchorPoint.node,
   213→      anchorPoint.offset,
   214→    )
   215→    let endOffset = $getTextOffsetInBlock(
   216→      block,
   217→      focusPoint.node,
   218→      focusPoint.offset,
   219→    )
   220→
   221→    if (startOffset > endOffset) {
   222→      ;[startOffset, endOffset] = [endOffset, startOffset]
   223→    }
   224→
   225→    const text = block.getTextContent()
   226→    const quote = text.slice(startOffset, endOffset)
   227→    const prefix = text.slice(Math.max(0, startOffset - 50), startOffset)
   228→    const suffix = text.slice(endOffset, endOffset + 50)
   229→
   230→    return {
   231→      ok: true,
   232→      anchor: {
   233→        mode: 'range',
   234→        blockId,
   235→        blockType: block.getType(),
   236→        blockFingerprint: computeBlockFingerprint(block),
   237→        snapshotText: text.slice(0, 300),
   238→        quote,
   239→        prefix,
   240→        suffix,
   241→        startOffset,
   242→        endOffset,
   243→      },
   244→    }
   245→  })
   246→}
   247→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     8→} from '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro'

    14→

    18→const fallbackSansFont =
    19→  "var(--app-font-sans, system-ui), -apple-system, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, 'noto sans sc', 'hiragino sans gb', sans-serif, Apple Color Emoji, Segoe UI Emoji, Not Color Emoji"
    20→const fallbackSerifFont =
    21→  "'Noto Serif CJK SC', 'Noto Serif SC', var(--app-font-serif, 'Source Han Serif SC'), 'Source Han Serif SC', 'Source Han Serif', source-han-serif-sc, SongTi SC, SimSum, 'Hiragino Sans GB', system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, 'Microsoft YaHei', 'WenQuanYi Micro Hei', sans-serif"
    22→const fallbackMonoFont =
    23→  "'OperatorMonoSSmLig Nerd Font', 'Cascadia Code PL', 'FantasqueSansMono Nerd Font', 'operator mono', JetBrainsMono, 'Fira code Retina', 'Fira code', Consolas, Monaco, 'Hannotate SC', monospace, -apple-system"
    24→
    25→const linkCardFetchContext: LinkCardFetchContext = {
    26→  adapters: {
    27→    github: {
    28→      request: fetchGitHubApi,
    29→    },
    30→    tmdb: {
    31→      request: (url) => {
    32→        const u = new URL(url)
    33→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/3\//, '')
    34→        return fetch(`/api/tmdb/${path}${u.search}`).then((r) => r.json())
    35→      },
    36→    },
    37→    bangumi: {
    38→      request: (url) => {
    39→        const u = new URL(url)
    40→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/v0\//, '')
    41→        return fetch(`/api/bangumi/${path}`).then((r) => r.json())
    42→      },
    43→    },
    44→    leetcode: {
    45→      request: (_url, init) => {
    46→        return fetch('/api/leetcode', init).then((r) => r.json())
    47→      },
    48→    },
    49→    'netease-music': {
    50→      request: (url) => {
    51→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    52→        return fetch('/api/music/netease', {
    53→          method: 'POST',
    54→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    55→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    56→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    57→      },
    58→    },
    59→    'qq-music': {
    60→      request: (url) => {
    61→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    62→        return fetch('/api/music/tencent', {
    63→          method: 'POST',
    64→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    65→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    66→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    67→      },
    68→    },
    69→    'mx-space': {
    70→      request: (path) => {
    71→        return fetch(`/api/v2/${path}`).then((r) => r.json())
    72→      },
    73→    },
    74→  },
    75→}
    76→
    77→export interface LexicalContentProps {
    78→  content: string
    79→  variant?: RichEditorVariant
    80→  className?: string
    81→}
    82→
    83→export function LexicalContent({
    84→  content,
    85→  variant,
    86→  className,
    87→}: LexicalContentProps) {
    88→  const editorState = useMemo<SerializedEditorState | null>(() => {
    89→    try {
    90→      return JSON.parse(content)
    91→    } catch {
    92→      return null
    93→    }
    94→  }, [content])
    95→
    96→  const isDark = useIsDark()
    97→
    98→  const baseVarStyle = useMemo(
    99→    () =>
   100→      ({
   101→        '--font-sans': fallbackSansFont,
   102→        '--font-serif': fallbackSerifFont,
   103→        '--font-mono': fallbackMonoFont,
   104→      }) as React.CSSProperties,
   105→    [],
   106→  )
   107→
   108→  const editorOverrideStyle = useMemo(
   109→    () =>
   110→      createThemeStyle({
   111→        color: {
   112→          accent: 'var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8))',
   113→          link: 'var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8))',
   114→          accentLight:
   115→            'color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8)) 20%, transparent)',
   116→          quoteBorder: 'var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8))',
   117→        },
   118→        layout: { maxWidth: '100%' },
   119→        typography: {
   120→          fontFamily:
   121→            variant === 'note'
   122→              ? `var(--note-font-override, ${fallbackSerifFont})`
   123→              : fallbackSansFont,
   124→          fontFamilySerif: fallbackSerifFont,
   125→          fontMono: fallbackMonoFont,
   126→        },
   127→      }),
   128→    [variant],
   129→  )
   130→
   131→  if (!editorState) return null
   132→
   133→  return (
   134→    <LinkCardFetchProvider value={linkCardFetchContext}>
   135→      <ShiroRenderer
   136→        value={editorState}
   137→        variant={variant}
   138→        theme={isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'}
   139→        className={clsx(className, 'bg-transparent!')}
   140→        style={{ ...baseVarStyle, ...editorOverrideStyle }}
   141→      />
   142→    </LinkCardFetchProvider>
   143→  )
   144→}
   145→
```

## Complete Architecture & Data Flow Analysis

Based on thorough investigation, here's the complete understanding of how content and comments are connected:

### 1. **PostContent.tsx** - Format Decision Layer
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/PostContent.tsx` (lines 1-15)

Decides rendering based on `contentFormat`:
```typescript
if (contentFormat === 'lexical' && content) {
  return <PostLexicalRenderer />
}
return <PostMarkdownRenderer />
```
- Receives `contentFormat` and `content` props from page component
- No data access itself - delegates to renderer

### 2. **NoteContent.tsx** - Same Pattern for Notes
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/notes/[id]/NoteContent.tsx` (lines 1-15)

Identical logic for notes with markdown/lexical rendering.

### 3. **MainLexicalContent.tsx** - Wraps Lexical Renderer
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/MainLexicalContent.tsx` (lines 1-26)

- Wraps content in `div#main-content-id`
- Calls `setMainMarkdownElement()` for reading tracking
- Dispatches `RefreshToc` event on content change
- Delegates actual rendering to `<LexicalContent>`

### 4. **LexicalContent.tsx** - Lexical Renderer Core
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx` (lines 1-144)

**Key details**:
- Parses content string as `SerializedEditorState` (JSON)
- Renders via `ShiroRenderer` from `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro`
- Handles theme, fonts, link card fetching
- Props: `content: string`, `variant?: 'note'`, `className?: string`

### 5. **CommentRoot.tsx** - Main Comment Container
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentRoot.tsx` (lines 1-40)

**Critical structure**:
```typescript
export const CommentAreaRoot: FC<CommentBaseProps & { allowComment: boolean }>
```
- Receives `refId` (content ID) and `allowComment` flag
- Returns JSX with:
  - `<CommentBoxRoot refId={refId} />` - Comment input form
  - `<Comments refId={refId} />` - Comment list
- Handles comment disabled state

### 6. **CommentRootLazy.tsx** - Error Boundary Wrapper
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentRootLazy.tsx` (lines 1-11)

Wraps `CommentAreaRoot` in `<ErrorBoundary>` for safety.

### 7. **CommentBox/providers.tsx** - State Management
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/providers.tsx` (lines 1-112)

**Three context layers**:

1. **CommentBoxContext** (lines 14-35): Form state atoms
   ```typescript
   refId: atom(''),
   text: atom(''),
   author: atomWithStorage(`comment-author`, ''),
   mail: atomWithStorage(`comment-mail`, ''),
   url: atomWithStorage(`comment-url`, ''),
   avatar: atom(''),
   source: atom(''),
   isWhisper: atomWithStorage(`comment-is-whisper`, false),
   syncToRecently: atomWithStorage(`comment-sync-to-recently`, true),
   ```

2. **CommentBoxLifeCycleContext** (lines 37-39): Post-submit callback
   ```typescript
   afterSubmit?: () => void
   ```

3. **CommentIsReplyProvider** (lines 78-95): Reply-to-comment context
   ```typescript
   isReply: boolean
   originalRefId: string
   onCompleted?: (comment: CommentModel) => void
   ```

**Critical provider pattern** (lines 41-71): `CommentBoxProvider` initializes `CommentBoxContext` with `refId` atom.

### 8. **CommentProvider.tsx** - Data Management for Comment List
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/comment/CommentProvider.tsx` (lines 1-166)

**Key context variables**:
- `CommentReaderMapContext` - maps reader IDs to user profiles
- `CommentListContext` - Jotai atom of comment map `Record<string, CommentModel>`
- `CommentMarkdownContainerRefContext` - reference to rendered markdown container

**Data flow** (lines 57-82):
1. Fetches comments via `useCommentsQuery(refId)` 
2. Flattens paginated data into flat comment map
3. Provides via `jotaiStore.set(commentAtom, commentsMap)`
4. Exposes via context for child components

### 9. **Data Flow: How refId Flows Down**

#### Post Page (`/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/page.tsx`)
- **Line 240-243**: Component receives `data.id` (post ID):
  ```typescript
  <CommentAreaRootLazy
    refId={data.id}
    allowComment={data.allowComment}
  />
  ```
- **Line 228**: Post data provided via `<CurrentPostDataProvider data={data} />`

#### Note Page (`/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/notes/[id]/page.tsx`)
- **Line 278-281**: Component receives `data.data.id` (note ID):
  ```typescript
  <CommentAreaRootLazy
    refId={data.data.id}
    allowComment={data.data.allowComment}
  />
  ```
- **Line 266**: Note data provided via `<CurrentNoteDataProvider data={data} />`

### 10. **CurrentPostDataProvider.tsx** - Post Data Context
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/post/CurrentPostDataProvider.tsx` (lines 1-37)

- Uses `jojoo/react` library's `createModelDataProvider` pattern
- Exports: `ModelDataProvider`, `useModelDataSelector`, `useSetModelData`
- Stores `PostModel & { liked, translations }`
- Available throughout post page tree

### 11. **CurrentNoteDataProvider.tsx** - Note Data Context
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/note/CurrentNoteDataProvider.tsx` (lines 1-49)

- Same pattern as post provider
- Stores `NoteWrappedWithLikedAndTranslationPayload`
- Enables runtime note data sync after login

### 12. **WrappedElementProvider.tsx** - Content Container State
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/shared/WrappedElementProvider.tsx` (lines 1-170)

**Exposes via context state**:
- `useWrappedElement()` - HTMLDivElement reference
- `useWrappedElementSize()` - `{ h: number, w: number }`
- `useWrappedElementPosition()` - `{ x: number, y: number }`
- `useIsEoFWrappedElement()` - boolean (end-of-article detect)

**Used by**: Post/Note pages wrap content in `<WrappedElementProvider eoaDetect>` (lines 161, 125).

**Purpose**: Content measurement for:
- TOC positioning
- Reading progress tracking
- Comment UI positioning (for future enhancement)

### 13. **Comment Model Structure**
From `Comment.tsx` (lines 70-79), comments contain:
```typescript
id: string
text: string
key: string           // Comment fingerprint for deduplication
location: string      // Browser location where comment was made
isWhispers: boolean   // Private comment flag
url: string           // Comment author URL
source: string        // Auth source (github, etc)
readerId: string      // Link to reader profile
avatar: string        // Author avatar
author: string        // Author name
parent: string | CommentModel // Parent comment (for replies)
children: CommentModel[]       // Reply tree
created: Date
editedAt?: Date
```

### 14. **Comment Query Structure**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/hooks.tsx` (lines 7-38)

```typescript
apiClient.comment.getByRefId(refId, { page })
```
Returns paginated `CommentModel[]` with pagination info.

Query key: `['comments', refId]` (**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/queries/keys/comment.ts`)

### 15. **Comment Rendering - Comment.tsx**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/Comment.tsx` (lines 42-245)

**Key features**:
- Renders individual comment with avatar, author, text
- Displays `location` and `key` metadata (lines 199-202)
- Handles single-link content specially (BlockLinkRenderer)
- Recursively renders `comment.children` (lines 236-241)
- Uses `CommentHolderContext` + portal for reply UI injection

### 16. **New Block ID Plugin (Recently Added)**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/BlockIdPlugin.tsx` (lines 1-62)

- Auto-generates unique `blockId` for each block
- Maintains deduplication via `blockIdState`
- Ensures all root blocks have IDs for anchoring

### 17. **Comment Anchor Utilities (Recently Added)**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/packages/rich-editor/src/utils/comment-anchor.ts` (lines 1-246)

**Supports two anchor modes**:

1. **BlockAnchor** - Comment on entire block:
   ```typescript
   mode: 'block'
   blockId: string
   blockType: string
   blockFingerprint: string (hash of first 200 chars)
   snapshotText: string (first 300 chars)
   ```

2. **RangeAnchor** - Comment on text selection within block:
   ```typescript
   mode: 'range'
   blockId: string
   blockType: string
   blockFingerprint: string
   snapshotText: string
   quote: string (selected text)
   prefix: string (50 chars before)
   suffix: string (50 chars after)
   startOffset: number
   endOffset: number
   ```

**Functions**:
- `buildBlockAnchor(editor, blockKey?)` - Create block anchor
- `buildRangeAnchor(editor)` - Create range anchor
- `computeBlockFingerprint()` - Hash-based stability checking

---

## Summary: Content-Comment Architecture

```
POST/NOTE PAGE
  ├─ CurrentPostDataProvider/CurrentNoteDataProvider
  │   └─ data.id, data.content, data.contentFormat, data.allowComment
  │
  ├─ WrappedElementProvider (content measurement)
  │   ├─ PostContent / NoteContent (format router)
  │   │   ├─ PostLexicalRenderer / NoteLexicalRenderer
  │   │   │   └─ MainLexicalContent → LexicalContent → ShiroRenderer
  │   │   └─ PostMarkdownRenderer / NoteMarkdownRenderer
  │   │       └─ MainMarkdown
  │   │
  │   └─ useWrappedElement() - for UI positioning
  │
  └─ CommentAreaRootLazy (refId = data.id, allowComment)
      ├─ CommentBoxRoot (input form)
      │   └─ CommentBoxProvider (form state atoms + refId)
      │       └─ CommentBoxRoot renders form UI
      │
      └─ Comments (list display)
          └─ CommentProvider (fetches & manages comment data)
              └─ renders Comment tree recursively
```

**refId is the single connection point**: Content rendered in `WrappedElementProvider` → Comments scoped to same `refId` below it.

## Comprehensive API and Comment System Investigation Results

Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, here are my findings organized by your seven investigation points:

---

### 1. **apiClient.comment Methods** 
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/node_modules/.pnpm/@mx-space+api-client@2.1.1/node_modules/@mx-space/api-client/dist/index.d.mts` (lines 1529-1620)

The API client exports these core methods:

```typescript
// Line 1530-1537: CommentDto interface (request payload)
interface CommentDto {
  author: string
  text: string
  mail: string
  url?: string
  source?: 'github' | 'google'
  avatar?: string
}

// Line 1545-1620: CommentController class
class CommentController<ResponseWrapper> implements IController {
  // Get comment by ID with children
  getById(id: string): RequestProxyResult<CommentModel & { ref: string }, ...>
  
  // Get comments by refId (post/note/page ID)
  getByRefId(refId: string, pagination?: PaginationParams): RequestProxyResult<
    PaginateResult<CommentModel & { ref: string }> & { readers: Record<string, ReaderModel> },
    ...
  >
  
  // Create a top-level comment
  comment(refId: string, data: CommentDto): RequestProxyResult<CommentModel, ...>
  
  // Reply to a comment
  reply(commentId: string, data: CommentDto): RequestProxyResult<CommentModel, ...>
}
```

The `CommentDto` currently accepts only: `author`, `text`, `mail`, `url`, `source`, `avatar`. No `anchor` field is exposed here.

---

### 2. **How the Proxy Pattern Works**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/hooks.tsx` (lines 159-166, 180-184)

The proxy pattern is used like this:

```typescript
// Owner comments use the proxy pattern (lines 159-166)
if (isLogged) {
  return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
    .reply(refId)
    .post<CommentModel>({
      data: {
        text,
        source,
      },
    })
    .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
}

// Normal comment (lines 180-184)
if (isLogged) {
  return apiClient.comment.proxy.owner
    .comment(refId)
    .post<CommentModel>({
      data: { text, source },
    })
    .then(wrappedCompletedCallback)
}
```

**How the proxy works**: The `IRequestHandler<ResponseWrapper>` interface (line 32 of index.d.mts) allows dynamic path building:
- `apiClient.comment.proxy` → returns `IRequestHandler`
- `.owner` → calls the handler function, returning another `IRequestHandler` for path `/comment/owner`
- `.comment(refId)` → calls the handler with `refId`, returning `IRequestHandler` for `/comment/owner/comment/{refId}`
- `.post({ data: {...} })` → sends POST with the data object

**Extra fields**: You CAN add extra fields to the data object. The data payload is not strictly typed in the proxy call—it's just passed through to `$fetch`. The server would need to accept and handle extra fields.

---

### 3. **apiClient Setup**
**Files**: 
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/lib/fetch/fetch.client.ts`
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/lib/fetch/fetch.server.ts`
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/lib/fetch/shared.ts`

**Client setup** (fetch.client.ts, lines 23-57):

```typescript
export const $fetch = createFetch({
  defaults: {
    timeout: 8000,
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: globalConfigureHeader,
    onRequest(context) {
      // Adds X-Session-Uuid header from sessionStorage or generates nanoid
      headers['X-Session-Uuid'] = globalThis?.sessionStorage?.getItem(uuidStorageKey) ?? uuid
      context.options.headers = headers
    },
  },
})
export const apiClient = createApiClient(createFetchAdapter($fetch))
```

**Adapter** (shared.ts, lines 8-50):

```typescript
export const createFetchAdapter = ($fetch: FetchType): IRequestAdapter<typeof $fetch> => ({
  default: $fetch,
  get(url, options) { return $fetch(url, { method: 'GET', query: params }) }
  post(url, options) { return $fetch(url, { method: 'post', query: params, body: data }) }
  // etc.
})

export const createApiClient = (fetchAdapter) =>
  createClient(fetchAdapter)(API_URL, {
    controllers: allControllers,
    getDataFromResponse(response) {
      return response as any
    },
  })
```

The apiClient is created from `@mx-space/api-client` version **2.1.1** (from package.json).

---

### 4. **CommentDto Extension Strategies**

**Current limitation**: The `CommentDto` interface in the API client is fixed and doesn't include `anchor`.

**Three approaches to add anchor**:

**Approach A: Cast to `any` (quickest)**
```typescript
// In CommentBox/hooks.tsx
const data = { text, source, anchor: commentAnchor } as any
apiClient.comment.proxy.owner.comment(refId).post({ data })
```

**Approach B: Custom fetch wrapper**
```typescript
// In lib/fetch/shared.ts, wrap the apiClient.comment methods
apiClient.request({
  url: `${apiClient.endpoint}/comment/owner/comment/${refId}`,
  method: 'post',
  data: { text, source, anchor: commentAnchor }
})
```

**Approach C: Extend type at application level**
```typescript
// Create types.ts
type CommentDtoWithAnchor = CommentDto & { anchor?: CommentAnchor }
```

The simplest working solution is **Approach A** (cast as any) until the mx-space server API schema is updated to accept anchor.

---

### 5. **CommentBox ActionBar.tsx**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/ActionBar.tsx`

Current structure (lines 85-133):

```typescript
export const CommentBoxActionBar: Component = ({ className }) => {
  const t = useTranslations('comment')
  const hasCommentText = useCommentBoxHasText()

  return (
    <footer className={...}>
      <span className="flex-1 ...">
        <span className="hidden md:inline">
          {t('support_markdown')}
          <b>Markdown</b>
          {t('and')}{' '}
          <MarkdownLink
            noIcon
            href="https://docs.github.com/zh/..."
          >
            GFM
          </MarkdownLink>
        </span>
        <CommentBoxSlotProvider />
      </span>
      <AnimatePresence>
        {hasCommentText && (
          <m.aside key="send-button-wrapper" ...>
            <TextLengthIndicator />
            <WhisperCheckbox />
            <SyncToRecentlyCheckbox />
            <SubmitButton />
          </m.aside>
        )}
      </AnimatePresence>
    </footer>
  )
}
```

**To add anchor badge**: Insert between `TextLengthIndicator` and `WhisperCheckbox`:

```typescript
{commentAnchor && (
  <m.span className="text-xs px-2 py-1 rounded bg-blue-100 dark:bg-blue-900">
    📍 Anchored to block
  </m.span>
)}
```

---

### 6. **CommentBox AuthedInput.tsx**
**File**: `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentBox/AuthedInput.tsx`

Current component (lines 15-75):

```typescript
export const CommentBoxAuthedInput: Component<{ autoFocus?: boolean }> = ({
  autoFocus,
}) => {
  const setter = useSetCommentBoxValues()
  const reader = useSessionReader()
  const displayName = reader ? reader.name || 'Anonymous' : ''

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!reader) return
    setter('author', displayName)
    setter('avatar', reader.image)
    setter('mail', reader.email)
    reader.handle && setter('url', getUserUrl({...}))
    setter('source', reader.provider)
  }, [displayName, reader, setter])

  return (
    <div className="flex space-x-4">
      <div className="...">
        <Avatar.Root>
          <Avatar.Image ... />
          <Avatar.Fallback ... />
        </Avatar.Root>
        <UserAuthFromIcon className="..." />
      </div>
      <div className="relative h-[150px] w-full ...">
        <UniversalTextArea className="pb-5" autoFocus={autoFocus} />
      </div>
      <CommentBoxActionBar className="..." />
    </div>
  )
}
```

Uses `useSetCommentBoxValues()` hook to populate the comment state atom. This is where you'd add logic to capture and store `anchor` data if needed.

---

### 7. **Accessing Serialized Editor State**
**Key files**:
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/ui/rich-content/LexicalContent.tsx` (lines 83-94)
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/providers/post/CurrentPostDataProvider.tsx`
- `<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/app/[locale]/posts/(post-detail)/[category]/[slug]/PostLexicalRenderer.tsx`

**Access pattern**:

```typescript
// In LexicalContent.tsx (lines 88-94)
const editorState = useMemo<SerializedEditorState | null>(() => {
  try {
    return JSON.parse(content)  // content is a JSON string
  } catch {
    return null
  }
}, [content])

// In PostLexicalRenderer.tsx (lines 8-11)
export const PostLexicalRenderer = () => {
  const content = useCurrentPostDataSelector((data) => data?.content)
  if (!content) return null
  return <MainLexicalContent content={content} className="..." />
}

// Content comes from CurrentPostDataProvider via:
// import { useCurrentPostDataSelector } from '~/providers/post/CurrentPostDataProvider'
// which wraps the TextBaseModelLexical.content field
```

**SerializedEditorState structure**: The `content` field from the API is a JSON string representing Lexical's `SerializedEditorState`, which includes:

```typescript
{
  root: {
    children: [
      // Each child is a block with:
      {
        type: 'paragraph' | 'heading' | 'codeblock' | 'alert' | etc.,
        version: number,
        children: [ text nodes or nested blocks ],
        // Custom state stored via blockIdState:
        __blockId?: string  // Added by BlockIdPlugin
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Block ID extraction**: To get blockIds, you need access to a Lexical editor instance:

```typescript
// From comment-anchor.ts (lines 147-175)
export function buildBlockAnchor(editor: LexicalEditor, blockKey?: string): AnchorResult<BlockAnchor> {
  return editor.read(() => {
    if (blockKey) {
      const node = $getRoot().getChildren().find((c) => c.getKey() === blockKey)
      // Get blockId via $getState(node, blockIdState)
    }
    // ...
  })
}
```

**At comment time**: When the reader clicks to comment, you'd need:
1. A reference to the `RichRenderer`'s editor instance (not available by default—it's read-only)
2. Or: Pass the serialized state + parse it to extract blockIds manually

**Simpler approach**: Store blockIds in a hidden DOM attribute or data structure when rendering, then read them when commenting.

---

### Summary Table

| Component | Location | Key Finding |
|-----------|----------|------------|
| **CommentDto** | API Client 2.1.1 | No `anchor` field; needs custom handling |
| **comment() method** | `apiClient.comment.comment(refId, data)` | Accepts CommentDto, sends POST /comment/{refId} |
| **Proxy pattern** | `apiClient.comment.proxy.owner.comment(refId).post({data})` | Dynamic path building; data can include extra fields |
| **apiClient setup** | shared.ts | Uses ofetch adapter; created with `allControllers` from @mx-space/api-client |
| **ActionBar** | CommentBox/ActionBar.tsx | Good place to display anchor badge indicator |
| **AuthedInput** | CommentBox/AuthedInput.tsx | Uses Jotai atoms via setter; extends comment context state |
| **Serialized state** | LexicalContent.tsx | `content` is JSON string; parse via `JSON.parse()` to get SerializedEditorState |
| **Block IDs** | BlockIdPlugin.tsx + comment-anchor.ts | Added by plugin; stored in node's blockIdState; extracted via `buildBlockAnchor()` function |

```text
    20→const fallbackSerifFont =
    21→  "'Noto Serif CJK SC', 'Noto Serif SC', var(--app-font-serif, 'Source Han Serif SC'), 'Source Han Serif SC', 'Source Han Serif', source-han-serif-sc, SongTi SC, SimSum, 'Hiragino Sans GB', system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, 'Microsoft YaHei', 'WenQuanYi Micro Hei', sans-serif"
    22→const fallbackMonoFont =
    23→  "'OperatorMonoSSmLig Nerd Font', 'Cascadia Code PL', 'FantasqueSansMono Nerd Font', 'operator mono', JetBrainsMono, 'Fira code Retina', 'Fira code', Consolas, Monaco, 'Hannotate SC', monospace, -apple-system"
    24→
    25→const linkCardFetchContext: LinkCardFetchContext = {
    26→  adapters: {
    27→    github: {
    28→      request: fetchGitHubApi,
    29→    },
    30→    tmdb: {
    31→      request: (url) => {
    32→        const u = new URL(url)
    33→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/3\//, '')
    34→        return fetch(`/api/tmdb/${path}${u.search}`).then((r) => r.json())
    35→      },
    36→    },
    37→    bangumi: {
    38→      request: (url) => {
    39→        const u = new URL(url)
    40→        const path = u.pathname.replace(/^\/v0\//, '')
    41→        return fetch(`/api/bangumi/${path}`).then((r) => r.json())
    42→      },
    43→    },
    44→    leetcode: {
    45→      request: (_url, init) => {
    46→        return fetch('/api/leetcode', init).then((r) => r.json())
    47→      },
    48→    },
    49→    'netease-music': {
    50→      request: (url) => {
    51→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    52→        return fetch('/api/music/netease', {
    53→          method: 'POST',
    54→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    55→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    56→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    57→      },
    58→    },
    59→    'qq-music': {
    60→      request: (url) => {
    61→        const songId = new URL(url).pathname.split('/').pop()
    62→        return fetch('/api/music/tencent', {
    63→          method: 'POST',
    64→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    65→          body: JSON.stringify({ songId }),
    66→        }).then((r) => r.json())
    67→      },
    68→    },
    69→    'mx-space': {
    70→      request: (path) => {

    72→      },
    73→    },
    74→  },
    75→}
    76→
    77→export interface LexicalContentProps {
    78→  content: string
    79→  variant?: RichEditorVariant
    80→  className?: string
    81→}
    82→
    83→export function LexicalContent({
    84→  content,
    85→  variant,
    86→  className,
    87→}: LexicalContentProps) {
    88→  const editorState = useMemo<SerializedEditorState | null>(() => {
    89→    try {
    90→      return JSON.parse(content)
    91→    } catch {
    92→      return null
    93→    }
    94→  }, [content])
    95→
    96→  const isDark = useIsDark()
    97→
    98→  const baseVarStyle = useMemo(
    99→    () =>
   100→      ({
   101→        '--font-sans': fallbackSansFont,
   102→        '--font-serif': fallbackSerifFont,
   103→        '--font-mono': fallbackMonoFont,
   104→      }) as React.CSSProperties,
   105→    [],
   106→  )
   107→
   108→  const editorOverrideStyle = useMemo(
   109→    () =>
   110→      createThemeStyle({
   111→        color: {
   112→          accent: 'var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8))',
   113→          link: 'var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8))',
   114→          accentLight:
   115→            'color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8)) 20%, transparent)',
   116→          quoteBorder: 'var(--color-accent, var(--color-primary, #33a6b8))',
   117→        },
   118→        layout: { maxWidth: '100%' },
   119→        typography: {
   120→          fontFamily:
   121→            variant === 'note'
   122→              ? `var(--note-font-override, ${fallbackSerifFont})`
   123→              : fallbackSansFont,
   124→          fontFamilySerif: fallbackSerifFont,
   125→          fontMono: fallbackMonoFont,
   126→        },
   127→      }),
   128→    [variant],
   129→  )
   130→
   131→  if (!editorState) return null
   132→
   133→  return (
   134→    <LinkCardFetchProvider value={linkCardFetchContext}>
   135→      <ShiroRenderer
   136→        value={editorState}
   137→        variant={variant}
   138→        theme={isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'}
   139→        className={clsx(className, 'bg-transparent!')}
   140→        style={{ ...baseVarStyle, ...editorOverrideStyle }}
   141→      />
   142→    </LinkCardFetchProvider>
   143→  )
   144→}
   145→
```

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import type { CommentModel } from '@mx-space/api-client'
     4→import { atom } from 'jotai'
     5→import { atomWithStorage } from 'jotai/utils'
     6→import type { FC, PropsWithChildren } from 'react'
     7→import { createContext, memo, useEffect, useMemo } from 'react'
     8→
     9→import { useBeforeMounted } from '~/hooks/common/use-before-mounted'
    10→import { jotaiStore } from '~/lib/store'
    11→
    12→import { setCommentActionLeftSlot, useCommentActionLeftSlot } from './hooks'
    13→
    14→export const commentStoragePrefix = 'comment-'
    15→export const createInitialValue = () => ({
    16→  refId: atom(''),
    17→
    18→  text: atom(''),
    19→  author: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}author`, ''),
    20→  mail: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}mail`, ''),
    21→  url: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}url`, ''),
    22→
    23→  avatar: atom(''),
    24→  source: atom(''),
    25→
    26→  // settings
    27→  isWhisper: atomWithStorage(`${commentStoragePrefix}is-whisper`, false),
    28→  syncToRecently: atomWithStorage(
    29→    `${commentStoragePrefix}sync-to-recently`,
    30→    true,
    31→  ),
    32→})
    33→export const CommentBoxContext = createContext<
    34→  ReturnType<typeof createInitialValue>
    35→>(null!)
    36→
    37→export const CommentBoxLifeCycleContext = createContext<{
    38→  afterSubmit?: () => void
    39→}>(null!)
    40→
    41→export const CommentBoxProvider = (
    42→  props: PropsWithChildren & {
    43→    refId: string
    44→    afterSubmit?: () => void
    45→    initialValue?: string
    46→  },
    47→) => {
    48→  const { refId, children, afterSubmit, initialValue } = props
    49→
    50→  const ctxValue = useMemo(
    51→    () => ({
    52→      ...createInitialValue(),
    53→      refId: atom(refId),
    54→    }),
    55→    [refId],
    56→  )
    57→  useBeforeMounted(() => {
    58→    if (initialValue) {
    59→      jotaiStore.set(ctxValue.text, initialValue)
    60→    }
    61→  })
    62→  return (
    63→    <CommentBoxContext key={refId} value={ctxValue}>
    64→      <CommentBoxLifeCycleContext
    65→        value={useMemo(() => ({ afterSubmit }), [afterSubmit])}
    66→      >
    67→        {children}
    68→      </CommentBoxLifeCycleContext>
    69→    </CommentBoxContext>
    70→  )
    71→}
    72→
    73→export const CommentIsReplyContext = createContext(false)
    74→export const CommentOriginalRefIdContext = createContext('')
    75→export const CommentCompletedCallbackContext = createContext<
    76→  null | ((comment: CommentModel) => void)
    77→>(null)
    78→export const CommentIsReplyProvider = (
    79→  props: PropsWithChildren<{
    80→    isReply: boolean
    81→    originalRefId: string
    82→    onCompleted?: (comment: CommentModel) => void
    83→  }>,
    84→) => {
    85→  const { isReply, originalRefId, onCompleted, children } = props
    86→  return (
    87→    <CommentOriginalRefIdContext value={originalRefId}>
    88→      <CommentIsReplyContext value={isReply}>
    89→        <CommentCompletedCallbackContext value={onCompleted || null}>
    90→          {children}
    91→        </CommentCompletedCallbackContext>
    92→      </CommentIsReplyContext>
    93→    </CommentOriginalRefIdContext>
    94→  )
    95→}
    96→
    97→export const CommentBoxSlotPortal = memo((props: PropsWithChildren) => {
    98→  const { children } = props
    99→  useEffect(() => {
   100→    setCommentActionLeftSlot(children)
   101→    return () => {
   102→      setCommentActionLeftSlot(null)
   103→    }
   104→  }, [children])
   105→  return null
   106→})
   107→
   108→export const CommentBoxSlotProvider: FC = memo(() => useCommentActionLeftSlot())
   109→
   110→CommentBoxSlotProvider.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotProvider'
   111→CommentBoxSlotPortal.displayName = 'CommentBoxSlotPortal'
   112→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
    19→export const WithArticleSelectionAction: Component<{
    20→  refId: string
    21→  title: string
    22→  canComment: boolean
    23→}> = ({ refId, title, children, canComment }) => {
    24→  const t = useTranslations('common')
    25→  const isMobile = useIsMobile()
    26→  const [pos, setPos] = useState({
    27→    x: 0,
    28→    y: 0,
    29→  })
    30→  const [show, setShow] = useState(false)
    31→  const [selectedText, setSelectedText] = useState('')
    32→  const ref = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    33→
    34→  useEffect(() => {
    35→    const handler = (e: MouseEvent): void => {
    36→      if (window.getSelection()?.toString().length === 0) {
    37→        setShow(false)
    38→        return
    39→      }
    40→      if (ref.current?.contains(e.target as Node)) return
    41→      setShow(false)
    42→    }
    43→    document.addEventListener('mousedown', handler)
    44→    document.addEventListener('mouseup', handler)
    45→    return () => {
    46→      document.removeEventListener('mousedown', handler)
    47→      document.removeEventListener('mouseup', handler)
    48→    }
    49→  }, [])
    50→
    51→  const isClient = useIsClient()
    52→  const { present } = useModalStack()
    53→
    54→  useEffect(() => {
    55→    // wait for toc listener to be registered
    56→    const timer = setTimeout(() => {
    57→      document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent(DOMCustomEvents.RefreshToc))
    58→    }, 1000)
    59→    return () => {
    60→      clearTimeout(timer)
    61→    }
    62→  }, [])
    63→
    64→  const canCopy = !useIsInBanCopyContext()
    65→
    66→  const actionCanActivated = canCopy || canComment
    67→
    68→  if (isMobile || !isClient) return children
    69→  if (!actionCanActivated) return children
    70→  return (
    71→    <div
    72→      className="relative"
    73→      ref={ref}
    74→      onMouseUp={(e) => {
    75→        const $ = ref.current
    76→        if (!$) return
    77→
    78→        const selection = window.getSelection()
    79→        if (!selection) return
    80→        const selectedText = selection.toString()
    81→        if (selectedText.length === 0) return
    82→        const { top, left } = $.getBoundingClientRect()
    83→        setShow(true)
    84→        setSelectedText(selectedText)
    85→        setPos({
    86→          x: e.clientX - left + 10,
    87→          y: e.clientY - top + 10,
    88→        })
    89→      }}
    90→    >

   100→            )}

   103→              top: pos.y - 40,
   104→            }}
   105→            initial={{ y: 10, opacity: 0.8, scale: 0.1 }}
   106→            animate={{ y: 0, opacity: 1, scale: 1 }}
   107→            exit={{
   108→              scale: 0,
   109→              opacity: 0,
   110→            }}
   111→          >
   112→            {canCopy && (
   113→              <>
   114→                <MotionButtonBase
   115→                  data-event="selection-comment"
   116→                  className="rounded-md px-2 py-1 hover:bg-slate-100/80 dark:hover:bg-zinc-900/90"
   117→                  onMouseUp={stopPropagation}
   118→                  onClick={() => {
   119→                    navigator.clipboard.writeText(selectedText)
   120→                    setShow(false)
   121→                    toast.success(t('selection_copy_success'))
   122→                  }}
   123→                >
   124→                  {t('selection_copy')}
   125→                </MotionButtonBase>
   126→                <DividerVertical className="mx-1" />
   127→              </>
   128→            )}
   129→            {canComment && (
   130→              <MotionButtonBase
   131→                data-event="selection-comment"
   132→                className="rounded-md px-2 py-1 hover:bg-slate-100/80 dark:hover:bg-zinc-900/90"
   133→                onClick={() => {
   134→                  present({
   135→                    title: t('selection_comment'),
   136→
   137→                    content: (rest) => (
   138→                      <CommentModal
   139→                        refId={refId}
   140→                        title={title}
   141→                        initialValue={`> ${selectedText
   142→                          ?.split('\n')
   143→                          .join('')}\n\n`}
   144→                        {...rest}
   145→                      />
   146→                    ),
   147→                  })
   148→                }}
   149→              >
   150→                {t('selection_quote_comment')}
   151→              </MotionButtonBase>
   152→            )}
   153→          </m.div>
   154→        )}
   155→      </AnimatePresence>
   156→    </div>
   157→  )
   158→}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-27 14:36:10Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The core service `createComment()` at line 648-656 does handle anchor:

```typescript
const normalizedAnchor = this.resolveAnchorForCreate(
  doc.anchor as CommentAnchorInput | undefined,
  ref,
)
if (normalizedAnchor) {
  doc.anchor = normalizedAnchor
} else {
  delete (doc as Partial<CommentModel>).anchor
}
```

So the service layer is ready. The problem is the **controller layer** - `CommentDto` strips anchor via `.omit({ anchor: true })`.

But wait - let me look at the controller more carefully. 

At line 223, the controller spreads the validated body into the model, but since `CommentDto` omits anchor during validation, the anchor never makes it through the `ZodValidationPipe` in the first place. So the fix is to remove the anchor omit from `CommentDto` and instead use the base `CommentSchema` that includes anchor as optional, then pass anchor from the web client in the API call. I have a clear plan now. I'll extend the existing selection action system by capturing the text range when a user clicks "Comment", building a `RangeAnchor` object, and passing it through a Jotai atom to the comment creation API. Then I'll use the CSS Custom Highlight API to visually mark range comments in the content, and add a hover button for block-level comments. I need to create a few new components starting with the comment anchor highlighting module. I need to reconsider the implementation strategy. The `WithArticleSelectionAction` wrapper handles all content types, but anchor building should only happen for lexical content since markdown doesn't support proper range anchors. When a selection occurs in lexical content, I need to identify which block contains it and construct the anchor accordingly before passing it through to the comment modal.

For block-level comments, I'll add a hover state that shows a comment button on lexical blocks, which opens the modal with a BlockAnchor. For highlighting, I'll read existing anchored comments and render their ranges in the content.

To extract block information from the rendered output, I can use the fact that `ShiroRenderer` places each block as a direct child of `.rich-content`, so the DOM element's position maps directly to the serialized state's children array. From there I can determine the block boundaries and calculate the selection's position relative to it.

The approach mirrors what the demo already does with `SelectionCommentPopup` and `BlockCommentGutter`, but integrated into the web app's existing comment flow. Since the user wants both range and block commenting for lexical articles, I need to extend the current `WithArticleSelectionAction` to also handle block hovering.

For range comments, I'll enhance the selection handler to build a `RangeAnchor` when dealing with lexical content. For block comments, I'll add a hover button component. I also need to wire up CSS Custom Highlight for visual feedback and ensure comment cards display the anchor information appropriately. The content type, JSON structure, and comment data are all accessible through the existing selectors, so the integration should be straightforward.

The tricky part is that comments are fetched in the `CommentProvider` deep in the component tree, but I need that data available at the content level for the highlight plugin. I'm considering whether to fetch comments separately at the content level or use a shared Jotai atom to pass the data between layers. The `CommentProvider` already manages comments in an atom, so I should check if I can access that atom from the content area without duplicating the fetch.

Actually, the cleanest solution is to create a module-level atom that stores anchored comments separately from the comment list. When `CommentProvider` fetches comments, it also updates this global atom, and the highlight plugin reads from it. This way the data flows naturally without needing to restructure the component hierarchy.

Looking at the component tree, I need to add a `CommentAnchorProvider` near the top to fetch and provide anchored comments, then place `CommentAnchorHighlight` inside the content container and `BlockCommentButton` as a sibling. I should also extend the selection action wrapper to build anchor data for lexical content.

But I'm overcomplicating this. Let me focus on the minimal changes needed to get this working.

I'll create an atom to store pending comment anchors and anchored comments, modify the selection action handler to build a RangeAnchor when the user comments on lexical content, pass that anchor through to the CommentModal and then to CommentBoxRoot, and update the comment creation hook to read from the atom and include the anchor in the API request.

I also need a new component to highlight anchored comments using the CSS Custom Highlight API, and a BlockCommentButton that appears on hover for block-level content to create BlockAnchors. Finally, I should remove the `.omit({ anchor: true })` from the CommentDto so anchors are properly persisted.

For passing the anchor through the CommentModal chain, I'm thinking about adding an `anchor` property to `CommentBase` so it flows naturally from the selection action through the modal and into the comment creation hook.

Actually, a cleaner approach is to leverage the existing Jotai atoms—I'll add an `anchor` atom to the comment state in `providers.tsx`, then have `useSendComment` read from it when making the API call. When the selection action opens the modal, it just sets this atom, and everything else follows the established pattern.

For block highlighting in the sidebar, I need to think through how to highlight the entire block when hovering a comment with `anchor.mode === 'block'`, though the current layout has comments displayed below the content rather than alongside it.

The main visual features I want are range comments with underlined text using the CSS Custom Highlight API, block comments with some indicator on the block itself, and anchor information displayed in the comment cards. For now I'll prioritize the range highlighting, storing anchor data during comment creation, and showing the quote or block reference in the cards.

I'm also trying to figure out how to extract block information from the serialized state. I need to understand how `blockIdState` gets persisted when the content is exported—whether it's included in the serialized JSON output and how I can access it when parsing on the web app side.
</details></think>

 `./packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/BlockIdPlugin.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useLexicalComposerContext } from '@lexical/react/LexicalComposerContext'
     2→import {
     3→  $addUpdateTag,
     4→  $getRoot,
     5→  $getState,
     6→  $setState,
     7→  createState,
     8→} from 'lexical'
     9→import { useEffect } from 'react'
    10→
    11→export const blockIdState = createState('blockId', {
    12→  parse: (v: unknown) => (typeof v === 'string' ? v : ''),
    13→})
    14→
    15→const NORMALIZATION_TAG = 'block-id-normalization'
    16→
    17→export function BlockIdPlugin() {
    18→  const [editor] = useLexicalComposerContext()
    19→
    20→  useEffect(() => {
    21→    return editor.registerUpdateListener(({ tags }) => {
    22→      if (tags.has(NORMALIZATION_TAG)) return
    23→
    24→      editor.read(() => {
    25→        const children = $getRoot().getChildren()
    26→        let needsUpdate = false
    27→        const seen = new Set<string>()
    28→
    29→        for (const child of children) {
    30→          const id = $getState(child, blockIdState)
    31→          if (id === '' || seen.has(id)) {
    32→            needsUpdate = true
    33→            break
    34→          }
    35→          seen.add(id)
    36→        }
    37→
    38→        if (!needsUpdate) return
    39→
    40→        editor.update(
    41→          () => {
    42→            $addUpdateTag('history-merge')
    43→            const children = $getRoot().getChildren()
    44→            const seen = new Set<string>()
    45→
    46→            for (const child of children) {
    47→              let id = $getState(child, blockIdState)
    48→              if (id === '' || seen.has(id)) {
    49→                id = crypto.randomUUID()
    50→                $setState(child, blockIdState, id)
    51→              }
    52→              seen.add(id)
    53→            }
    54→          },
    55→          { tag: NORMALIZATION_TAG },
    56→        )
    57→      })
    58→    })
    59→  }, [editor])
    60→
    61→  return null
    62→}
    63→
```
</details></tool-use>
